text:
**How to apply**
- Access the link with the same userID/password already used for the program application

- Complete the "Residence selection" section by choosing the residence hall you prefer among those available.

- Select the room and click on "Save" at the end of the section

- Complete the "Contact details" section and click on "Save" at the end of it

- Click on "Confirm data": this will be the necessary step to secure the room

- Proceed with the payment of the accommodation through the link available in the subtitle of the "Payment&documents" section; then upload the receipt of payment in the dedicated upload space

- Print, sign (attention! handwritten signature is required and mandatory) and let your parent/legal guardian sign all the documents available in the application; then upload each of them in the correspondent upload space

- Click on "Submit application"
The office will get back to you by publishing the confirmation of your housing assignment and all the information for your check-in on the same portal around mid-May 2026.

Summer school in Law for High School students

Students of the Summer school in Law will be accommodated at the Bligny residence for the period **Sunday, July 5th - Friday, July 10th, 2026**.

Please check all the details and pictures of the Bligny residence [here](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus/buildings-and-classrooms/bligny-residence).

The price for accommodation at the Bligny residence for the whole period will be **800,00 euros**.

Rooms available will be all single bedrooms, with bathroom shared with only one other student of the same gender. All rooms are placed in apartments of 2 or 4 bedrooms, or alternatively in clusters of single rooms with shared bathroom. The Summer School participation does not include meal plan.

If you are interested in staying at the Bligny residence, you will have to submit a **separate housing application**, after having completed your enrollment in the program. When accessing the application, please select the option "Summer School in LAW - Bligny residence" as it will be the only one available for the Law program; the other residence halls will not be available. Please apply for housing as soon as possible after your enrollment, as availability cannot be guaranteed for all participants due to the overall limited number of rooms.

The link to apply for housing will be available on this webpage starting from 11 March 2026 and it will be available until rooms will be finished, and in any case until 21 April 2026 at 6 pm.

Before you apply for housing, it is necessary that you have completed the application for the Summer School program.

[**ONLINE APPLICATION - LAW STUDENTS Summer school accommodation**](https://admissions.unibocconi.it/admissions/Students/OpenFromExtUrl?concourseId=14458&ordType=)

**How to apply**
- Access the link with the same userID/password already used for the program application

- Complete the "Residence selection" section by choosing ONLY the the "Summer school in LAW - Bligny residence" option. Please do not select other options as they will NOT be available for LAW students.

- Select the room and click on "Save" at the end of the section

- Complete the "Contact details" section and click on "Save" at the end of it

- Click on "Confirm data": this will be the necessary step to secure the room

- Proceed with the payment of the accommodation through the link available in the subtitle of the "Payment&documents" section; the upload the receipt of payment in the dedicated upload space

- Print, sign (attention! handwritten signature is required and mandatory) and let your parent/legal guardian sign all the documents available in the application; then upload each of them in the correspondent upload space

- Click on "Submit application"

The office will get back to you by publishing the confirmation of your housing assignment and all the information for your check-in on the same portal around mid-May 2026.

Summer school for Bachelor and Master students

Students of the Summer School for Bachelor students or of the Summer School for Master students will be offered the opportunity to apply for a room for the period **Sunday, June 28th - Saturday, July 18th 2026** at Aparto Giovenale, an external residence located on campus with which Bocconi has a partnership and that already hosts numerous Bocconi students.

**From April 1st, 2026: please contact the office at [summerschool@unibocconi.it](mailto:summerschool@unibocconi.it) if you need accommodation**

ONLINE APPLICATION - BACHELOR and MASTER summer school accommodation - expired

How to apply
- Access the link with the same userID/password already used for the application for the program

- Complete the "Reserve your room" section by choosing the room type you prefer (ENSUITE room)

- Select the room and click on "Save" at the end of the section

- Click on "Submit data/Conferma dati" at the top right hand corner

- Now you see a new section of application ("Ensuite room reservation"): it means that your have secured a spot. The section contains the document "How to complete your reservation". Please follow the instructions contained in it, in order to complete the reservation of your room at Aparto Giovenale.

- Fill in your contact details and click on "Save" at the end of the section

- Click on "Submit application/Inoltra application"
**Important note**

Aparto reservations will NOT be considered valid unless the procedure described above is completed; i.e. Aparto will only manage reservations submitted through the Bocconi housing application, meaning that the student has accessed the Bocconi Housing portal, selected and confirmed one of the Aparto options, viewed the subsequent sections of the application, and only then completed the procedure on the Aparto portal. The Bocconi Housing Office will share the official reservation list with Aparto. Any reservations not included in this list and/or submitted through different channels will be automatically cancelled.

[Join Us at Our Open Day](https://bocconiopenday.liveforum.space/membership/login?lang=en-GB&source=float_login "Join Us at Our Open Day")

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/summer-school-2026-housing#addtoany "Show all")

Applying to Bocconi

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi)

Bachelor and Law Programs

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs)

If you are enrolled in one of the Bocconi Summer School programs that will take place during summer 2026, you have the opportunity to make the most of your experience by choosing accommodation among one of the options offered by the University. Please check on this page all the details and how to apply!

Summer school for High School students

High school students will be offered accommodation in the Bocconi residence halls for the period **Sunday, July 5th - Friday, July 17th 2026.**

Bocconi will reserve 6 entire buildings to the high school students, namely the following residences: Bocconi, Javotte, Bligny, Castiglioni, Dubini, Spadolini. Please check all the details and pictures of each residence [here](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/bocconi-residence-halls). The price for accommodation in one of our residence halls for the whole period will be **1600,00 euros**.

Rooms available will be all single bedrooms, with bathroom shared with only one other student of the same gender. All rooms are placed in apartments of 2 or 4 bedrooms, or alternatively in clusters of single rooms with shared bathroom. The Summer School participation does not include meal plan.

If you wish to stay at the same residence with another Summer school High school student, please apply at the same time together and select the "Bocconi residence". We will only be able to put you together in that residence.

In order to submit the **separate housing application**, after having completed your enrollment in the program, please check the information below. We suggest you to apply for housing as soon as possible after your enrollment, as availability cannot be guaranteed for all participants due to the overall limited number of rooms.

The link to apply for housing will be available on this webpage starting from 11 March 2026 and it will be available until rooms will be finished, and in any case until 21 April 2026 at 6 pm.

Before you apply for housing, it is necessary that you have completed the application for the Summer School program.

Attention!

Application is open to high school students in their third-to-last and second-to-last year in Italy or abroad.

Only students who will have already turned 15 years old by the start of the program are eligible to participate in Bocconi Summer School.

[**ONLINE APPLICATION - Summer school for high school students housing**](https://admissions.unibocconi.it/admissions/Students/OpenFromExtUrl?concourseId=14458&ordType=)

**no rooms left**

Applying to Bocconi

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi)

Summer School

Overview


If you're interested in further developing your passion for **business, economics, data science, AI or law**, apply to take part in our Summer School, a learning experience focused on leading-edge subjects.

You will be taught by Bocconi University faculty members and attend classes with top students that share your interests.

Bocconi Summer School offers a challenging study program open to motivated students. It's a unique opportunity to participate in an **exciting laboratory of teaching and active learning**.

[Find out more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/summer-school)


[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/summer-school#addtoany "Show all")

Housing

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing)


Spring semester a.y. 2024-25

If for the Spring semester a.y. 2024-25 you will be an incoming exchange student and you are interested in accommodation on campus, you will have the possibility to apply for a room at the **Aparto Giovenale residence**, located at a 5-minute walking distance from the Bocconi campus.

The residence is owned and managed by an external company, and it was inaugurated in early 2022. Bocconi has achieved a **special partnership** that will allow our students to get reserved places and special prices for the rooms available.

Below an abstract of our partner’s manifesto:

“We are local, vibrant, globally connected student housing that will enable the unstoppable and emerging talent community to discover their possibilities. Ours is a place where you’ll be supported, encouraged and welcomed. Here you’ll find lifelong friends, you’ll live experiences that will shape the rest of your life. From the minute you arrive, we are a resource, a base, a home and a friend!”

Our exchange students will be reserved places in twin (double) rooms.

The price for this accommodation will be 2975€ for the whole period.

All the rooms are equipped with bed, private desk and wardrobe, a curtain to guarantee your privacy and a shared (in-room) bathroom. The twin rooms may be shared with another student of the same gender only.

Single rooms with private bathroom may also be available, depending on the availability left. This room type will cost 4200€ for the whole period.

The residence also offers common areas, kitchen and living rooms, study rooms, gym, equipped outdoor areas, maintenance and cleaning service. The price includes all the bills.

The period of room assignment will cover the entire duration of the exchange program: the room will be assigned **from January 20th, 2025 – June 1st, 2025.**

Application and dossier have to be uploaded at Punto Blu by the deadline. Students can choose **from one and up to all the destinations** available for their MSc Program in order of preference.
The dossier must include the following documents:
- Curriculum vitae in English
\- Copy of ID/Passport
- Copy of Bachelor Degree and transcript of records (only for students that did not attend the Bachelor at Bocconi)

accepted until **January, 26 2026 (11:59pm)** at Punto Blu. | | | | | --- | --- | | **ONLINE VIDEO INTERVIEWS** | | **ALL MSc PROGRAMS** | By **January 8, 2026 (11:59pm)**
Only applicants meeting the minimum requirements will receive an ad hoc email with the instructions on how to access the online video interview platform. | | | | | --- | --- | | **LANGUAGES/GMAT or GRE** | | **ALL MSc PROGRAMS** | English/French/GMAT or GRE: students holding a valid certificate should upload it at Punto Blu by the application deadline ( **November 27, 2025 at 11:59pm)**. Otherwise, certificates will be accepted at Punto Blu until **January 26, 2026 (11:59pm)\*\***.
**Important!** TOEFL and IELTS certificates must have been awarded no more than 2 years prior to the Double Degree application deadline. text:
**IMPORTANT:**

\*Students failing to submit all requested documents by the deadlines will be excluded from the DD selections.

\*\*If students apply for a destination where the French proficiency is required (level B2 for ACME - HEC Paris and HEC Montréal; level C1 for GIO - Sciences Po Masters taught in French) and do not satisfy the requirement with a valid certificate, they will be automatically enrolled in the French test organized by the Global Alliances office. The date and time of the internal French test will be shared with the relevant candidates only, at a later stage.

_\\*\\*\\*_ Students may also apply for the Exchange Program; however, they must confirm their participation in the Double Degree before the Exchange Program rankings are published. Students' names will appear in the Exchange Program rankings only if they decline their place in the Double Degree Program, without paying the administrative contribution. Students selected for the Double Degree program will have a few days to confirm their participation by paying the required administrative contribution.

Students who do not accept the destination assigned are automatically excluded from the program. They will not be assigned any other destination.

A destination that was not included among the original application choices, cannot be added among the choices of the candidate after the publishing of the Double Degree rankings (even if spots for said destination are still available).

Once selected, please make sure to start the VISA application process on time.

\*\*\*\*ESS and IM Concentrations candidates will be admitted to the Double Degree Program sub iudice, provided they submit a valid language certificate by January 26, 2026 (if needed). Students who fail to provide a valid certificate by the deadline will be excluded from the Program, even ifpreviously selected.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/double-degree-program/application-selection-timeline-referred-mobility-ay-20262027#addtoany "Show all")

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


Costs

Students selected for the Double Degree program will not be required to pay **any tuition fee to the host institution**, but should confirm their participation to the Double Degree by paying **to Bocconi an administrative contribution of 2.000 Euros.** This contribution will not be returned even if the student does not complete the Double Degree program.

Students will continue to pay fees at Bocconi while abroad.

Some partner institutions may require the payment of administrative fees for different reasons (application, graduation fee, visa costs, insurance, teaching material or other costs). Students are responsible for these expenses, and Bocconi cannot reimburse them.

For all the Double Degree programs with selection at the time of [Bocconi MSc programs' application (LSE, Sciences Po, China MIM, Essec-Bocconi DD, Yale SOM)](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/master-science/application-and-admission/master-science-programs-202425-ay) the administrative contribution is of 2.000 Euros and specific financial scheme applies.

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

Bocconi has developed a double qualification program within the School of Law, offering students a unique portfolio of opportunities with outstanding universities.  Participants will have the chance to live a unique academic and life experience fostering their career in legal professions, becoming able to master the complexity of future challenges for companies and institutions in a plural context.

The Program

The Double Degree program offers 4th-year Law students the possibility to spend their 5th and last year of the Integrated Master of Arts in Law in another prestigious Partner School, specializing in their field of interest.

Provided that both institutions' academic requirements have been met, participants will be receiving a graduate degree both from Bocconi and the foreign institution.

Application

The admission process for the Double Degree program happens during the first semester, while students are enrolled in the fourth year of Bocconi Law program.

The application is filled in at Punto Blu: students can choose their destinations (min. one up to all the available destinations) in order of preference

**Application period**: November/December

**Selection results**: mid/end of February

**Students' confirmation**: mid/end of February (before Themis and Exchange Program rankings)

Additional information on the application procedure, timing and deadlines are presented during the International Opportunities Presentation for Law students and available in the [Restricted Area](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/reserved-area/international-mobility-reserved-area/dd-law-area?check_logged_in=1).

Please note that students can apply for Double Degree, Themis, and Exchange Program at the same time (check the application timeline for each program) however, they must confirm their participation in the Double Degree before the Exchange Program and Themis rankings are published.

Students' names will appear in the Exchange Program rankings only if they decline their place in the Double Degree Program, without paying the administrative contribution. Students selected for the Double Degree program will have a few days to confirm their participation by paying the required administrative contribution of 2000 €

Pre-Requisites

Students enrolled in the 4th year of the Integrated Master of Arts in Law can apply to the Double Degree program if they hold:

- minimum GPA 25/30
- 100% credits 1st year, 60% credits 2nd year, 60% credits 3rd year
- English language proficiency: IELTS min. 7 or TOEFL IBT min. 100.

Please carefully check details and each destination's language requirements in the [Restricted Area](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/reserved-area/international-mobility-reserved-area/dd-law-area)

Selection Criteria

- Outstanding profile and academic curriculum
- Strong motivation for the program and the destinations
- Each applicant will be assigned an Overall Score\* based on the weighted average (70% weight) and on an Individual Assessment Score that considers the on line interview and the application dossier (30% weight).

- \*Credits taken into consideration for calculating the overall score include all exams registered within the official deadline of the September exam session. Exams that do not pertain to the 1st, 2nd and 3rd year will not be taken into consideration when calculating the score.

Destinations

Selected students will spend their entire 5th year at one of the partner institutions.

Bocconi will make every effort to guarantee that selected students can take advantage of the Double Degree opportunity. We are working very closely with our partners in order to ensure academic continuity and the regular delivery of the program. Please consider that in light of the general uncertainty and fluidity of the global situation, teaching methods (physical, blended, online) at Partner Universities may be subject to change. Please consider that temporary national travel and visa restrictions – beyond Bocconi’s control – may further limit access to specific Countries or Regions.

The safety and well-being of students and of all the members of the community remain our utmost priority.

Bocconi keeps monitoring regularly the development of the Risk rate in each country, in accordance with official indications of the MAECI, the local authorities, and the Partner Institutions.

Moreover, in order to ensure a thoughtful and safe travel experience, Bocconi provides International SOS services. Enter your yoU@B (under International Relations / International SOS section) to access the International SOS portal, and to read the Students Travel Risk Policy.

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


The Program

_Bocconi University and Peking University in Beijing (China) have sealed a partnership to offer the “_ [_**Future Leaders International Double Degree Undergraduate Program**_](https://futureleaders.gsm.pku.edu.cn/) _**”**._

[_Peking University_](http://english.pku.edu.cn/) _is ranked among the best universities in China. As a founding member, Bocconi has joined the global consortium of renewed universities offering this Double Degree Program, initiated by_ [_Guanghua School of Management_](http://en.gsm.pku.edu.cn/) [_._](http://www.bri.pku.edu.cn/Home/Programs1/International_Undergraduate_Dual__Degree_Program/Program_Overview.htm)

_This Double Degree program with Peking University aims to select top undergraduate students enrolled in the Bocconi BSc in International Economics and Management (BIEM) who, after studying at Bocconi for the first two academic years, will study for two more academic years at **Guanghua School of Management** of Peking University in Beijing._

_The Program annually enrolls approximately 40 new students from both Mainland China and abroad, to study and live together for two years in Beijing. By studying in two different learning environments in Italy and China, this Double Degree offers a variety of experiences that allow students to challenge themselves and develop a multicultural understanding of an increasingly globalized world._

_Participating students will attend the regular courses of the BSc in International Economics and Management at Bocconi for the first two academic years. During the third and fourth academic years at Guanghua School of Management, students will attend core and elective courses taught in English that introduce them to **major topics in the field of China studies** plus a Chinese language course that is compulsory for all international students._

_Upon fulfilling the graduation requirements of both institutions, students will earn two degrees: the **Bocconi Bachelor Degree in International Economics and Management** as well as **Peking’s Bachelor Degree in Management**._

Application

The admission process for the Double Degree Program with Peking University happens during **the second year-first semester of** the **Bocconi BSc in International Economics and Management**.

There are two steps of selection. The first one is directly managed by Bocconi University. Candidates who successfully pass the first step (between September and October) will proceed to the second step directly managed by Peking University (between November and December).

**Selection Step 1 by Bocconi:**

- Online application: end of September
- Online Interview: mid October
- Selection results: second half of October

**Selection Step 2 by Peking** (for students who pass Step 1):

- Online application: October/November
- Interview: November
- Selection results: second half of December
- Students' confirmation: beginning of January (before Exchange Program rankings)

The above application timeline could slightly change according to Peking’s selection timing.

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


The Program

The Double Degree program offers **graduate students** the possibility to spend the first year at Bocconi, acquiring core knowledge and skills, and the second year in another prestigious Partner School specializing in their field of interest. Provided that both institutions' academic requirements have been met, participants will be receiving a graduate degree both from Bocconi and the foreign institution.

Application

The admission process for the Double Degree program happens during the **first semester**, while students are already enrolled in the **first year of Bocconi MSc program**.

- **Application**: November/December
- **Selection results**: end of January (ESS; M); end of February (other MSc's)
- **Students' confirmation**: March (before Exchange Program rankings)

Additional information related to the application procedure, timing and deadlines are presented during the International Opportunities Presentation for enrolled Bocconi MSc students and available on You@B Agenda (International Relations section).

Please note that students can apply both for Double Degree and Exchange (check the application timeline for each program).

Pre-Requisites

- Undegraduate degree with a minimum grade of 100/110 (Italian grading scale). N.B. students with a foreign Bachelor degree need to have their final grade converted into the Italian system. If the grade is slightly below 100/110, applications may still be considered for evaluation by the Selection Committee;
- English language proficiency: most partner schools accept the enrollment in a Bocconi MSc entirely taught in English as proof of the English knowledge. If the school has additional English language requirements, you can find them in the Fact Sheets available in the Reserved Area;
- Specific partner school requirements (eg. GMAT/GRE; minimum IELTS threshold; knowledge of a second foreign language) are also indicated in the Fact Sheets available in the Reserved Area.

Selection Criteria

- Outstanding Profile and academic curriculum
- Motivation for the program and the destinations

Each applicant will be assigned an Overall Score based on Bachelor Degree Final Grade (70% weight) and an Individual Assessment Score (30% weight).

**IMPORTANT:**

- The final decision on the admission is up to the Partner School for which the student has been selected.
- Partner institutions may not accept Bocconi students holding a Bachelor degree issued in the same country of that institution; or applicants who were rejected for (or refused participation to) Graduate Programs offered by that institution; or nationals of the same country of the partner school.

Students selected for the Double Degree, in an European destination adhering to the Erasmus+ Program, are automatically eligible for the **Erasmus+ scholarship**. For further information, please check [Erasmus+ scholarship dedicated webpage](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/erasmus-scholarship "Erasmus+ Scholarship") [.](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/services/erasmus-scholarship-study-abroad)

text:
Students who do not reply to selection notifications within the outlined deadline will be excluded from the Program. Failure to respond will be considered as a **withdrawal** from the Exchange Program.

Selected students can withdraw from the exchange program **at any time** during the academic year, until their scheduled departure. Selected students who accept and decide to withdraw at a later stage must send a message through [B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/articles/11847669036690-I-wish-to-withdraw-from-the-Exchange-Program-what-should-I-do).

While there is no specific deadline for withdrawal, we kindly invite students to inform us as soon as the decision is made so that another student from the waiting list may have the opportunity to participate in the Exchange Program.

Students who do not accept the destination they have been assigned to (among the choices in the application), are automatically excluded from the current program and **will not be assigned to any other destination**.

Selected students who decide to withdraw will not be allowed to apply for the Free-Mover Semester initiative nor any further selection of the Exchange Program within their current study cycle.

Students who meet all the requirements and are not selected will be placed on the Waiting List for the destinations listed in their application.

Students on the Waiting List who are subsequently selected will receive a nomination email from the Study Abroad Office and will need to respond **within** the outlined **deadline** confirming or declining the proposed destination. Failure to do so within the specified timeframe will result in being considered as a definitive **withdrawal** from the Exchange Program.

The Study Abroad Office will continue to select students from the Waiting List in case receiving withdrawals from departing students. This option is only possible according to the nomination deadline set by partner universities and may vary from each destination. For this reason, please note that the selection from the waiting list will predominantly be done for the **Spring Term.**

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/apply-accept-or-withdraw#addtoany "Show all")

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

Exchange Program 2026/27

Applications for the Exchange Program must be submitted within the deadlines outlined on our website.

It is crucial to upload any required documents correctly and accept the assigned destination by the deadline specified in the communication from the Study Abroad Office.

If you are not selected for the exchange or wish to withdraw, please refer to the information in the boxes below.

How to apply

To participate in the Exchange Program, the application must be submitted at Punto Blu by the specified [deadline](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program).

If the Academic Requirements are satisfied, students will have access to the application and, if necessary, will be able to upload the required certificates based on the destinations they wish to apply for.

Students will be able to see only the destinations for which they satisfy the requirements and can indicate **up to 10 destinations\*** in order of preference (ie: the choice n. 1 is the most preferred destination).

Once the application is completed, students are strongly advised to verify the completion of the application by saving all entered data and downloading the **application receipt** before the deadline.

It is in the student’s best interest to apply **only** to destinations they are willing to depart for.

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


Exchange Program 2026-27

Selection is based on merit evaluation, taking into consideration the student's **academic weighted average** and the **number of credits**.

**Student WA + \[0.1 x (Student NC - MIN NC required)\]**

0.1 points will be attributed for each credit above the minimum number of credits required (40% of 1st year credits registered). These points will be added to the weighted credit average.

The calculation of credits thus includes all the exams (part of the 1st year study plan and 2nd year - 1st semester - study plan) officially taken by **February 3rd, 2026** (included), end of the ordinary exam session.

If the student wants to be considered eligible for a specific destination, **in case of additional language requirements** are specified, he/she **must have a certificate valid for both terms indicated on the brochure.**

When **rankings** are published, students will be notified the assigned destination.

**Please note:**

- Courses subdivided in moduleswill be considered as **separated courses** for the academic requirements and exchange score calculation: they can be viewed by printing the unofficial academic transcript at the Punto Blu.

- If applicants have equal final scores for a specific destination and there is one last slot available, the students' preferences linked to the destination will be used as criteria in order to set the ranking. In case of equal preferences, priority will be given to the student who applied earlier (considering hh:mm:ss).

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/bsc-international-government-selection-criteria#addtoany "Show all")

metadata.title: BSc in International Government Selection Criteria | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: BSc in International Government Selection Criteria | Bocconi University

text:
To be eligible for the Exchange Program students must:
- **Enrollment:** be regularly enrolled in the 2nd year of the BSc in International Politics and Government
- **Credits:** have 40% of 1st-year credits registered on their transcript.
Please note that:
- The calculation of GPA and credits is based on Bocconi exams taken **by the end of the winter exams session**: 3rd February 2026 for the AY 2026/27 selection. **Make sure all your grades are registered** in your Study Plan before the Exchange Program application closes, as only those exams will be considered for the rankings;

- If you would like to convert the English certificate to have the exam passed in your Bocconi career and counting for the Exchange program score, it is essential to do it in advance, and in any cases before February 3rd 2026. For further instructions and timeline, please visit the Bocconi [Language Center's website](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/language-certification-recognition);
- Exams which are not part of the 1st year and 2nd year - 1st semester - study plan are not included, as they must be taken during the ordinary exam session;

- Courses subdivided in moduleswill be considered as separated courses for the minimum academic requirements and exchange score calculation;

- Some destinations might require a specific minimum GPA. When necessary, the details can be found on the Brochure;

- If the applicant moves from a bachelor's degree course to another one, exams which are not part of the 1st year and 2nd year - 1st semester - of the NEW study plan are not included.
Language Requirements

The enrollment in the BSc in International Politics and Government is sufficient to fulfill the **minimum** language requirements for English.

In addition to the minimum requirements for the Exchange program, some partner universities have **additional** language requirements. You can check them in our destination brochure.

Please read carefully **both** sections below:

To be eligible for the Exchange Program it is always required to have a minimum level of English, French, German, Spanish according to the institutions you are applying for.

**Level B2** for **English** proved automatically throughenrollment in the BSc in International Politics and Government in English.

**Level B1** or higher for **French, German and Spanish,** provedby **one** of the options listed below:
- Citizenship
- International Certification
- University Language Exam
- Enrollment in the Bocconi Second Language Course
- Secondary School Diploma - Please contact the Study Abroad office providing any document which can be useful to evaluate your case.
**How to fulfill the Minimum Language Requirements for French, German, or Spanish:**

text:
| | | | --- | --- | | Citizenship | Citizens from a country where French, German or Spanish is the first language, can certify their **minimum** language proficiency by submitting their passport at the time of application to the program at Punto Blu. | | Bocconi Language Exam | Applicants with the exam already registered in their Bocconi career **do not have** to submit any international certification at the time of application to the program unless the university they are applying to has additional requirements. | | International Certifications for Other Languages | - Certificates must be recognized by [Bocconi Language Center](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/certifications-recognized-bachelor-science-programs) with an equal or higher level of B1 and must submitted at the time of application. For the minimum scores accepted, please refer to the Bocconi Language Center tables;
- For French, the DELF B1 certificate is accepted;
- For Spanish, the SIELE GLOBAL certificate at B1 level in all its components is accepted, in on-site or remote mode;
- Certificates must be awarded no more than **3 years** prior the Exchange Program application deadline. | | Enrollment in Bocconi Second Language Course | - When the language of selection is French, German or Spanish, BSc in International Politics and Government applicants can **temporarily** meet the requirement if enrolled in the Bocconi course of the required language;
- the final exam must be passed and **registered** **before departure;**
- selected students who do not pass the language exam by the deadline, will no longer be eligible to leave for the exchange and will be **automatically** **excluded** from the program. | To apply for destinations with the additional language requirements all Bocconi students, **even native speakers**, must submit an international certificate to be uploaded on Punto Blu at the time of application(e.g. a British student must submit TOEFL or IELTS for the University of Pennsylvania). Students must hold **one of the alternatives** explicitly listed in the additional language requirements for the destinations they are applying to (e.g. international certificate or enrollment, where applicable). For the language additional requirement, please note that: - Applicants will need to satisfy **both** minimum and additional requirements; - FCE, CAE, CPE, TOEFL My Best Scores, and IELTS General Training are **not** accepted; - TOEFL and IELTS Academic certificates must be issued no more than **2 years** **before the departure date;** - All other certificates must be issued no more than **3 years before the departure date;** - The Bocconi Study Abroad Office accepts IELTS Online, TOEFL Home Edition, and IELTS One Skill Retake to meet additional language requirements. However, **many institutions may require the** **standard in-person version**. Therefore, we strongly recommend choosing the most widely accepted format, as it cannot be excluded that some partner institutions may request the standard certificate to be submitted within a short timeframe after the Bocconi selection and during the Host School application process; - For students applying for a destination with limited slots per semester, the certificate needs to be valid at the beginning of the **latest semester available**(e.g. if you are interested in a destination where the latest departure date is in April 2026, your IELTS/TOEFL must have been obtained no earlier than April 2024). [More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/bsc-international-politics-and-government-requirements#addtoany "Show all")

text:
List of destinations for a specific Academic Year with links to Partner Universities' websites and/or fact sheets, other than search parameters based on levels, degree programs, etc.

In this Brochure, you can have also an overview of the list of courses converted in the previous exchange editions.

#### Citizenship and visa requirements

International students or students with dual citizenship cannot apply for some Schools in the countries for which they hold current citizenship. Details will be available in the 2026-27 Destinations Brochure.

Rules for entry the Countries may differ according to students’ citizenship. It is up to the student to verify the suitable procedures for obtaining the appropriate visa, if required, and any other documents necessary for the Exchange Program to be carried out in full compliance with local laws.

#### Accommodation

Bocconi Study Abroad Office is not in charge of students' accommodation; the host university will provide some help and information directly to selected students (if available).

The accommodation offered by the host universities abroad can vary: check each partner school's website/factsheet and read former exchange students' reports in the [Reserved Area](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/reserved-area/international-mobility-reserved-area) for details.

#### Costs and scolarships

Students who study abroad pay regular Bocconi tuition for the semester they are abroad, they do not have to pay fees to the partner institution.

Consider that [Scholarships for international mobility](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/funding/international-mobility-grant) are available and allocated by the Fees Funding and Housing Office, in cooperation with Università Bocconi. Moreover, [Erasmus + scholarships](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/erasmus-scholarship) (for destinations included in the Erasmus +) and other possible scholarships (to be allocated based on specific agreements with partner Institutions) are available.

#### Guides to the Exchange Program

[**BSc in International Politics and Government**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/Guide%20to%20the%20Exchange%20Program-BIG_26-27%20%281%29.pdf "BSc in International Politics and Government")

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)



The Exchange Program offers students a unique international experience, allowing them to spend a term abroad at one of our 293 partner universities in 56 countries across five continents.

Through our bilateral agreements, students attending the program will carry out a study term, without paying tuition fees at the host school. However, students are responsible for covering their own travel expenses, accommodation, meals, and any other associated costs. Scholarships, such as those offered by ISU and Erasmus, are available. Courses completed at the partner institution can be recognized, subject to specific rules and procedures that students must adhere to.

Students can access the Exchange Program only once during a study program, through an application, upon possession of specific requirements. The Program entails a selection, with subsequent publication of the ranking, which takes place annually.

For more details, please refer to the sections below.

For A.Y. 2026-27

### Timing and deadlines

- Deadline for registering examinations (corresponding to the end of the ordinary winter exam period)\*: **February 3rd, 2026.**

- Publication of Brochures: **mid February 2026.**

- Application opens: **February 25th, 2026.**

- Deadline for application at Punto Blu and uploading of Language certificates\*\*: **March 3rd, 2026 at 3 pm (Italian time).**

- Results and confirmation: **end of March/beginning of April 2026.**

- For students selected for a destination on [sub iudice basis](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/articles/10798686905874-I-was-selected-sub-judice-for-a-destination-what-does-that-mean), deadline for passing the study plan second language oral exam: **by the June/July 2026 exam session, for all the programs. By the departure date, for BIG students.**

- For IM Global Experience and FIN Global Experience Track students, deadline for the submission of the Curriculum Vitae and the Video Interview: **March 3rd, 2026 at 3 pm (Italian time)**.

For the method for collecting the material please check [IM Global Experience and FIN Global Experience Track Selection Criteria.](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/im-global-experience-selection-criteria "https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/im-global-experience-selection-criteria")

\*To ensure that the **first language exam** is recorded in your study plan and considered in the **Exchange Program score calculation** through the **conversion of an international certificate**, it is essential to follow the timeline set by the Language Center. For additional details, please refer to the [dedicated page](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/language-certification-recognition) on the Bocconi Language Center's website.

\*\*If you need a certificate in order to comply with the language requirements, we strongly recommend that you take it as soon as possible. Please allow **enough time to receive the certificate**, always considering the above mentioned deadlines.

[**Undergraduate Exchange Program**\\
\\
including the Exchange Program integrated in the BSc in International Politics and Government](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/undergraduate-exchange-program " ")

[**Graduate Exchange Program**\\
\\
including the Exchange Program integrated in:\\
\\
- the MSc in International Management Global Experience\\
- the MSc in Finance Global Experience Track](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/graduate-exchange-program " ")

[**Law Exchange Program**\\
\\
including the Exchange Program in the BSc in Global Law](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/law-exchange-program " ")

text:
## For more information

[![B in Touch](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/images/b_in_touch_logo_0.png)\\
\\
**Read the FAQs and send Requests**](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/it/categories/4407343627794-Outgoing-Exchange-Program "Read the FAQs and send Requests")

[![](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/images/youb_0.png)\\
\\
**Book an Appointment through yoU@B**](https://app.unibocconi.it/Appointments/Studente "Book an Appointment through yoU@B")

[![Telefono](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/images/immagine_2.png)\\
\\
**Phone us on weekdays from 3 pm to 4 pm**](tel: +39-02-5836-2248 "Phone us on weekdays from 3 pm to 4 pm")

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program#addtoany "Show all")

Before the selection | | | --- | | **1\. Check the list of courses previously available**
When considering the various alternatives in terms of schools and destinations, look at the information on the courses offered by the host university (indicated in the [Exchange destinations](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/international+relations/bocconi+students/graduate+school/study+abroad/what/exchange+program/exchange+program_monzini+2008+12+09+10+04)), visit the list of courses taken abroad in the [Course history](https://iroffer.unibocconi.it/programs), and read the experience of the former exchange [students' reports](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/international+relations/restricted+area). 
Where **no course information is available**, these are new schools, schools reintroduced to the network after a few years, or destinations where there have been no students from your degree program recently.
Please consider that at the Pre-selection stage, **it is not possible to know with certainty which courses will be available at the partner schools**. In most cases, the updated course offer for your mobility year does not yet exist, and part of the other schools' offerings may not be open to Exchange students, or may have limited seats, or may require academic prerequisites. | | **2\. Check the courses that are forbidden to take abroad**
Please consider, that some courses cannot be validated if taken abroad:
- computer tests
- compulsory courses from another specific degree course
- banned courses: see List of compulsory courses which cannot be recognized (see Guide to the University chapter 7.14). | | **3\. It is not necessary to change your Study Plan in advance**
In order to give more flexibility in the courses choices, Bocconi students participating in International programs have the possibility to modify their study plan one extra time once back from the exchange through Punto Blu available in the yoU@B student Diary. (see Guide to the University chapter 9.3.4). | Before the departure for the Exchange Program 2025-26

| | | --- | | **1) Check the Academic Offer at the Partner School**
The official list of courses available at the partner university will be given after the application to the partner school, according to their schedule. It is your responsibility to look at the course content summaries in the [online course profiles](http://didattica.unibocconi.it/ts/home_insegnamenti.php?cambial=E) and find possible matches with the courses taught at the partner schools through the above-mentioned web tools.
Consider that both courses with and without correspondence (need to be validated), but they involve different administrative procedures for you to follow:
- **Conversion with correspondence**

These courses have a Bocconi equivalent course and must be approved on the Recognition Platform by the professor responsible for the corresponding course. For DSBA and IM Global Experience students, approval is given by the Degree Program Director or delegate.

These conversions can be inserted in your Bocconi career as **mandatory, elective,** or **additional** exams (in this last scenario, please always verify on the Guides to the University cap 9. the Study Plan Office rules for additional educational activities exam insertion).

**Language courses:** curricular languages (i.e. those included in the study plan as first and second foreign language) can be recognized through the exchange program, upon approval.

Languages taken as additional subjects (“in sovrannumero”) can only be registered if passed internally at Bocconi or converted through an international certification included in the official list published by the Bocconi Language Center.

Credits referring to foreign languages are included in the maximum number of 30 ECTS allowed for the conversion.

- **Conversion without correspondence**

These conversions do not have a corresponding course at Bocconi and they will be approved, through the Recognition Platform, by the Program Director who has an overall view of your Study Plan. These conversions can be inserted in your Bocconi career either as **elective** or **additional** exams (in this last scenario, please always verify on the Guides to the University cap 9. the Study Plan Office rules for additional educational activities). | | **2) Check the credits at the partner School**
The Bocconi credits system is based on the ECTS, the European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System.
**For courses with Bocconi correspondence, after the conversion, you will have in your Study Plan the number of credits assigned to the correspondent Bocconi course; for courses without Bocconi correspondence, the number of credits assigned to a Bocconi elective course (6 cfu).**
Please note that, in many partner schools, especially outside Europe, there might be a different credit system.
Therefore, check within the recognition platform for credit equivalency before making conversion requests. |

| **3) Start inserting your requests**
If the exam you want to take abroad is not available on the list on the Recognition Platform you can enter a new request.
In some cases, it is possible that you will find many courses already approved (green flag). If you select these courses you do not need to get any preliminary approval: once back, after you send the recognition request, these courses will be automatically registered into your study plan. (see Sez. Upon return).
Remember that in order to obtain a preliminary approval, for each new request student must send to the professor/Director an overall description of the course you wish to attend abroad. The Bocconi professor/Director has to receive the pdf version of the analytic syllabus of the course (e.g. course description and level - objectives, contents, lectures and/or special workloads, written/oral exam, the number of credits assigned to the course and/or the number of classroom and lecture hours, the local instructor's contacts). 
**It is not possible to have validated 1 course taken abroad with 2 Bocconi courses**; on the other hand, 2 or more courses taken abroad can be either recognized into 1 Bocconi course, or can be recognized into 1 elective without correspondence.
In case of two or more foreign exams covering one Bocconi exam, the final grade is obtained by the grade point average of the corresponding Bocconi grades obtained, using the foreign credit points specified on the Transcript issued by the host university as the weighted coefficient. 
If you need more information on the procedures please check our video tutorial through [B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/it/sections/4407350015762-Riconoscimento-esami-Exchange-Program). |

| | | --- | | **1) Check the Academic Offer at the Partner School**
The official list of courses available at the partner university will be provided after your application to the partner school, according to their schedule. It is your responsibility to review the course content summaries in the online course profiles and identify possible matches with the courses offered at the partner universities through the above-mentioned web tools.
Starting from the 2026/27 academic year, **ALL** recognition requests - **whether with or without course correspondence** \- will be evaluated by the Director of your Degree Program and/or the Assistant to the Program Director.
When planning your requests, please consider that it is always at the discretion of the Program Director to evaluate whether the courses taken abroad is eligible for a 1:1 conversion in terms of credits/content
In terms of credits, as a **general guideline**
- courses taken abroad should cover **at least 2/3 of the credits** of the corresponding Bocconi course for 1:1 recognition. (i.e a course worth 8 credits at Bocconi requires at least 5.5 equivalent credits abroad).
- A maximum of three (3) courses below the 2/3 threshold may **still be approved** for 1:1 recognition when deemed appropriate by the Director. Alternatively, students may always request the combination of two foreign courses to reach the equivalence with one Bocconi course.
- Students can submit up to a maximum of **12 new requests** for authorization for recognition. This does not include already existing authorizations that the student can select.
The above criteria are intended as general guidelines to support the evaluation process. **The final decision on course recognition and credit conversion is always at the sole discretion of the Program Director.**
- **Conversion with correspondence**

These courses have a Bocconi equivalent course and must be approved on the Recognition Platform by the Degree Program Director. These conversions can be included in your Bocconi career as mandatory, elective, or additional courses.

**Language courses:** curricular languages (i.e. those included in the study plan as first and second foreign language) can be recognized through the exchange program, upon approval.

Languages taken as additional subjects (“in sovrannumero”) can only be registered if passed internally at Bocconi or converted through an international certification included in the official list published by the Bocconi Language Center.

Credits referring to foreign languages are included in the maximum number of 30 ECTS allowed for the conversion.
- **Conversion without correspondence**

These conversions do not have a corresponding course at Bocconi and they will be approved, through the Recognition Platform, by the Program Director who has an overall view of your Study Plan. These conversions can be inserted in your Bocconi career either as elective or **additional** exams (in this last scenario, please always verify on the Guides to the University the Study Plan Office rules for additional educational activities).
**Please note:** compulsory courses that do not have an exact correspondence in terms of content **may still be approved by the Program Director, provided they belong to the same Settore Scientifico Disciplinare (SSD**) or – when the sector is particularly broad – to the relevant macro-discipline. |

| **2) Check the credits at the partner School**
The Bocconi credits system is based on the ECTS, the European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System.
**For courses with Bocconi correspondence, after the conversion, you will have in your Study Plan the number of credits assigned to the correspondent Bocconi course; for courses without Bocconi correspondence, the number of credits assigned to a Bocconi elective course (6 cfu).**
Please note that, in many partner schools, especially outside Europe, there might be a different credit system. Therefore check within the recognition platform for credit equivalency before making conversion requests. | | **3) Start inserting your requests**
If the exam you want to take abroad is not available on the list on the Recognition Platform you can enter a new request.
In some cases, it is possible that you will find many courses already approved (green flag). If you select these courses you do not need to get any preliminary approval: once back, after you send the recognition request, these courses will be automatically registered into your study plan.(see sez. Upon return)
**It is not possible to have validated 1 course taken abroad with 2 Bocconi courses;** on the other hand, 2 or more courses taken abroad can be either recognized into 1 Bocconi course, or can be recognized into 1 elective without correspondence. 
In case of two or more foreign exams covering one Bocconi exam, the final grade is obtained by the grade point average of the corresponding Bocconi grades obtained, using the foreign credit points specified on the Transcript issued by the host university as the weighted coefficient. 
Remember that in order to obtain a preliminary approval, for each new request student must send to the Director an overall description of the course you wish to attend abroad. The Bocconi Director has to receive the pdf version of the analytic syllabus of the course (e.g. course description and level - objectives, contents, lectures and/or special workloads, written/oral exam, the number of credits assigned to the course and/or the number of classroom and lecture hours, the local instructor's contacts).
If you need more information on this procedure, please check our video tutorial through [B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/it/sections/4407350015762-Riconoscimento-esami-Exchange-Program). |

Academic Recognition & Learning Agreement - Erasmus Students Please if you need more information on the OLA visit our website at this [link](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/erasmus-scholarship-area). Upon return | | | --- | | **1) Wait for the Transcript of Records**
Only at the end of the Exchange, students can finalize the recognition of the exams taken abroad by entering a request through the Recognition platform available in the yoU@B Diary.
The requests can be submitted by students **only if the final and official Transcript of Records** with grades of the exams taken abroad has been received (electronic version is allowed).
The time to receive the transcript can vary from school to school and in some cases, it **can take months** from the end of the term abroad: students that receive the Transcript at their home address shall upload a copy of it through the Recognition platform, available by the yoU@B student Diary.
Please remember that **there is no deadline** for the recognition procedure, but students need to pay attention to the Study Plan completion date depending on the graduation session they want to join. | | **2) Submit the request to convert the exams taken abroad**
Students are allowed to send **only one** request for recognition, therefore it is important that this request includes all of the courses that the student may wish to convert from their experience abroad. | | **3) Pay attention to the graduation timeline**
Students will be informed via their yoU@B student Diary when the request has been processed.
For students taking part in the Exchange Program during the **Spring Term**: consider that the timing of semesters and Transcript release might not be aligned with Bocconi timeline.
Therefore, it may not be possible to graduate in July and/or to be compliant for the **2° Round of MSc applications** at Bocconi due to the time needed to transfer exams taken abroad. | Conversion Tables Bocconi has worked out differentiated conversion tables (Tabelle di Conversione) for each Partner School in order to ensure fair grade conversion between different academic contexts. Conversion tables imply a one-to-one correspondence between every Partners grades and a Bocconi one. They are based on information given by Partner Schools about their grading distribution (percentages, curves of distribution, description of grades and if any Country specifics) and compared to Bocconi grading system. Being based on such one-to-one association, **some Bocconi grades cannot find any correspondence on some Partners' grading scales**; therefore, **you must be aware that not all the 13 Bocconi grades or the laude (from 18 to 30/30) can be assigned.** After selection, it will be possible to see, through Punto Blu, the conversion table of the school you have been selected for. To access the conversion table from Punto Blu, you can select the "International Mobility" option from the right menu, then choose "Mobility Calls," position yourself, and click on the magnifying glass icon to the right of the "Exchange Program call" while scrolling through the various tabs of the "Call Details." **\\*\\* For more details, please refer to the Guide to the Exchange Program on the** [**home page**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program "Exchange Program") **\*\*** [More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/graduate-academic-recognition#addtoany "Show all")

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

Exchange Program 2026-27

**ACADEMIC RECOGNITION  OF COURSES TAKEN ABROAD**

If you are interested in taking part in the exchange program, please note that you will need to follow specific rules and a procedure to have the examinations taken abroad converted into the Università Bocconi.

First of all, you have to know that **only at the end** of the Exchange Program it is possible to convert the exams taken abroad. Students **must convert a minimum of 1 exam** in order to have the Exchange Program validated in the Bocconi career.

Graduate Exchange students will be able to **recognize a maximum of 30 Bocconi credits** (with and/or without correspondence) additional educational activities included, without considering the credits of each course.

**PLEASE NOTE:** proctored exams in Bocconi are not allowed.

Procedures for Graduate Programs in:
- **IM Global Experience**
- **FIN Global Experience Track**

- [Guide to the Exchange program 2026-27](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Guide%20to%20the%20Exchange%20Program-2026-27.pdf?VersionId=m6ALVg3U1nKXSWVv.D9hlczxd3SlzTve)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/graduate-exchange-program#addtoany "Show all")

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

The Exchange Program is open to graduate students regularly **enrolled in their 1st year** of a Bocconi Master Degree.  Students will be able to attend a single term at one of the foreign universities **during their 2nd year**, after passing a selection, and just during the academic year the selection is related to.

**\\*\\* For more details, please refer to the Guide to the Exchange Program below\*\***

Procedures for Graduate Programs

To be eligible for the Exchange Program all students need to meet the following academic requirements:
- **GPA**: minimum 22/30 of average weighted on credits, the average is not rounded.

- **Credits**: at least 16 credits registered
Please note that:
- The calculation of GPA and credits is based on Bocconi exams taken **by the end of the winter exams session**: 3rd February 2026 for the AY 2026/27 selection. **Make sure all your grades are registered** in your Study Plan before the Exchange Program application closes, as only those exams will be considered for the rankings;

- Exams which are not part of the 1st year study plan - 1st semester - are not included as the exams must be taken in the ordinary exam session;

- **Courses subdivided in modules** will be considered as separated courses for the minimum academic requirements and exchange score calculation;

- Some destinations might require a specific minimum GPA. When necessary, the details can be found on the Brochure;

- If the applicant moves from an MSc program to another one, exams which are not part of the 1st year - 1st semester of the NEW MSc program study plan will not be included.
Language Requirements

**Minimum** language requirements are sufficient to apply for all the destinations that do not state further requirements other than the language of selection inside the destination brochure.

In addition to the minimum requirements for the Exchange program, some partner universities have **additional** language requirements. You can check them in our destination brochure.

Please read carefully **both** sections below:

To be eligible for the Exchange Program it is always required to have a minimum level of English, French, German, Spanish or Portuguese according to the institutions you are applying for.

**Level B2** for **English proved automatically** throughenrollment in a **MSc. taught in English.**

**Level B1** or higher for **French, German and Spanish,** proved by **one** of the options listed below:
- Citizenship
- International Certification
- University Language Exam
- Secondary School Diploma or Bachelor's Degree - Please contact the Study Abroad office providing any document which can be useful to evaluate your case
- Previous Exchange Program completed - Please contact the Study Abroad office providing any document which can be useful to evaluate your case
**Level A2** or higher for **Portuguese,** proved by **one** of the options listed before or by the enrollment to the Bocconi Language Center course.

**How to fulfill the Minimum Language Requirements:**

| | | | --- | --- | | Citizenship | Citizens from a country where French, German, Portuguese or Spanish is the first language, can certify their **minimum** language proficiency by submitting their passport at the time of application to the program at Punto Blu. | | University Language Exam | Applicants with the exam already registered in their undergraduate Bocconi career **do not have** to submit any international certification at the time of application to the program unless the university they are applying to has additional requirements.
Students who passed the exam at a University other than Bocconi must provide proof of **level** and **grade** during the application procedure. | | International Certifications for French, German and Spanish | - Certificates must be recognized by [Bocconi Language Center](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/certifications-recognized-master-science-programs) with an equal or higher level of B1 and must submitted at the time of application. For the minimum scores accepted, please refer to the Bocconi Language Center tables;
- For French, the DELF B1 certificate is accepted;
- For Spanish, the SIELE GLOBAL certificate at B1 level in all its components is accepted, in on-site or remote mode;
- Certificates must be awarded no more than **3 years prior** the Exchange Program **application deadline.** | | Portuguese Language | Students who do not meet the Portuguese language requirement [must attend and **complete**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/supplementary-activities/supplementary-activities) the [Portuguese Language course](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/supplementary-activities/supplementary-activities-0) provided by Bocconi Language Center.
Alternatively, students can present a CIPLE or CELPE-Bras A2 language certificate directly to our office. The certificate must be sent to our office [through B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/sections/4407343663890-Before-departure-and-during-the-Exchange-Program) **by July and, in any case, before the departure date.**
Please note that the release of official certificate results may take approximately two months; students are therefore strongly advised to **carefully plan the timing of the exam and the submission of the certificate**. | To apply for destinations with the additional language requirements all Bocconi students, **even native speakers**, must submit an international certificate to be uploaded on Punto Blu at the time of application (e.g. a British student must submit TOEFL or IELTS for the University of Pennsylvania). Students must hold **one of the alternatives** explicitly listed in the additional language requirements for the destinations they are applying to (e.g. international certificate **or** enrollment, where applicable). For the language additional requirement, please note that:

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


Exchange Program 2026-27

Selection is based on **merit** evaluation, taking into consideration the following elements:

- Student academic weighted credit average ( **WA):** 50%

- Number of credits earned ( **NC)** divided by the maximum number of credits that can be earned ( **MAX NC):** 20%

- Final degree grade for the Bachelor program - **Laurea Triennale**: 30%

The score formula will be the following:


The result will be then multiplied by 1000. That will be your final score.

The calculation of credits thus includes all the exams of the 1st year - 1st semester - study plan officially taken by **February 3rd, 2026** (included), end of the ordinary exam session.

Exams which are not part of the 1st year - 1st semester study plan( **internship, languages, anticipated exams and anticipated curricular supplementary activities)** are not included.

Students can indicate up to 10 exchange destinations in order of preference. The Study Abroad office will elaborate the ranking, according to the above-mentioned criteria. Each student will be assigned to the first destination for which there are still places available among the ones s/he listed in her/his application in order of preference.

**Please note:**

- Courses subdivided in moduleswill be considered as **separated courses** for the academic requirements and exchange score calculation.

- If applicants have equal final scores for a specific destination and there is one last slot available, the students' preferences linked to the destination will be used as criteria in order to set the ranking. In case of equal preferences, priority will be given to the student who applied earlier (considering hh:mm:ss).

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/graduate-selection-criteria#addtoany "Show all")

The main features of the Exchange program for the students are **:**
- **Reserved** slots available
- **Guaranteed** allocation
- **Dedicated** selection process
The **application** opens once a year, during the **1st year** of the study cycle.

The two mobility programs follow the **same procedures**. Each program will have dedicated brochures destinations and the **selection process** will be carried out **independently** for each program.

Academic Requirements

To be eligible for the Exchange Program all students need to meet the following academic requirements:
- **Enrollment** to the 1st year of the program
Please note that:
- The calculation of GPA and credits is based on Bocconi exams taken **by the end of the winter exams session**: 3rd February 2026 for the AY 2026/27 selection. **Make sure all your grades are registered** in your Study Plan before the Exchange Program application closes, as only those exams will be considered for the rankings;

- Exams which are not part of the 1st year study plan - 1st semester - are not included as the exams must be taken in the ordinary exam session;

- **Courses subdivided in modules** will be considered as separated courses for the minimum academic requirements and exchange score calculation;

- Some destinations might require a specific minimum GPA. When necessary, the details can be found on the Brochure;

- If the applicant moves from an MSc program to another one, exams which are not part of the 1st year - 1st semester of the NEW MSc program study plan will not be included.
Language Requirements

The enrollment in the Master of Science program taught in English is sufficient to fulfill the **minimum** language requirements for English. In addition to the minimum requirements for the Exchange program, some partner universities have **additional** language requirements. You will be able to check them in our destination Brochure once available.

Please read carefully **both** sections below:

To be eligible for the Exchange Program it is always required to have a minimum level of English, French, German, Spanish or Portuguese according to the institutions you are applying for.

**Level B2** in **English, proved automatically** through the enrollment in a degree program taught in English.

**Level B1** or higher for **French, German and Spanish,** proved by one of the options listed below:
- Citizenship
- International Certification
- University Language Exam
- Secondary School Diploma or Bachelor's Degree - Please contact the Study Abroad office providing any document which can be useful to evaluate your case
- Previous Exchange Program completed - Please contact the Study Abroad office providing any document which can be useful to evaluate your case
**How to fulfill the Minimum Language Requirements for French, German or Spanish:**

Students who passed the exam at a University other than Bocconi must provide proof of **level** and **grade** during the application procedure. | | International Certifications | - Certificates must be recognized by [Bocconi Language Center](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/certifications-recognized-bachelor-science-programs) with an equal or higher level of B1 and must submitted at the time of application. For the minimum scores accepted, please refer to the Bocconi Language Center tables;

- For Spanish, SIELE GLOBAL is accepted in on-site or remote mode;
- Certificates must be awarded no more than **3 years** prior the Exchange Program application deadline. | To apply for destinations with the additional language requirements all Bocconi students, **even native speakers**, must submit an international certificate to be uploaded on Punto Blu at the time of application (e.g. a British student must submit TOEFL or IELTS for the University of Pennsylvania). Students must hold **one of the alternatives** explicitly listed in the additional language requirements for the destinations they are applying to (e.g. international certificate **or** enrollment, where applicable). For the language additional requirement, please note that: - Applicants will need to satisfy **both** minimum and additional requirements. - FCE, CAE, CPE, TOEFL My Best Scores, and IELTS General Training are **not** accepted. - TOEFL and IELTS Academic certificates must be issued no more than **2 years** **before the departure date.** - All other certificates must be issued no more than **3 years before the departure date.** - The Bocconi Study Abroad Office accepts IELTS Online, TOEFL Home Edition, and IELTS One Skill Retake to meet additional language requirements. However, **many institutions may require the** **standard in-person version**. Therefore, we strongly recommend choosing the most widely accepted format, as it cannot be excluded that some partner institutions may request the standard certificate to be submitted within a short timeframe after the Bocconi selection and during the Host School application process. - For students applying for a destination with limited slots per semester, the certificate needs to be valid at the beginning of the **latest semester available** (e.g. if you are interested in a destination where the latest departure date is in April 2027, your IELTS/TOEFL must have been obtained no earlier than April 2025). text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

Exchange Program 2026-27

Both the MSc in International Management Global Experience and in Finance Global Experience Track include a **mandatory experience abroad**, either an Exchange program or an internship. It is **highly recommended** to carry out the mobility in a country other than the student's country of origin.
