text:
Before the selection | | | --- | | **1) Check the list of courses previously available**
When considering the various alternatives in terms of schools and destinations, look at the information on the courses offered by the host university (indicated in the [Exchange destinations](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/international+relations/exchange+program_bracco+2010+07+28+03+31)), visit the list of courses taken abroad in the [Course history](https://iroffer.unibocconi.it/programs), and read the experience of the former exchange [students' reports](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/international+relations/restricted+area). 
Where no course information is available, these are new schools, schools reintroduced to the network after a few years, or destinations where there have been no students from your degree program recently.
Please consider that at the Pre-selection stage, **it is not possible to know with certainty which courses will be available at the partner schools**. In most cases, the updated course offer for your mobility year does not yet exist, and part of the other schools' offerings may not be open to Exchange students, or may have limited seats, or may require academic prerequisites. | | **2) Check the courses that are forbidden to take abroad**
Please consider, that some courses cannot be validated if taken abroad:
- computer tests;
- compulsory courses from another specific degree course
- banned courses: see List of compulsory courses which cannot be recognized (see Guide to the University chapter 7.14). | | **3) It is not necessary to change your Study Plan in advance**
In order to give more flexibility in the courses choices, Bocconi students participating in International programs have the possibility to modify their study plan one extra time once back from the exchange through Punto Blu available in the yoU@B student Diary. (see Guide to the University chapter 9.3.4). | Before the departure for the Exchange Program 2025-26

| | | --- | | **1) Check the Academic Offer at the Partner School**
The official list of courses available at the partner university will be given after the application to the partner school, according to their schedule. It is your responsibility to look at the [course content summaries](https://didattica.unibocconi.it/ts/index.php?anno=2024) in the [online course profiles](http://didattica.unibocconi.it/ts/home_insegnamenti.php?cambial=E) and find possible matches with the courses taught at the partner schools through the above-mentioned web tools.
Consider that both courses with and without correspondence need to be validated by the Program Director, but they involve different courses within your study plan:
- **Conversion with correspondence**

These conversions can be inserted in your Bocconi career as **mandatory, elective**, or **additional** exams (in this last scenario, please always verify on the Guides to the University cap 9. the Study Plan Office rules for additional educational activities exam insertion).

**Language courses:** curricular languages (i.e. those included in the study plan as first and second foreign language) can be recognized through the exchange program, upon approval.

Languages taken as additional subjects (“in sovrannumero”) can only be registered if passed internally at Bocconi or converted through an international certification included in the official list published by the Bocconi Language Center.

Credits referring to foreign languages are included in the maximum number of 5 exams allowed for course conversion.

- **Conversion without correspondence**

These conversions can be inserted in your Bocconi career either as **elective** or **additional** exams (in this last scenario, please always verify on the Guides to the University cap 9. the Study Plan Office rules for additional educational activities). | | **2) Check the credits at the partner School**
The Bocconi credits system is based on the ECTS, the European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System.
**For courses with Bocconi correspondence, after the conversion, you will have in your Study Plan the number of credits assigned to the correspondent Bocconi course; for courses without Bocconi correspondence, the number of credits assigned to a Bocconi elective course (6 cfu).**
Please note that, in many partner schools, especially outside Europe, there might be a different credit system.
Therefore, check within the recognition platform for credit equivalency before making conversion requests. |

| **3) Start inserting your requests**
If the exam you want to take abroad is not available on the list on the Recognition Platform you can enter a new request.
In some cases, it is possible that you will find many courses already approved (green flag). If you select these courses you do not need to get any preliminary approval: once back, after you send the recognition request, these courses will be automatically registered into your study plan. (see Sez. Upon return)
**It is not possible to have validated 1 course taken abroad with 2 Bocconi courses**; on the other hand, 2 or more courses taken abroad can be either recognized into 1 Bocconi course, or can be recognized into 1 elective without correspondence.
In case of two or more foreign exams covering one Bocconi exam, the final grade is obtained by the grade point average of the corresponding Bocconi grades obtained, using the foreign credit points specified on the Transcript issued by the host university as the weighted coefficient. 
Remember that in order to obtain a preliminary approval, for each new request student must send to the Director an overall description of the course you wish to attend abroad. The Bocconi Director has to receive the pdf version of the analytic syllabus of the course (e.g. course description and level - objectives, contents, lectures and/or special workloads, written/oral exam, the number of credits assigned to the course and/or the number of classroom and lecture hours, the local instructor's contacts).
If you need more information on this procedure please check our video tutorial through [B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/it/sections/4407350015762-Riconoscimento-esami-Exchange-Program). |

| | | --- | | **1) Check the Academic Offer at the Partner School**
The official list of courses available at the partner university will be provided after your application to the partner school, according to their schedule. It is your responsibility to review the course content summaries in the online course profiles and identify possible matches with the courses offered at the partner universities through the above-mentioned web tools.
Starting from the 2026/27 academic year, **all** recognition requests - **whether with or without course correspondence** \- will be evaluated by the Director of your Degree Program and/or the Assistant to the Program Director.
When planning your requests, please consider that it is always at the discretion of the Program Director to evaluate whether the courses taken abroad is eligible for a 1:1 conversion in terms of credits/content
In terms of credits, as a **general guideline**
- courses taken abroad should cover **at least 2/3 of the credits** of the corresponding Bocconi course for 1:1 recognition. (i.e a course worth 8 credits at Bocconi requires at least 5.5 equivalent credits abroad).
- A maximum of three (3) courses below the 2/3 threshold may **still be approved** for 1:1 recognition when deemed appropriate by the Director. Alternatively, students may always request the combination of two foreign courses to reach the equivalence with one Bocconi course.
- Students can submit up to a maximum of **12 new requests** for authorization for recognition. This does not include already existing authorizations that the student can select.
The above criteria are intended as general guidelines to support the evaluation process. **The final decision on course recognition and credit conversion is always at the sole discretion of the Program Director.**
- **Conversion with correspondence**

These courses have a Bocconi equivalent course and can be included in your Bocconi career as mandatory, elective, or additional courses.

**Language courses:** curricular languages (i.e. those included in the study plan as first and second foreign language) can be recognized through the exchange program, upon approval.

Languages taken as additional subjects (“in sovrannumero”) can only be registered if passed internally at Bocconi or converted through an international certification included in the official list published by the Bocconi Language Center.

Credits referring to foreign languages are included in the maximum number of 5 exams allowed for course conversion.
- **Conversion without correspondence**

These conversions do not have a corresponding course at Bocconi and can be inserted in your Bocconi career either as elective or **additional** exams (in this last scenario, please always verify on the Guides to the University the Study Plan Office rules for additional educational activities).
**Please note:** compulsory courses that do not have an exact correspondence in terms of content **may still be approved by the Program Director, provided they belong to the same Settore Scientifico Disciplinare (SSD**) or – when the sector is particularly broad – to the relevant macro-discipline. |

| **2) Check the credits at the partner School**
The Bocconi credits system is based on the ECTS, the European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System.
**For courses with Bocconi correspondence, after the conversion, you will have in your Study Plan the number of credits assigned to the correspondent Bocconi course; for courses without Bocconi correspondence, the number of credits assigned to a Bocconi elective course (6 cfu).**
Please note that, in many partner schools, especially outside Europe, there might be a different credit system. Therefore check within the recognition platform for credit equivalency before making conversion requests. | | **3) Start inserting your requests**
If the exam you want to take abroad is not available on the list on the Recognition Platform you can enter a new request.
In some cases, it is possible that you will find many courses already approved (green flag). If you select these courses you do not need to get any preliminary approval: once back, after you send the recognition request, these courses will be automatically registered into your study plan.(see sez. Upon return)
**It is not possible to have validated 1 course taken abroad with 2 Bocconi courses;** on the other hand, 2 or more courses taken abroad can be either recognized into 1 Bocconi course, or can be recognized into 1 elective without correspondence. 
In case of two or more foreign exams covering one Bocconi exam, the final grade is obtained by the grade point average of the corresponding Bocconi grades obtained, using the foreign credit points specified on the Transcript issued by the host university as the weighted coefficient. 
Remember that in order to obtain a preliminary approval, for each new request student must send to the Director an overall description of the course you wish to attend abroad. The Bocconi Director has to receive the pdf version of the analytic syllabus of the course (e.g. course description and level - objectives, contents, lectures and/or special workloads, written/oral exam, the number of credits assigned to the course and/or the number of classroom and lecture hours, the local instructor's contacts).
If you need more information on this procedure, please check our video tutorial through [B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/it/sections/4407350015762-Riconoscimento-esami-Exchange-Program). |

Academic Recognition & Learning Agreement - Erasmus Students Please if you need more information on the OLA visit our website at this [link](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/erasmus-scholarship-area) [.](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/myconnect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/international+relations/bocconi+students/erasmus) Upon Return | | | --- | | **1) Wait for the Transcript of Records**
Only at the end of the Exchange, students can finalize the recognition of the exams taken abroad by entering a request through the Recognition platform available in the yoU@B Diary.
The requests can be submitted by students **only if the final and official Transcript of Records** with grades of the exams taken abroad has been received (electronic version is allowed).
The time to receive the transcript can vary from school to school and in some cases, it **can take months** from the end of the term abroad: students that receive the Transcript at their home address shall upload a copy of it through the Recognition platform, available by the yoU@B student Diary.
Please remember that **there is no deadline** for the recognition procedure, but students need to pay attention to the Study Plan completion date depending on the graduation session they want to join. | | **2) Submit the request to convert the exams taken abroad**
Students are allowed to send **only one** request for recognition, therefore it is important that this request includes all of the courses that the student may wish to convert from their experience abroad. | | **3) Pay attention to the graduation timeline**
Students will be informed via their yoU@B student Diary when the request has been processed.
For students taking part in the Exchange Program during the **Spring Term**: consider that the timing of semesters and Transcript release might not be aligned with Bocconi timeline.
Therefore, it may not be possible to graduate in July and/or to be compliant for the **2° Round of MSc applications** at Bocconi due to the time needed to transfer exams taken abroad. | Conversion Tables Bocconi has worked out differentiated conversion tables (Tabelle di Conversione) for each Partner School in order to ensure fair grade conversion between different academic contexts. Conversion tables imply a one-to-one correspondence between every Partners grades and a Bocconi one. They are based on information given by Partner Schools about their grading distribution (percentages, curves of distribution, description of grades and if any Country specifics) and compared to Bocconi grading system. Being based on such one-to-one association, **some Bocconi grades cannot find any correspondence on some Partners' grading scales**; therefore, **you must be aware that not all the 13 Bocconi grades or the laude (from 18 to 30/30) can be assigned.** After selection, it will be possible to see, through Punto Blu, the conversion table of the school you have been selected for. To access the conversion table from Punto Blu, you can select the "International Mobility" option from the right menu, then choose "Mobility Calls," position yourself, and click on the magnifying glass icon to the right of the "Exchange Program call" while scrolling through the various tabs of the "Call Details." [More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/law-academic-recognition#addtoany "Show all")

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

Exchange Program 2026-27

**ACADEMIC RECOGNITION  OF COURSES TAKEN ABROAD**

If you are interested in taking part in the exchange program, please note that you will need to follow specific rules and a procedure to have the examinations taken abroad converted into the Università Bocconi.

First of all, you have to know that **only at the end** of the Exchange Program it is possible to convert the exams taken abroad. Students **must convert a minimum of 1 exam** in order to have the Exchange Program validated and to obtain an added point to the Master of Arts degree final grade.

Law Exchange students will be able to **recognize a maximum of 5 Bocconi courses** (with and/or without correspondence) additional educational activities included, without considering the credits of each course.

**PLEASE NOTE:** proctored exams in Bocconi are not allowed.

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

Law students are offered a "tailor-made" network made of bilateral agreements between Bocconi and our partner institutions.

Students will be able to attend a single term at one of the foreign universities **during the study cycle**, after passing a selection, and just during the academic year the selection is related to.

The participation in the Exchange Program allows students to add one point to their final (degree) grade, by recognising at least one exam passed at the foreign university.

**\*\*For more details, please refer to the Guide to the Exchange Program below**\*\*

Procedures for BSc in Global Law

Procedures for Law Students

- [Academic and Language Requirements](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/undergraduate-programs-english-requirements)

- [Results](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/results-and-waiting-lists)
- [Start of the Mobility](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/start-mobility)
- [Academic Recognition](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/law-academic-recognition)

Swipe

- [Guida al programma scambi 2026-27](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Guida%20al%20Programma%20Scambi%2026_27_2.pdf?VersionId=xZ0cBVQgvr8oPGHjff63R6Hb8jiNgUOF)
- [Guide to the Exchange Program 2026-27](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Guide%20to%20the%20Exchange%20Program-2026-27.pdf?VersionId=m6ALVg3U1nKXSWVv.D9hlczxd3SlzTve)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/law-exchange-program#addtoany "Show all")

To be eligible for the Exchange Program all students need to meet the following academic requirements:
- **GPA**: minimum 22/30 of average weighted on credits, the average is not rounded.
- **3rd year students credits**: at least 100% of 1st year and 60% of 2nd year credits registered;
- **4th year students credits**: at least 100% of 1st year, 60% of 2nd year credits and 60% of 3rd year credits registered.
Please note that:
- The calculation of GPA and credits is based on Bocconi exams taken **by the end of the winter exams session**: 3rd February 2026 for the AY 2026/27 selection. **Make sure all your grades are registered** in your Study Plan before the Exchange Program application closes, as only those exams will be considered for the rankings;

- If you would like to convert the English certificate to have the exam passed in your Bocconi career and counting for the Exchange program score, it is essential to do it in advance, and in any cases before February 3rd 2026. For further instructions and timeline, please visit the Bocconi [Language Center's website](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/language-certification-recognition);

- **3rd year students**: exams which are not part of the 1st, 2nd year study plan and 3rd year - 1st semester - study plan are not included;
- **4th year students**: exams which are not part of the 1st, 2nd, 3rd year study plan and 4th year - 1st semester - study plan are not included;
- **Courses subdivided in modules** will be considered as separated courses for the minimum academic requirements and exchange score calculation;
- Some destinations might require a specific minimum GPA. When necessary, the details can be found on the destination brochure.
Language Requirements

**Minimum** language requirements are sufficient to apply for all the destinations that do not state further requirements other than the language of selection inside the destination brochure.

In addition to the minimum requirements for the Exchange program, some partner universities have **additional** language requirements. You can check them in our destination brochure.

Please read carefully **both** sections below:

To be eligible for the Exchange Program it is always required to have a minimum level of English, French, German, Spanish or Portuguese according to the institutions you are applying for.

**Level B2** of **English** proved by **one** of the options listed below:
- International Certification: IELTS or TOEFL
- University Language Exam - Starting from Exchange Program AY 2026-27 no minimum grade is required.
- University Legal Language Exam - Starting from Exchange Program AY 2026-27 no minimum grade is required.
**Level B1** or higher for **French, German and Spanish,** proved by **one** of the options listed below:
- Citizenship
- International Certification
- University Language Exam
- Enrollment in the Bocconi Second Language Course - option available **only for 3rd year students**
- Secondary School Diploma or Bachelor's Degree - Please contact the Study Abroad office providing any document which can be useful to evaluate your case
- Previous Exchange Program completed - Please contact the Study Abroad office providing any document which can be useful to evaluate your case
**Level A2** or higher for **Portuguese,** proved by **one** of the options listed before or:
- By the enrollment to the Bocconi Language Center course;
- International Certification.
**How to fulfill the Minimum Language Requirements:**

| | | | --- | --- | | Citizenship | Citizens from a country where French, German, Portuguese or Spanish is the first language, can certify their **minimum** language proficiency by submitting their passport at the time of application to the program at Punto Blu. | | University Language Exam and Legal Language Exam | Applicants with the exam already registered in their Bocconi career **do not have** to submit any international certification at the time of application to the program unless the university they are applying to has additional requirements.
Students who passed the exam at a University other than Bocconi must provide proof of **level** and **grade** during the application procedure. | | International Certifications for English | - The Study Abroad Office accepts only **IELTS or TOEFL certificates** attesting to a level of B2 or higher for English. Such certificates must be submitted at the time of application. For detailed information on the required scores, please refer the [Language Center website](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/certifications-recognized-bachelor-science-programs) or our [article on B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/articles/21923942778898-How-can-I-meet-the-minimum-English-language-requirements-in-order-to-apply-for-the-Exchange-program);
- TOEFL/IELTS: must be awarded no more than **2 years** prior the Exchange Program application deadline
- IELTS Indicator and TOEFL Essentials are **not** accepted;
- IELTS General Training is accepted for the minimum language requirement only. | | International Certifications for Other Languages | - Certificates must be recognized by Bocconi Language Center with an equal or higher level of B1 and must submitted at the time of application. For the minimum scores accepted, please refer to the Bocconi [Language Center website;](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/certifications-recognized-integrated-master-arts-law)
- For French, the DELF B1 certificate is accepted;
- For Spanish, the SIELE GLOBAL certificate at B1 level in all its components is accepted, in on-site or remote mode;
- Certificates must be awarded no more than **3 years** prior the Exchange Program application deadline. | | Enrollment in Bocconi Second Language Course - 3rd year students only | - When the language of selection is French, German or Spanish, undergraduate applicants can **temporarily** meet the requirement if enrolled in the Bocconi course of the required language;
- Sub iudice students must have the final exam passed and **registered** by the day of the last scheduled exam **within the June/July session;**
- Selected students who do not pass the language exam by the deadline, will no longer be eligible to leave for the exchange and will be **automatically** **excluded** from the program. |

| Portuguese Language | Students who do not meet the Portuguese language requirement [must attend and **complete**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/supplementary-activities/supplementary-activities) the [Portuguese Language course](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/supplementary-activities/supplementary-activities-0) provided by Bocconi Language Center.
Alternatively, students can present a CIPLE or CELPE-Bras A2 language certificate directly to our office. The certificate must be sent to our office [through B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/sections/4407343663890-Before-departure-and-during-the-Exchange-Program) **by July and, in any case, before the departure date.**
Please note that the release of official certificate results may take approximately two months; students are therefore strongly advised to **carefully plan the timing of the exam and the submission of the certificate**. |

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

Exchange Program 2026-27

Bocconi law students have the chance to join the Exchange Program during the 4th **or** 5th year of their Integrated Master of Arts in Law.

The **application** opens once a year, during the **3rd** and **4th year.** Students can be selected for the Exchange Program **only once** for the entire study cycle.

Academic Requirements

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


Exchange Program 2026-27

Selection is based on merit evaluation, taking into consideration the **student's academic weighted average** and the **number of** **credits** registered up to February 3rd, 2026:

**Student WA + \[0.1 x (Student NC - MIN NC required)\]**

0.1 points will be attributed to every applicant from any Degree Program for each credit above the minimum number of credits required:

- For **3rd year** students: 100% of 1st year and 60% of 2nd year credits registered;

- For **4th year** students: at least 100% of 1st year , 60% of 2nd year credits and 60% of 3rd year credits registered.

These points will be added to the weighted credit average, calculated according to the above criteria.

The calculation of credits thus includes all the exams officially taken by **February 3rd, 2026** (included), end of the ordinary exam session.

Students can indicate up to 10 exchange destinations in order of preference. The Study Abroad office will elaborate the ranking, according to the above-mentioned criteria. Each student will be assigned to the first destination for which there are still places available among the ones s/he listed in her/his application in order of preference.

**Please note:**

- Courses subdivided in moduleswill be considered as **separated courses** for the minimum academic requirements and exchange score calculation.

- If applicants have equal final scores for a specific destination and there is one last slot available, the students' preferences linked to the destination will be used as criteria in order to set the ranking. In case of equal preferences, priority will be given to the student who applied earlier (considering hh:mm:ss).

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/law-selection-criteria#addtoany "Show all")

### Timeline: Preparing your Exchange

#### October and November: join our Drop-in sessions and presentations!

From the end of October to the end of November, our office will be available during specific time slots to support students with any questions about the Exchange Program.

You will receive a notification in your agenda with the exact dates and times.

#### GPA & Credits

Check that your GPA and number of credits meet the minimum academic requirement and allow you to apply for the Exchange Program.

#### Language requirements

Make sure you meet the language requirements and verify if you need to submit a language certificate.

#### Explore the destinations

Explore the destinations to check if you meet the requirements for the ones you are most interested in.

The destinations currently listed refer to the previous selection. The updated list for the next academic year will be available in mid February, you can take the current one as a point of reference.

#### Scholarships

Check for the [scholarships](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/erasmus-scholarship "Erasmus+ Scholarship") within and outside Europe.

#### Passport

Make sure your passport is valid according to your destination. It must remain valid for at least six months after the end of the Exchange Program, so check its validity in advance.

[Read more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program-prova-timeline-e-how#accordion-boxed-135249)

Get informed on how to apply for the Exchange Program by exploring the sections below.

Here you will find a helpful link to guide you through the application, the timeline and what happens after you submit your application.

- _**Tutorial for the application**_

Click [here](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/articles/9088838296850-How-and-when-can-I-apply-for-the-Exchange-Program) to learn how to complete your application through our tutorial on B in Touch.

text:
### Timeline: Your Exchange Application

#### 25 February 2026 - The application opens

Check the destinations you are interested in, upload the certificates if necessary and apply for the Exchange Program!

#### 03 March 2026 - The application closes at 3 pm (Italian time)

Make sure to submit your documents and complete your application before the deadline!

#### Results

Results will be announced between the end of March and the beginning of April.

You will be notified via your Bocconi email.

#### Accept

Once selected, you will have a few days to accept or decline your Exchange offer.

Remember to respect the deadline, otherwise you will be withdrawn from the Exchange.

#### Erasmus+

Erasmus+ scholarship is automatically assigned to students selected for destinations within the Erasmus+ Program.

You will be contacted by the relevant office in due time with details about the required procedures.

[Erasmus+ before the mobility](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/erasmus-scholarship-area/erasmus-scholarship-mobility "Erasmus+ Scholarship: Before Mobility")

[Read more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program-prova-timeline-e-how#accordion-boxed-135290)

Get ready to go abroad by exploring the sections below.

You’ll find useful links and resources to help you after your selection, including the tutorial for the recognition platform. It also guides you through the timeline and all the steps to complete before your departure.

- _**Tutorial for the Recognition platform**_

To have the approvals before your departure, please check our [tutorial](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/articles/18366949331858-Where-can-I-find-the-tutorial-for-the-Exchange-Program-recognition-platform).
- _**Download the documents available for the Partner University application**_

During the application process to the partner university, you may be asked to provide some documents that you can download independently.

Please refer to our [guidelines](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/articles/10942099153682-Where-can-I-find-the-certificates-needed-for-the-application-at-the-partner-school) to see how to download the available documents.

text:
### Discovering the Next Steps After the Selection

#### Wait to be contacted by the partner school

After being selected, please wait to be contacted directly by your host university.

Students nominated for the first semester are usually contacted between April and May, while those nominated for the second semester are generally contacted between August and September.

Timelines may vary depending on the partner university.

#### Pre-departure recognition

Before your exchange starts, please ensure that all the courses you intend to take abroad are approved on the Recognition Platform.

You will receive detailed instructions by email as soon as you have been granted access to the Recognition Platform.

#### Erasmus+

If you have been selected for an Erasmus+ destination, you will receive specific guidelines by our office regarding:

\- The Mobility Agreement

\- The Online Learning Agreement

For the release of the scholarship please feel free to contact Fees, Funding and Housing office.

Meet all the deadlines to receive the scholarship!

#### VISA

If you are going abroad, you may need to apply for a student visa.

Check the host university website and the country’s consulate or embassy for updated requirements.

The application process can take time, so start early!

#### Exchange Starter Pack

Before your departure, you will receive your Exchange Starter Pack with practical information about the next steps and our pre-departure checklist.

#### International SOS

Before your departure, remember to register and download the International SOS app. You will receive all the necessary information and activation details in advance.

Please note: International SOS is not a health insurance. You must have valid medical coverage for the entire duration of your stay abroad.

[Read more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program-prova-timeline-e-how#accordion-boxed-135293)

Get ready to return to Bocconi by exploring the sections below.

Here you will find an useful link on how to proceed with the conversion of the exams taken abroad.

The second section outlines the steps you need to complete, or wait for, before starting the conversion process.

Complete the final steps after your Exchange to complete your Exchange journey!

- _**Start the conversion process for the exams taken abroad**_

After your Exchange period ends, you will receive an email from Bocconi confirming that you are authorized to proceed with the recognition and grade conversion.

See [our article](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/articles/15928425015954-How-can-I-convert-exams-when-I-return-from-the-Exchange) for the step-by-step procedure.

metadata.title: Exchange Program - prova timeline e how to | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Timeline and steps of the Exchange Program > Discovering the Next Steps After the Selection

text:
### What to do after your Exchange

#### Transcript of Records

After your Exchange, you will need to wait for your official grades to be released by the host university.

Once you receive it, you can proceed with the recognition process through the Recognition Platform.

Please note that in some cases, the release of the Transcript may take several months. If you have specific needs, contact your host university in advance to check expected timelines.

#### Erasmus+

Please remember that your Online Learning Agreement must be approved and consistent with your Transcript of Records.

You will also need to complete the EU Survey, for which you will receive a direct link by email.

For the release of the scholarship please feel free to contact Fees, Funding and Housing office.

Meet all the deadlines to receive the scholarship!

[Erasmus+ After the mobility](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/erasmus-scholarship-area/erasmus-scholarship-after-mobility "Erasmus+ Scholarship: After Mobility")

#### Recognition

Once you are back at Bocconi and you have the Transcript of Records, you can ask for the conversion of the exams taken abroad.

You will be notified when your grades and credits have been officially transferred into your Bocconi career.

#### Relations and evaluations of the Exchange Program

After your return, our office will contact you to share your feedback on your Exchange experience.

Your comments are extremely valuable: they help us improve the program and provide useful insights for future students choosing their destinations.

Don’t forget to complete the survey!

[Read more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program-prova-timeline-e-how#accordion-boxed-135296)

text:
## How we support you

Our role in the Free Mover process includes:

- verify whether the chosen destination has already been attended by Bocconi students
- assess and recognize courses once you return, according to the academic recognition process
- forward transcripts bought through yoU@B to the final destination school via e-mail

The Study Abroad Office is not involved in nominations or in managing individual applications.

metadata.title: Exchange Program - prova timeline e how to | Bocconi University

text:
## What is managed directly by students

The Free Mover semester allows students to independently organize an international experience.

As this is an independently arranged experience, students are responsible for:

- identifying and applying to the host institution
- completing the admission process and meeting deadlines
- complying with the host university’s academic and administrative procedures

How we support you

Our role in the Free Mover process includes:

verify whether the chosen destination has already been attended by Bocconi students

evaluate ad hoc destinations

assess and recognize courses once back, according to the academic recognition process, provided you have secured a place at Punto Blu

forward transcripts bought through yoU@B to the final destination school via e-mail

The Study Abroad Office is not involved in nominations or in managing individual applications.

What is managed directly by the students

The Free Mover semester allows students to independently organize an international experience.

As this is an independently arranged experience, students are responsible for:

\- identifying and applying to the host institution

\- completing the admission process and meeting deadlines

complying with the host university’s academic and administrative procedures

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program-prova-timeline-e-how#addtoany "Show all")

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


Exchange Program 2026-27

**To prepare yourself for the semester abroad, there are some important things you will need to check:**

Before the application

**Safety**

we remind you to read the **Bocconi Students Travel Risk Policy**(on yoU@B – International SOS) and access **International SOS** services, which include:

- Before leaving: information about the country of destination;
- while abroad: advice on health and security issues;
- in case of emergency: assistance in case of medical or security emergency.

Before the application, we encourage you to seek information about the countries you want to apply to and for which you could potentially be selected, by browsing through International SOS portal including **Country Guides** and **Travel, Security and Medical Risk Ratings**.

**International SOS is not a medical insurance** and does not replace your personal medical insurance.

**Passport/Visa**

Please check the immigration policies of the countries you are applying to, and ensure you have a **valid passport**. If a passport and/or visa is required, you will be asked to provide a copy of your valid passport immediately after the selection process to issue the necessary documents.

If your passport expires during or within six months after the end of your study abroad program, please renew it as soon as possible.

After the selection

**Host School Application**

Most of the host schools have very strict deadlines for applications (for enrolment, courses selection, accommodation, etc).

Please check your e-mail daily as the Study Abroad Office (or the Partner School directly) will forward you instructions and forms/documents to be filled in and returned.

**Insurance**

Please check the insurance policies of the host country. In case needed, you will have to provide an insurance coverage for your exchange period abroad. Some schools require to purchase their own insurance plan, in some cases upon arrival.

**Scholarships**

Students selected for those European Universities which take part in the **Erasmus+** Programme will automatically benefit from getting the scholarship. If you are a grantee, you will be required to submit some documents to our Office. For further information, please check: [Erasmus+ Scholarship website](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/international+relations/bocconi+students/erasmus) and [B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/categories/4407572389778-Funding).

Other international mobilities may have scholarships assigned on a need basis and upon availability of funds.​ For further information, please check [Fees and Funding office website](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/funding).

**Enroll in your next academic year**

Just like students not participating in the Exchange Program you will need to enroll in the next academic year and choose the elective courses (check the timeline on the Student Administration Center website). However, Exchange Students (who may insert tentative elective choices) have the chance to change their electives choice once back from the exchange period, after the Academic Recognition procedures.​

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/start-mobility#addtoany "Show all")

text:
Before the selection | | | --- | | **1) Check the list of courses previously available**
When considering the various alternatives in terms of schools and destinations, look at the information on the courses offered by the host university (indicated in the [Exchange destinations](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/international+relations/exchange+program_bracco+2010+07+28+03+31)), visit the list of courses taken abroad in the Course history on yoU@B, and read the experience of the former exchange [students' reports](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/international+relations/restricted+area). 
Where no course information is available, these are new schools, schools reintroduced to the network after a few years, or destinations where there have been no students from your degree program recently.
Please consider that at the Pre-selection stage, **it is not possible to know with certainty which courses will be available at the partner schools**. In most cases, the updated course offer for your mobility year does not yet exist, and part of the other schools' offerings may not be open to Exchange students, or may have limited seats, or may require academic prerequisites. | | **2) Check the courses that are forbidden to take abroad**
Please consider, that some courses cannot be validated if taken abroad:
- computer tests;
- compulsory courses from another specific degree course
- banned courses: see List of compulsory courses which cannot be recognized (see Guide to the University chapter 7.14).



**Course's language requirement:**
- For **CLEAM** Exchange Program or Free-Mover Semester students in countries where sitting exams in English is not possible, the Bocconi Study Plan requirement is zero elective taught in English instead of 1. (see Guide to the University chapter 9.3.1) | | **3) It is not necessary to change your Study Plan in advance**
In order to give more flexibility in the courses choices, Bocconi students participating in International programs have the possibility to modify their study plan one extra time once back from the exchange through Punto Blu available in the yoU@B student Diary. (see Guide to the University chapter 9.3.4). | Before the departure for the Exchange Program 2025-26

| | | --- | | **1) Check the Academic Offer at the Partner School**
The official list of courses available at the partner university will be given after the application to the partner school, according to their schedule. It is your responsibility to look at the courses content summaries  in the [online course profiles](http://didattica.unibocconi.it/ts/home_insegnamenti.php?cambial=E) and find possible matches with the courses taught at the partner schools through the above-mentioned web tools.
Consider that both courses with and without correspondence (need to be validated), but they involve different administrative procedures for you to follow:
- **Conversion with correspondence**

These courses have a Bocconi equivalent course and must be approved on the Recognition Platform by the professor responsible for the corresponding course. For BAI, BGL, and BEMACS students, approval is given by the Degree Program Director or delegate. These conversions can be inserted in your Bocconi career as mandatory, elective, or additional exams (in this last scenario, please always verify on the Guides to the University the Study Plan Office rules for additional educational activities exam insertion).

**Language courses:** curricular languages (i.e. those included in the study plan as first and second foreign language) can be recognized through the exchange program, upon approval.

Languages taken as additional subjects (“in sovrannumero”) can only be registered if passed internally at Bocconi or converted through an international certification included in the official list published by the Bocconi Language Center.

Credits referring to foreign languages are included in the maximum number of 5 exams allowed for course conversion.

- **Conversion without correspondence**

These conversions do not have a corresponding course at Bocconi and they will be approved, through the Recognition Platform, by the Program Director who has an overall view of your Study Plan. These conversions can be inserted in your Bocconi career either as elective or **additional** exams (in this last scenario, please always verify on the Guides to the University the Study Plan Office rules for additional educational activities). | | **2) Check the credits at the partner School**
The Bocconi credits system is based on the ECTS, the European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System.
**For courses with Bocconi correspondence, after the conversion, you will have in your Study Plan the number of credits assigned to the correspondent Bocconi course; for courses without Bocconi correspondence, the number of credits assigned to a Bocconi elective course (6 cfu).**
Please note that, in many partner schools, especially outside Europe, there might be a different credit system. Therefore check within the recognition platform for credit equivalency before making conversion requests. |

| **3) Start inserting your requests**
If the exam you want to take abroad is not available on the list on the Recognition Platform you can enter a new request.
In some cases, it is possible that you will find many courses already approved (green flag). If you select these courses you do not need to get any preliminary approval: once back, after you send the recognition request, these courses will be automatically registered into your study plan.(see sez. Upon return)
**It is not possible to have validated 1 course taken abroad with 2 Bocconi courses;** on the other hand, 2 or more courses taken abroad can be either recognized into 1 Bocconi course, or can be recognized into 1 elective without correspondence. 
In case of two or more foreign exams covering one Bocconi exam, the final grade is obtained by the grade point average of the corresponding Bocconi grades obtained, using the foreign credit points specified on the Transcript issued by the host university as the weighted coefficient. 
Remember that in order to obtain a preliminary approval, for each new request student must send to the professor/Director an overall description of the course you wish to attend abroad. The Bocconi professor/Director has to receive the pdf version of the analytic syllabus of the course (e.g. course description and level - objectives, contents, lectures and/or special workloads, written/oral exam, the number of credits assigned to the course and/or the number of classroom and lecture hours, the local instructor's contacts).
If you need more information on this procedure, please check our video tutorial through [B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/it/sections/4407350015762-Riconoscimento-esami-Exchange-Program). |

| | | --- | | **1) Check the Academic Offer at the Partner School**
The official list of courses available at the partner university will be provided after your application to the partner school, according to their schedule. It is your responsibility to review the course content summaries in the [online course profiles](https://didattica.unibocconi.eu/ts/index.php?anno=2026) and identify possible matches with the courses offered at the partner universities through the above-mentioned web tools.
Starting from the 2026/27 academic year, **ALL** recognition requests - **whether with or without course correspondence** \- will be evaluated by the Director of your Degree Program and/or the Assistant to the Program Director.
When planning your requests, please consider that it is always at the discretion of the Program Director to evaluate whether the courses taken abroad is eligible for a 1:1 conversion in terms of credits/content
In terms of credits, as a **general guideline**
- courses taken abroad should cover **at least 2/3 of the credits** of the corresponding Bocconi course for 1:1 recognition. (i.e a course worth 8 credits at Bocconi requires at least 5.5 equivalent credits abroad).
- A maximum of three (3) courses below the 2/3 threshold may **still be approved** for 1:1 recognition when deemed appropriate by the Director. Alternatively, students may always request the combination of two foreign courses to reach the equivalence with one Bocconi course.
- Students can submit up to a maximum of **12 new requests** for authorization for recognition. This does not include already existing authorizations that the student can select.
The above criteria are intended as general guidelines to support the evaluation process. **The final decision on course recognition and credit conversion is always at the sole discretion of the Program Director.**
- **Conversion with correspondence**

These courses have a Bocconi equivalent course and must be approved on the Recognition Platform by the Degree Program Director. These conversions can be included in your Bocconi career as mandatory, elective, or additional courses.

**Language courses:** curricular languages (i.e. those included in the study plan as first and second foreign language) can be recognized through the exchange program, upon approval.

Languages taken as additional subjects (“in sovrannumero”) can only be registered if passed internally at Bocconi or converted through an international certification included in the official list published by the Bocconi Language Center.

Credits referring to foreign languages are included in the maximum number of 5 exams allowed for course conversion.
- **Conversion without correspondence**

These conversions do not have a corresponding course at Bocconi and they will be approved, through the Recognition Platform, by the Program Director who has an overall view of your Study Plan. These conversions can be inserted in your Bocconi career either as elective or **additional** exams (in this last scenario, please always verify on the Guides to the University the Study Plan Office rules for additional educational activities).
**Please note:** compulsory courses that do not have an exact correspondence in terms of content **may still be approved by the Program Director, provided they belong to the same Settore Scientifico Disciplinare (SSD**) or – when the sector is particularly broad – to the relevant macro-discipline. |

| **2) Check the credits at the partner School**
The Bocconi credits system is based on the ECTS, the European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System.
**For courses with Bocconi correspondence, after the conversion, you will have in your Study Plan the number of credits assigned to the correspondent Bocconi course; for courses without Bocconi correspondence, the number of credits assigned to a Bocconi elective course (6 cfu).**
Please note that, in many partner schools, especially outside Europe, there might be a different credit system. Therefore check within the recognition platform for credit equivalency before making conversion requests. | | **3) Start inserting your requests**
If the exam you want to take abroad is not available on the list on the Recognition Platform you can enter a new request.
In some cases, it is possible that you will find many courses already approved (green flag). If you select these courses you do not need to get any preliminary approval: once back, after you send the recognition request, these courses will be automatically registered into your study plan.(see sez. Upon return)
**It is not possible to have validated 1 course taken abroad with 2 Bocconi courses;** on the other hand, 2 or more courses taken abroad can be either recognized into 1 Bocconi course, or can be recognized into 1 elective without correspondence. 
In case of two or more foreign exams covering one Bocconi exam, the final grade is obtained by the grade point average of the corresponding Bocconi grades obtained, using the foreign credit points specified on the Transcript issued by the host university as the weighted coefficient. 
Remember that in order to obtain a preliminary approval, for each new request student must send to the Director an overall description of the course you wish to attend abroad. The Bocconi Director has to receive the pdf version of the analytic syllabus of the course (e.g. course description and level - objectives, contents, lectures and/or special workloads, written/oral exam, the number of credits assigned to the course and/or the number of classroom and lecture hours, the local instructor's contacts).
If you need more information on this procedure, please check our video tutorial through [B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/it/sections/4407350015762-Riconoscimento-esami-Exchange-Program). |

Academic Recognition & Online Learning Agreement - Erasmus Students Please if you need more information on the OLA visit our website at this [link](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/reserved-area/international-mobility-reserved-area/erasmus-scholarship-area). Upon return | | | --- | | **1) Wait for the Transcript of Records**
Only at the end of the Exchange, students can finalize the recognition of the exams taken abroad by entering a request through the Recognition platform available in the yoU@B Diary.
The requests can be submitted by students **only if the final and official Transcript of Records** with grades of the exams taken abroad has been received (electronic version is allowed).
The time to receive the transcript can vary from school to school and in some cases, it **can take months** from the end of the term abroad: students that receive the Transcript at their home address shall upload a copy of it through the Recognition platform, available by the yoU@B student Diary.
Please remember that **there is no deadline** for the recognition procedure, but students need to pay attention to the Study Plan completion date depending on the graduation session they want to join. | | **2) Submit the request to convert the exams taken abroad**
Students are allowed to send **only one** request for recognition, therefore it is important that this request includes all of the courses that the student may wish to convert from their experience abroad. | | **3) Pay attention to the graduation timeline**
Students will be informed via their yoU@B student Diary when the request has been processed.
For students taking part in the Exchange Program during the **Spring Term**: consider that the timing of semesters and Transcript release might not be aligned with Bocconi timeline.
Therefore, it may not be possible to graduate in July and/or to be compliant for the **2° Round of MSc applications** at Bocconi due to the time needed to transfer exams taken abroad. | Conversion Tables Bocconi has worked out differentiated conversion tables (Tabelle di Conversione) for each Partner School in order to ensure fair grade conversion between different academic contexts. Conversion tables imply a one-to-one correspondence between every Partners grades and a Bocconi one. They are based on information given by Partner Schools about their grading distribution (percentages, curves of distribution, description of grades and if any Country specifics) and compared to Bocconi grading system. Being based on such one-to-one association, **some Bocconi grades cannot find any correspondence on some Partners' grading scales**; therefore, **you must be aware that not all the 13 Bocconi grades or the laude (from 18 to 30/30) can be assigned.** After selection, it will be possible to see, through Punto Blu, the conversion table of the school you have been selected for. To access the conversion table from Punto Blu, you can select the "International Mobility" option from the right menu, then choose "Mobility Calls," position yourself, and click on the magnifying glass icon to the right of the "Exchange Program call" while scrolling through the various tabs of the "Call Details." [More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/undergraduate-academic-recognition#addtoany "Show all")

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

Exchange Program 2026-27

**ACADEMIC RECOGNITION OF COURSES TAKEN ABROAD**

Undergraduate exchange students, including students in the BSc in Global Law, will be able to **recognize a maximum of 5 courses** in Bocconi (with and/or without correspondence) additional educational activities included, without considering the credits of each course.

It will be possible to convert the exams taken abroad **only at the end** of the Exchange Program. Students **must convert a minimum of 1 exam** in order to have the Exchange Program validated in the Bocconi career and to obtain an added point to the Bachelor degree final grade.

If you are interested in taking part in the Exchange Program, please note that you will need to follow specific rules and a procedure to have the examinations taken abroad converted into the Università Bocconi.

**PLEASE NOTE:** proctored exams in Bocconi are not allowed.

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

The Exchange Program is open to undergraduate students regularly **enrolled in their 2nd year** of a Bocconi Bachelor Degree. Students will be able to attend a single term at one of the foreign universities **during their 3rd year**, after passing a selection, and just during the academic year the selection is related to.

The participation in the Exchange Program allows students to add one point to their final (degree) grade, by recognising at least one exam passed at the foreign university.

**\\*\\* For more details, please refer to the Guide to the Exchange Program below\*\***

Procedures for BSc in International Government Students

Procedures for Undergraduate programs in Italian

To be eligible for the Exchange Program all students need to meet the following academic requirements:
- **GPA**: minimum 22/30 average weighted on credits, the average is not rounded.

- **Credits**: at least 60% of 1st year credits registered
Please note that:
- The calculation of GPA and credits is based on Bocconi exams taken **by the end of the winter exams session**: 3rd February 2026 for the AY 2026/27 selection. **Make sure all your grades are registered** in your Study Plan before the Exchange Program application closes, as only those exams will be considered for the rankings;

- If you would like to convert the English certificate to have the exam passed in your Bocconi career and counting for the Exchange program score, it is essential to do it in advance, and in any cases before February 3rd 2026. For further instructions and timeline, please visit the Bocconi [Language Center's website](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/language-certification-recognition);
- Exams which are not part of the 1st year and 2nd year - 1st semester - study plan are not included, as they must be taken during the ordinary exam session;

- Courses subdivided in moduleswill be considered as separated courses for the minimum academic requirements and exchange score calculation;

- Some destinations might require a specific minimum GPA. When necessary, the details can be found on the Brochure;

- If the applicant moves from a bachelor's degree course to another one, exams which are not part of the 1st year and 2nd year - 1st semester - of the NEW study plan are not included.
Language Requirements

**Minimum** language requirements are sufficient to apply for all the destinations that do not state further requirements other than the language of selection inside the destination brochure.

In addition to the minimum requirements for the Exchange program, some partner universities have **additional** language requirements. You can check them in our destination brochure.

Please read carefully **both** sections below:

To be eligible for the Exchange Program it is always required to have a minimum level of English, French, German, Spanish or Portuguese according to the institutions you are applying for.

**Level B2** or higher for **English**, proved by **one** of the options listed below **:**
- International Certification: IELTS or TOEFL
- University Language Exam
**Level B1** or higher for **French, German and Spanish,** proved by **one** of the options listed below:
- Citizenship
- International Certification
- University Language Exam
- Enrollment in the Bocconi Second Language Course
- Secondary School Diploma – Please contact the Study Abroad office providing any document which can be useful to evaluate your case
**Level A2** or higher for **Portuguese,** proved by **one** of the options listed before or by the enrollment to the Bocconi Language Center course.

**How to fulfill the Minimum Language Requirements:**

text:
| | | | --- | --- | | Citizenship | Citizens from a country where **French**, **Germany**, **Portuguese** or **Spanish** is the first language, can certify their **minimum** language proficiency by submitting their passport at the time of application to the program at Punto Blu. | | Bocconi Language Exam | Applicants with the Bocconi exam already registered in their study plan do not need to submit any certificate at the time of application unless the university they are applying to has additional requirements. | | International Certifications for English | - The Study Abroad Office accepts only **IELTS or TOEFL certificates** attesting to a level of B2 or higher for English. Such certificates must be submitted at the time of application. For detailed information on the required scores, please refer the [Language Center website](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/certifications-recognized-bachelor-science-programs) or our [article on B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/articles/21923942778898-How-can-I-meet-the-minimum-English-language-requirements-in-order-to-apply-for-the-Exchange-program);
- TOEFL/IELTS: must be awarded no more than **2 years** prior the Exchange Program application deadline;
- IELTS Indicator and TOEFL Essentials arenot accepted;
- IELTS General Training is accepted for the minimum language requirement only. | | International Certifications for Other Languages | - Certificates must be recognized by Bocconi Language Center with an equal or higher level of B1 and must submitted at the time of application. For the certifications and scores accepted, please visit the [Language Center website](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/certifications-recognized-bachelor-science-programs);
- For French, the DELF B1 certificate is accepted;
- For Spanish, SIELE GLOBAL is accepted in on-site or remote mode;
- Certificates must be awarded no more than **3 years** prior the Exchange Program application deadline. | | Enrollment in Bocconi Second Language Course | - When the language of selection is French, German or Spanish, undergraduate applicants can **temporarily** meet the requirement if enrolled in the Bocconi course of the required language;
- **Sub iudice students** must have the final exam passed and **registered** by the day of the last scheduled exam **within the July session;**
- Selected students who do not pass the language exam by the deadline, will no longer be eligible to leave for the exchange and will be **automatically** **excluded** from the program. | | Portuguese Language | Students who do not meet the Portuguese language requirement [must attend and **complete**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/supplementary-activities/supplementary-activities) the [Portuguese Language course](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/supplementary-activities/supplementary-activities-0) provided by Bocconi Language Center.
Alternatively, students can present a CIPLE A2 or CELPE-Bras language certificate directly to our office. The certificate must be sent to our office [through B in Touch](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/sections/4407343663890-Before-departure-and-during-the-Exchange-Program) **by July and, in any case, before the departure date.**
Please note that the release of official certificate results may take approximately two months; students are therefore strongly advised to **carefully plan the timing of the exam and the submission of the certificate**. |

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


Exchange Program 2026-27

For undergraduate students, including students in the BSc in Global Law, selection is based on merit evaluation, taking into consideration the **student's academic weighted average** and the **number of** **credits** pertaining to the 1st year study plan and 2nd year - 1st semester - study plan and registered up to **February 3rd, 2026**.

**Student WA + \[0.1 x (Student NC - MIN NC required)\]**

0,1 points (0,098 for CLEACC; 0,102 for BAI, BESS and BEMACS) will be attributed to every applicant from any Degree Program for each credit above the minimum number of credits required (60% of 1st year credits registered). These points will be added to the weighted credit average, calculated according to the above criteria.

Students can indicate up to 10 exchange destinations in order of preference. The Study Abroad office will elaborate the ranking, according to the above-mentioned criteria. Each student will be assigned to the first destination for which there are still places available among the ones s/he listed in her/his application in order of preference.

**Please note:**

- Courses subdivided in moduleswill be considered as **separated courses** for the academic requirements and exchange score calculation.

- If applicants have equal final scores for a specific destination and there is one last slot available, the students' preferences linked to the destination will be used as criteria in order to set the ranking. In case of equal preferences, priority will be given to the student who applied earlier (considering hh:mm:ss).

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/undergraduate-selection-criteria#addtoany "Show all")

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)



The Exchange Program offers students a unique international experience, allowing them to spend a term abroad at one of our 293 partner universities in 56 countries across five continents.

Through our bilateral agreements, students attending the program will carry out a study term, without paying tuition fees at the host school. However, students are responsible for covering their own travel expenses, accommodation, meals, and any other associated costs. Scholarships, such as those offered by ISU and Erasmus, are available. Courses completed at the partner institution can be recognized, subject to specific rules and procedures that students must adhere to.

Students can access the Exchange Program only once during a study program, through an application, upon possession of specific requirements. The Program entails a selection, with subsequent publication of the ranking, which takes place annually.

For more details, please refer to the sections below.

For A.Y. 2026-27

### Timing and deadlines

- Deadline for registering examinations (corresponding to the end of the ordinary winter exam period)\*: **February 3rd, 2026.**

- Publication of Brochures: **mid February 2026.**

- Application opens: **February 25th, 2026.**

- Deadline for application at Punto Blu and uploading of Language certificates\*\*: **March 3rd, 2026 at 3 pm (Italian time).**

- Results and confirmation: **end of March/beginning of April 2026.**

- For students selected for a destination on [sub iudice basis](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/articles/10798686905874-I-was-selected-sub-judice-for-a-destination-what-does-that-mean), deadline for passing the study plan second language oral exam: **by the June/July 2026 exam session, for all the programs. By the departure date, for BIG students.**

- For IM Global Experience and FIN Global Experience Track students, deadline for the submission of the Curriculum Vitae and the Video Interview: **March 3rd, 2026 at 3 pm (Italian time)**.

For the method for collecting the material please check [IM Global Experience and FIN Global Experience Track Selection Criteria.](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/im-global-experience-selection-criteria "https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/im-global-experience-selection-criteria")

\*To ensure that the **first language exam** is recorded in your study plan and considered in the **Exchange Program score calculation** through the **conversion of an international certificate**, it is essential to follow the timeline set by the Language Center. For additional details, please refer to the [dedicated page](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/language-certification-recognition) on the Bocconi Language Center's website.

\*\*If you need a certificate in order to comply with the language requirements, we strongly recommend that you take it as soon as possible. Please allow **enough time to receive the certificate**, always considering the above mentioned deadlines.

[**Undergraduate Exchange Program**\\
\\
including the Exchange Program integrated in the BSc in International Politics and Government](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/undergraduate-exchange-program " ")

[**Graduate Exchange Program**\\

including the Exchange Program integrated in:\\
\\
- the MSc in International Management Global Experience\\
- the MSc in Finance Global Experience Track](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/exchange-program/graduate-exchange-program " ")
