International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


Integrated Master of Arts in Law (AY 2025-26)

**EXAMS RECOGNITION**

Students applying to the Free Mover Summer can recognize **only one** course, which will be registered the Bocconi academic career with a grade out of thirty. In case there is no indication of a grade, it will not be possible to recognize the exam (i.e.: pass/fail grading system is not valid).

Before the Summer Program

The partner school course you wish to attend must have a corresponding Bocconi course and it has to be approved by the Degree Program Director The program must be approved **before** the Summer Program begins. It is the student's responsibility to verify that the recognized course is coherent with his/her Bocconi study plan.

Once the Recognition platform is available, you must send the recognition request to the Degree Program Director in order to obtain his/her preliminary approval.

In order to do so you need to **upload** to the platform an overall description of the Summer course you wish to attend. The Degree Program Director has to receive all the major information on the course (e.g. course description - objectives, contents, lectures and/or special workloads, written/oral exam, the number of credits assigned to the course and/or the number of classroom and lecture hours, the local instructor's contacts).

After the Summer Program

Upload the partner school’s official transcript of records (mentioning course, grade and credits) to the Recognition platform and proceed with the recognition.

Exams that CANNOT be recognized are:

- foreign language exams and computer tests;
- courses that are not coherent with the student's study plan;
- list of courses for which credits cannot be granted if taken abroad (please refer to the Guide to the University).

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/academic-recognition-0#addtoany "Show all")

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


Undergraduate Students (AY 2025-26)

**EXAMS RECOGNITION**

Students applying to the Free Mover Summer can recognize **only one course**, which will be registered the Bocconi academic career with a grade out of thirty. In case there is no indication of a grade, it will not be possible to recognize the exam (i.e.: pass/fail grading system is not valid).

Before the Summer Program

The partner school course you wish to attend must have a corresponding Bocconi course and it has to be approved by the Degree Program Director or delegate. The program must be approved **before** the Summer Program begins. It is the student's responsibility to verify that the recognized course is coherent with the Bocconi study plan.

Once the Recognition platform is available, you must send the recognition request to the Degree Program Director in order to obtain his/her preliminary approval.

In order to do so you need to **upload** to the platform an overall description of the Summer course you wish to attend. The Degree Program Director has to receive all the major information on the course (e.g. course description - objectives, contents, lectures and/or special workloads, written/oral exam, the number of credits assigned to the course and/or the number of classroom and lecture hours, the local instructor's contacts).

After The Summer Program

Upload the partner school’s official transcript of records (mentioning course, grade and credits) to the Recognition platform and proceed with the recognition.

Exams that **CANNOT** be recognized are:

- foreign language exams and computer tests;
- courses that are not coherent with the student's study plan;
- list of courses for which credits cannot be granted if taken abroad (please refer to the Guide to the University).

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/academic-recognition#addtoany "Show all")

Only qualified students can apply for the Free-Mover experience and they are required to prove the following before departure:
- **Course year**: the period abroad must be the 1st or 2nd semester of the 2ndyear;
- **Credits**: at least **16 credits** of their 1st year registered before semesters departures (the Exchange Program rules on credits apply).
- **Language Requirements**: Language of instruction of the Host University - the Exchange Program language applies\* - at Level B1 or higher for all languages EXCEPT English language which must be at a minimum Level B2 (obtained with a minimum grade of 24/30) or higher.
- The language of selection indicated by Università Bocconi in the Exchange Program destinations is to be considered **compulsory** also for the Free-Movers.

metadata.title: Free Mover Semester Graduate | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Free Mover Semester Graduate | Bocconi University

text:
In addition, each hosting School has its own requirements which have to be fulfilled by the applicant.

Destinations

The lists provided on this page are designed to assist current Bocconi students in finding a suitable partner university for the Free-Mover Semester since most of the partner schools do not accept free-movers if they have already an Exchange agreement with Bocconi.

The universities listed in the pdf document "possible destinations" are those **who have accepted** Bocconi Students in the past. This does not guarantee these schools will accept students in the future.

Schools which inform Bocconi that they **do not** accept Free-Movers students are listed in a different pdf document.

The following information may help students decide which universities offer the most suitable courses for their study plan.

**Faculty:** This indicates the faculties opened in the past at each partner university.  For example, Law only means only Bocconi law students may apply.

**Level:** Indicates whether each partner accepted undergraduate or graduate or law students from Bocconi or both.

**Language:** The language required - but not the additional requirements if any - is the same as that indicated by Università Bocconi for the Exchange Program and it is compulsory **also** for the Free-Movers.

In addition, we strongly advise students to check if there is a link for each country to the relevant Government visa information website. These contain current advice about visa procedures for each country and should be used as the primary source of information about visas.

The list of foreign Universities is to be considered as **the only valid list** in terms of possibility of recognition of the tuition received by Free-Mover Semester students at foreign institutions.

Any other destination must be endorsed ex-ante, on a case-by-case basis, by the Study Abroad Office. The request, accompanied by a **letter of motivation** and information material on the University selected, has to be presented before enrolling in the foreign university as a Free-Mover Semester student and sent to the Study Abroad Office through B in Touch.

Unlike the students selected for the traditional Exchange Program, the Free-Movers Semester will have to directly contact the host University to request programs, deadlines, fees and all the information they need in order to finalize the enrollment procedures. The students will have to fund all their travel, board and accommodation expenses during their period abroad, **as well as the university fees both in Italy and abroad**. The student is advised to take out health insurance coverage for the period spent abroad.

How to apply

Please refer to this [**link**](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/international+relations/bocconi+students/graduate+school/study+abroad/what/free+mover+semester/how+to+collect+info_edauthor+2008+06+24+05+09), in order to get all the necessary information in terms of: selection process, application procedure & timeline.

Recognition

Study Abroad will provide eligible students with a Recognition Platform in their yoU@B Mobility sections once the process is finaiized.

At the end of the study period students must upload the official transcript of records in the Study Abroad Office Recognition Platform soon after the experience abroad.

Without this document the Study Abroad Office will not proceed with the conversion procedure.

Only upon admission is the table of conversion of grades available at Punto Blu _and_ it is also available in the Exchange Program dynamic brochure for reference.

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


Students interested in spending a semester abroad without participating in the Exchange Program can enroll as **Free-Movers** ( **Fee-paying Visiting Students** or **Independent Students** or **Contract Students**) at some foreign Universities where they can study for a semester on a fee-paying basis.

The difference between the Free-Mover and the Exchange is the admission process which is freely defined by the host institutions and out of any Bocconi responsibility or involvement. On top, Bocconi has its own process since a maximum of 10 students in each destination will be authorized to recognize their courses taken abroad during their Free-Mover Semester. This number considers the entire a.y. (considering both first and second term).

Bocconi click day will be each year in May. Please be aware that admission processes abroad may have **different timelines**. Students are strongly advised **not to undertake any binding financial commitment** before the release of the updated information and/or having secured their place at Punto Blu. Please refer to the Guides available in the following sections for each degree level.

Please note that the Free-Mover Semester initiative is **not** open to the following Degree courses: BSc in International Politics and Government, MSc in International Management Global Experience and MSc in Finance Global Experience Track as they have already a guaranteed Exchange experience.

For A.Y. 2026-27

### Timing and deadlines

- Deadline for obtaining the minimum credits and uploading the language certificates (if required to satisfy the minimum language requirement): **at least** **5 working days before departure**

- Publication of possible destinations and guides: **18 March 2026 (last update 4 May 2026)**

- Click day, opening days
  - Integrated Master of Arts in Law: **5 May 2026 at 12 pm (Italian time)**

  - Bachelor in Global Law: **6 May 2026 at 12 pm (Italian time)**

  - Undergraduate programs: **7 May 2026 at 12 pm (Italian time)**

  - Graduate program: **8 May 2026 at 12 pm (Italian time)**
- System closure for departures in 2026-27: **1 February 2027**

- Confirmation: Students must inform the Study Abroad Office via B in Touch once they have uploaded the required documents on Punto Blu.

**Please note that:**

- Students who withdraw from the Exchange Program cannot apply for the Free-Mover Semester.
- Students who are in waiting list for the Exchange Program can apply for the Free-Mover Semester but, if they apply for this initiative (by reserving a place at Punto Blu), their name will be removed from the waiting list by the Study Abroad staff and will not be contacted any further.

**Check the appropriate Guide for a successful experience abroad and in order to get all the necessary information in terms of requirements, destinations, procedures, timeline, and academic recognition.**

[**Undergraduate Guide**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/FREE-MOVER%20SEMESTER%20UNDERGRADUATE%20GUIDE%20_2026-27.pdf?VersionId=vb..7QjrlNfj9HnbCDsmpYa_4ElL8rzg "Undergraduate Guide")

[**Bachelor in Global Law Guide**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/FREE-MOVER%20SEMESTER%20BGL%20GUIDE%20_2026-27.pdf?VersionId=Rba5Kg74yTP7SNe551FEiq8zt_WlWe4M "Bachelor in Global Law Guide")

[**The Free Mover Semester Cycle of Life**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/FREE%20MOVER%20_CycleOfLife_2026.pdf "The Free Mover Semester Cycle of Life")

[**Graduate Guide**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/FREE-MOVER%20SEMESTER%20GRADUATE%20GUIDE_2026-27.pdf?VersionId=5HaP0X3REiREnr1v1Dm3ssNMkq0iEqlW "Graduate Guide")

[**Law Guide**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/FREE-MOVER%20SEMESTER%20LAW%20GUIDE_2026-27.pdf?VersionId=2oHHtzEaXAjgdG5Ozpvbqu5bT0D4Xn28 "Law Guide")

text:
## How we support you

Our role in the Free Mover process includes:

\- Verify whether the chosen destination has already been attended by Bocconi students

\- Evaluate ad hoc destinations

\- Assess and recognize courses once back, according to the academic recognition process, provided you have secured a place at Punto Blu

\- Forward transcripts bought through yoU@B to the final destination school via e-mail

The Study Abroad Office is not involved in nominations or in managing individual applications.

## For more information

[![B in Touch](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/images/b_in_touch_logo_0.png)\\
\\
**Read the FAQs and send Requests**](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/categories/4407569547154-Free-Mover "Read the FAQs and send Requests")

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/free-mover-semester#addtoany "Show all")

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

Integrated Master of Arts in Law (AY 2025-26)

Free Mover Summer

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/free-mover-summer)

_**Latest update - December 22, 2025**_

**ELIGIBILITY & DESTINATIONS**

The Free Mover Summer is open to Bocconi students from the five-year **Integrated Master of Arts in Law** who meet the **requirements** and who can attend a Summer Program once, in the summer of their third or fourth year. Participation in a program in the summer of the first year, second year or before will not be recognized, as well as the participation in a program in the summer of the fifth year.

The application to the Free Mover Summer will be on-hold and the recognition platform will not be available until the Bocconi requirements are met. If an applicant does not meet the requirements before the beginning of the Summer Program, the application to the Free Mover Summer will be declined.

We allow our students to participate in Free Mover Summer Program, if they have the Bocconi academic and language requirements, but we do not select nor officially nominate them. It is ultimately up to the host schools to accept or not Bocconi students.

Academic Requirements

**3rd year students**

**Enrollment year:** Students regularly enrolled in the 3rd year of the five-year Integrated Master of Arts in Law.

**Credits:** At least 100% of 1st year, 60% of 2nd year credits registered. The calculation includes all the exams officially registered in the academic career by February 3rd, 2026 (end of the ordinary session).

In addition, applicants **must meet** the academic requirements of the partner school (if any).

**4th year students**

**Enrollment year**: Students regularly enrolled in the 4th of the five-year Integrated Master of Arts in Law.

**Credits**: At least 100% of 1st year, 60% of 2nd year credits and 60% of 3rd year credits registered. The calculation includes all the exams officially registered in the academic career by February 3rd, 2026 (end of the ordinary session).

In addition, applicants **must meet** the academic requirements of the partner school (if any).

Language Requirements

**3rd and 4th year students**

**Level B2 or higher** for English, proved by one of the options listed below: - Bocconi English exam of level B2; - University Legal Language Exam; - English International language certificates of Level B2 or higher In addition, applicants **must meet the language requirements of the partner school (if any)** | | | | --- | --- | | Bocconi Language Exam | applicants with the Bocconi exam already registered in their study plan do not need to submit any certificate at the time of application. | | International Certifications for English | - IELTS or TOEFL with an equal or higher level of B2 must be submitted at the time of application. For the scores accepted, please visit [B in Touch here.](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/articles/25925486709394-Which-English-language-certifications-meet-the-minimum-language-requirements-for-applying-to-the-Free-Mover-Summer)
- IELTS Indicator and TOEFL Essentials arenot accepted;
- TOEFL/IELTS: must be awarded no more than **2 years** prior the beginning of the Summer Program; | Destinations Università Bocconi has selected a number of Partner schools from its international network that offer interesting and educationally valid Summer Programs. The following is the only list considered valid for the recognition of courses offered in the Summer Program through institutions abroad. Students can submit applications for other alternatives that must be **examined** individually in advance by the Study Abroad office, **before** applying to the Free Mover Summer The request, accompanied by a letter of motivation and information material on the University/Program, has to be submitted through **B in Touch,** available on yoU@B Diary, to the Study Abroad Office. - ESADE (ESADE Summer School) - King’s College London (Summer Programmes) - London School of Economics (LSE Summer School) - SciencesPo (SciencesPo Summer School) - University of California. Berkeley (Summer Sessions) - Université de Genève (Université de Genève Summer Schools)

### Steps to follow

- [How to apply](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/free-mover-summer-how-apply)
- [Academic Recognition](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/academic-recognition-0)

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)

Undergraduate and Bachelor in Global Law students (AY 2025-26)

Free Mover Summer

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/free-mover-summer)

#### Eligibility and Destinations

The Free Mover Summer is open to Bocconi students from all **Bachelor of Science Programs** and for the **Bachelor in Global Law** who meet the **requirements**, in the summer of their second year. Participation in a program in the summer of the first year or before will not be recognized, as well as the participation in a program in the summer of the third year.

The application to the Free Mover Summer will be on-hold and the recognition platform will not be available until the Bocconi requirements are met. **If an applicant does not meet the requirements** before the beginning of the Summer Program, the application to the Free Mover Summer will be **declined**.

Academic Requirements

**Enrollment year**: students regularly enrolled in the 2nd year of a BSc program or in the Bachelor of Global Law.

**Credits**: at least 60% of 1st year credits must be registered. The calculation includes all the exams officially registered in the academic career by February 3rd, 2026 (end of the ordinary session). If the applicant has changed the BSc program, exams which are not part of the 1st year of the NEW BSc study plan are not included in the calculation of the credits.

In addition, applicants **must meet the academic requirements** of the partner school (if any).

Language Requirements

**Level B2** or higher for English, proved by one of the options listed below: - Enrolment in a BSc program taught in English; - Bocconi English exam of level B2; - English International language certificates of Level B2 or higher. In addition, applicants **must meet the language requirements** of the partner school (if any). | | | | --- | --- | | Enrollment in a degree program in English | applicants enrolled in a degree program taught in English do not need to submit any certificate at the time of application. | | Bocconi Language Exam | applicants with the Bocconi exam already registered in their study plan do not need to submit any certificate at the time of application. | | International Certifications for English | - IELTS or TOEFL with an equal or higher level of B2 must be submitted at the time of application. For the scores accepted, please visit [B in Touch here.](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/articles/25925486709394-Which-English-language-certifications-meet-the-minimum-language-requirements-for-applying-to-the-Free-Mover-Summer)
- IELTS Indicator and TOEFL Essentials arenot accepted;
- TOEFL/IELTS: must be awarded no more than **2 years** prior the the Free Mover Sumemr application deadline; | Destinations Bocconi University has selected a number of Partner schools from its international network which offer interesting and educationally valid Summer Programs. The following is the only list considered **valid** for the recognition of courses offered in the Summer Program through institutions abroad. Students can submit applications for other alternatives that must be **examined** individually in advance by the Study Abroad office, **before** applying to the Free Mover Summer. The request, accompanied by a letter of motivation and information material on the University/Program, has to be submitted through **B in Touch,** available on the yoU@B Diary to the Study Abroad Office. - Copenhagen Business School (International Summer University Programme) - ESADE (ESADE Summer School) - HEC Paris (HEC Paris Summer School) - King's College London (Summer Programmes) - University of Leeds (Leeds International Summer School) - London School of Economics (LSE Summer School) - Maastricht University (Summer School) - SciencesPo (SciencesPo Summer School) - University of California - Berkeley (Berkeley Summer Sessions) - University of California - Los Angeles (UCLA Summer Sessions) - Stanford University (Summer Sessions)\* - University College London (UCL Summer School) - The University of Warwick (Warwick Summer School) - Hanyang University (International Summer School) - Lady Margaret Hall, University of Oxford (Summer Program) - The University of Melbourne (Short-Term Program) \*The majority of courses offered by Stanford University during Summer Quarter are eight weeks, but Bocconi University will recognize one exam only.

### Steps to follow

- [How to apply](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/how-apply)
- [Academic Recognition](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/academic-recognition)

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


Integrated Master of Arts in Law (AY 2025-26)

_**Latest update - April 16, 2026**_

Students wishing to attend a Summer Program in **Summer 2026** will have to formally apply at Punto Blu **starting from April 27, 2026 and no later than July 17, 2026**.

**The relevant dates will be updated on this page once available.**

Entry requirements and travel restriction to enter the Host Country must be verified by students themselves prior to departure, in order to guarantee that the Program is carried out in compliance with applicable health requirements and visa law.

Students are responsible for the Host School tuition fees, travel, board and lodging expenses during the Summer Program. Participants are also required to be covered by a comprehensive international sickness and accident insurance policy while abroad.

Before the Application

- **check** if you meet the Bocconi the requirements (see [Eligibility](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/free-mover-summer/free-mover-summer-0 "Free Mover Summer Law") section);
- **check** if you meet the partner school requirements;
- **ensure** you can meet the partner school application deadlines and procedures;
- **check** if the course you want to attend and recognize is coherent with your Bocconi study plan;

How to Apply

**Apply** to the Free Mover Summer at Punto Blu, uploading:

1\. a valid English certificate - if not already registered in your career (see [Eligibility](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/free-mover-summer/free-mover-summer-0 "Free Mover Summer Law") section);

2\. the ‘Free Mover Summer 2026 - Student info' document (available below).

It is necessary to **save** the application receipt.

- [Free Mover Summer 2026 - Student info](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/Free_Mover%2BSummer%2B2026_Student_info_0.docx)

After the Application

The Study Abroad Office will **check** if you meet the Bocconi academic and language requirements and will **send** you a notification once the recognition platform is available in your yoU@B diary.

Through this tool you can **send** a recognition request for the course you wish to attend during the Summer Program to the Director of the Integrated Master of Arts in Law for the course with or without correspondence, in order to obtain his/her preliminary approval. Please be aware that - during the request procedure - you are required to attach a detailed course syllabus or to provide the website link related to the course.

After the Bocconi recognition **approval**, you can carry out the **partner** school enrolment procedures. While applying to the Free Mover Summer, students can indicate **one Summer Program only.**

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/free-mover-summer-how-apply#addtoany "Show all")

text:
International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


_**Latest update - April 16, 2026**_

The information here available reflects the **current situation to date**.

Students can attend Summer Programs in **Summer 2026.** Nevertheless, whether the overall conditions would not safeguard the wellbeing and security of students, Bocconi University does not guarantee that students will be able to attend Summer Programs blended/in person. Before applying, you are required to carefully read the Bocconi Students **TRAVEL RISK POLICY**, available on yoU@B – International SOS and **the Travel Risk Policy Exceptions for A.Y. 2025/26:** in-person Summer Programs 2026 **will not be authorized** in the countries where the student Exchange is suspended.

**GENERAL OVERVIEW**

Bocconi University has selected a number of partner schools from its international network which offer interesting and educationally valid Summer Programs.

The **Free Mover Summer** allows students to spend a short period abroad in the summer months (around 2-3 weeks) to attend a Summer Program and take a curricular course for which they can receive credit. The course can be registered in the academic career at Bocconi **.**

Students wishing to attend a Summer Program in **Summer 2026** will have to formally apply at Punto Blu **starting from April 27, 2026 and no later than July 17, 2026**. Check the section 'How to apply' for details.

Click on the links below to discover the **undergraduate** and **law**  opportunities.

Please note that the Free Mover Summer **is not open to MSc students.**

- [Undergraduate and Bachelor in Global Law opportunities](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/free-mover-summer/free-mover-summer)
- [Integrated Master of Arts in Law opportunities](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/free-mover-summer/free-mover-summer-0)

After the Application

Through this tool you can send a recognition request to the Degree Program Director.

After the Bocconi recognition **approval**, you can carry out the **partner** school enrolment procedures. International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


_The Free Mover initiative is a self-organized type of mobility that takes place at foreign universities where students can join summer programs or study there for an entire semester on a fee-paying basis._

[See more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/free-mover-semester)

[See more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover/free-mover-summer)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/free-mover#addtoany "Show all")

International Mobility

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility)


**ATTENTION PLEASE! YOU'RE ACCESSING THIS PAGE VIA AN OLD LINK AND THE CONTENTS MAY BE NO LONGER VALID  (LAST UPDATE: APRIL 2024).**

**STARTING FROM MAY 2024, PLEASE REFER TO THE NEW CONTENTS AVAILABLE AT THE FOLLOWING LINKS FOR** [**STUDENTS**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/career-services/your-internship-start-credit-recognition "Your internship: from start to credit recognition") **AND** [**COMPANIES**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/employers-and-partners/activating-and-managing-internships "Activating and managing internships") **.**

\\*\\*\\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

The pursuit of **an unpaid internship abroad** (involving no monetary grant) may entitle the student to a scholarship provided by the University. The chart below will help you identify the scholarship best suited for your internship.

**Remember that each scholarship may entail additional requirements: check your eligibility by following the links below.**

If your internship (for credits or without credits) takes place in an **Erasmus country**

[**Erasmus+ scholarship**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/services/erasmus-scholarships-ay-2023-2024) **(a.y. 2023/2024)**

If your internship is hosted by an **International Organization**

[**Grant for internships at International Organization and Chambers of Commerce**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/services/grant-internships-international-organizations-and-chambers-commerce-ay-2023-2024) **(a.y. 2023/2024)**

If you have been selected for **Banco MAECI — MIUR — FCRUI**

[**Grant for internships at the offices of the MAECI (Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation)**](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/corsi-di-studio/area-studenti-iscritti/servizi/contributo-stage-presso-le-sedi-estere-del-maeci-ministero-degli-affari-esteri-e) _— **only in Italian**_

Moreover, check if you are eligible for funding from

[**Contributions for International Mobility Programs**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/services/isu-bocconi-scholarship-integrations-0)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/scholarships-internships-abroad#addtoany "Show all")

About Us

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us)

International Networks

Overview


Bocconi University is committed to providing students with an educational experience that **goes beyond national borders**, preparing them to address challenges and explore opportunities on a global scale.

Through collaboration with academic institutions and networks worldwide, Bocconi offers students access to a truly international perspective. Our **exchange programs**, **Double Degrees** and **other study abroad opportunities** allow students to immerse themselves in diverse cultures, acquire intercultural skills and develop a deeper understanding of the geopolitical and economic dynamics shaping the global business landscape.

Since our first exchange agreements in 1974, our network has continuously expanded, providing students with increasing opportunities overseas. Our extensive global network includes **290** **partner schools** in **55 countries** across six continents.

Additionally, Bocconi proudly holds founding membership in prestigious networks such as the CEMS, PIM - International Management Network, the Themis Law Network and CIVICA, the European University of Social Sciences.

For International Students

Current students

Overview


As a member of the Bocconi community, you now have access to a wide range of resources designed to support international students. The University offers an abundance of opportunities — from guidance on immigration procedures and information on work permits, to a dynamic array of campus activities — and we encourage you to make the most of them throughout your studies here.

## Important to Know

### Permit of Stay Renewal

If you are a **NON-EU student**, you must **keep your immigration status updated**.

The Permit of Stay (Permesso di soggiorno) must be renewed at least 60 days before the permit expires. For detailed information on how to renew your residence permit, click the button below.

Renew your residence permit at the following link: [https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/renewal-permit-stay](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/renewal-permit-stay).

### Requesting Official Academic Transcript

Before renewing your Permit of Stay to remain a student in Italy, you must request an **Official Academic Transcript** (for immigration purposes) certifying that you have **successfully passed** at least one exam for the first renewal and at least two exams for each subsequent renewal of the permit, except for in circumstances beyond one’s control.

You may request the transcript using the cert@B procedure, available on the **yoU@B Diary** \> **Certificates**. The process usually takes 4 working days, therefore it is suggested to submit your request well before your permit renewal appointment.

### Working as a Student in Italy

Non-Italian citizens with a residence permit for study are allowed to work in order to support themselves financially.

In the case of **subordinate work**, the law (art. 14, par. 4, DPR  394/99) requires that the job shall not exceed 20 hours per week, which may also be cumulated over 52 weeks.

Part-time work can either be “vertical” or “horizontal", meaning you can work 20 hours per week for 12 months or up to 40 hours per week for 6 months, considering that the annual limit is 1,040 hours. This is because your primary activity in Italy must be studying. Please also keep in mind that any work contract cannot extend beyond the validity of your student residence permit.

### Getting an Internship in Italy

As clarified in the Italian National Labor Inspectorate note of 14 February 2023, **a residence permit for study allows students to take part in all curricular traineeships (internships)** included in their degree program or professional training for which the permit was issued.

Since traineeships — curricular or extracurricular — are not considered employment, the legal limits on work activities do not apply (see section 'Working as a Student in Italy').

Bocconi has built strong, long-standing relationships with leading Italian and international companies as well as institutions. Through these connections, the University actively supports graduates as they transition into the job market.

Discover more about Bocconi Career Services here: [https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/career-services](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/career-services).

### Job Seeking Permit

#### (Permesso Attesa Occupazione)

The Job Seeking Permit allows non-EU students — who possess an accredited undergraduate/graduate degree, PhD or post-graduate specialized degree — to remain in Italy for a period of maximum 12 months and search for a job.

You can **convert** your Permit of Stay for study to a Job Seeking Permit (Permesso Attesa Occupazione), as long as you begin the process **two months before your current permit of stay expires.**

For detailed information on how to do so, please go to the **Relocation and Immigration** section on **BinTouch** in your yoU@B Agenda.

## Bocconi Student Life

As an international student, your university experience goes far beyond the classroom. Student life at Bocconi is vibrant, multicultural and safe, with the city center of Milan minutes away. You can enhance your skills at the Language Center and IT Education Center, stay active at the state-of-the-art Bocconi Sports Center, show your school spirit with the latest apparel from the Bocconi Shop, and much more.

Campus Life

Campus Life is the ultimate hub for cultural, artistic, social and volunteer activities at Bocconi. In addition to supporting student associations, the University also provides counseling services and initiatives on self-empowerment and wellbeing — helping students make the most of their university experience.

Click [here](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life) for upcoming activities and opportunities on campus!

Language Center

The Language Center at Bocconi develops and coordinates foreign language courses, proposing both curricular and extra-curricular options to help students prepare for exams and earn certifications. It also provides various services to promote learning, such as tutoring and language exchanges.

Click [here](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/welcome-days/language-center-glance) to find out more about the Language Center!

IT Education Center

The IT Education Center (ITEC) oversees ICT education at Bocconi University, providing supplementary courses and certifications, with faculty drawn from top ICT professionals and tutors selected from Bocconi’s finest students.

Click [here](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/welcome-days/it-education-center-glance) to see what else the ITEC has to offer!

Sports

Sports are an integral part of Bocconi’s urban campus, supported by modern facilities featuring an Olympic-size swimming pool, fitness and wellness areas, an elevated running track, various playing courts, and much more.

The Bocconi Sports Team is the University’s athletics organization, offering year-round opportunities for both individual and team athletes to participate in intramural or national games and tournaments.

Click [here](https://www.bocconisport.eu/en) to learn more about the Bocconi Sports Center!

Egea

Egea is the publishing house of Bocconi University, founded in 1988 to advance academic culture in economics and business. Its editorial system also includes one of Italy’s largest independent bookstores, offering a wide range of academic, professional, non-fiction and fiction titles in both Italian and English.

Click [here](https://www.egeaonline.it/bocconi/) to find out more about Egea!

Bocconi Shop

University merchandise is available for purchase at the Bocconi Shop on campus, located on the ground floor of the Leonardo Del Vecchio Building at Piazza Sraffa 13.

Click [here](https://www.unibocconishop.it/en_GB?utm_source=com&utm_medium=sito&utm_campaign=wd21) to see the latest collection, and don’t forget to follow us on Instagram [@unibocconishop](https://www.instagram.com/unibocconishop/) to get the latest updates. Show Bocconi pride, wear your merch, and tag us to get featured!

_**IMPORTANT!**_
1. According to Italian legislation, **you are required to provide the University with your Permit of Stay and subsequent renewals**. All information on how to proceed are available on [yoU@B](https://youatb.unibocconi.it/) Student Diary.
2. If your Schengen Permit of Stay does not cover the entire period of mobility in Italy or if this period **is superior to 360 days**, you must instead apply for a Permit of Stay in Italy.
3. **Non-EU incoming exchange students** holding a **Schengen permit of stay for study purposes issued by a European Union country (Schengen Area)**, that allows for the exemption from the entry visa and covers the entire mobility period (less than 360 days), do not have to apply for the permit of stay while in Italy. They must instead fill in a [**declaration of presence**](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/6d2c2be5-1c6f-4c20-9f81-3b4c4f46f329/Declaration+of+Presence+for+Non-EU+from+Schengen+countries+%281%29.pdf?MOD=AJPERES&CVID=nDIhdkZ) and submit it within 8 working days from their arrival at any Police Station in Milan (the one closest to Bocconi University is located at Via Tabacchi 6). You must always carry a copy of the declaration of presence, as you may be asked to show it in case of police checks.

For all further information you can refer to the dedicated section of **BinTouch** on your YoU@B agenda (Relocation and Immigration>First Permit of Stay and Fiscal Code) or to the [YesMilano website](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/how-to/residence-permit-students).

Permit of Stay application step 1 - Post Office

The first step to apply for the Permit of Stay is the application within 8 working days after entering Italy at the Post Office.

Students will have to submit a copy of the following required documents at the post office ( **do NOT submit original documents**):

**• Completed Post Office Application/Kit:** make sure to complete [Modulo 1 (Form 1)](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/fca201bd-8452-429c-926a-d5b22a640c25/Obtaining+Your+First+Residence+Permit.pdf?MOD=AJPERES&CVID=ogoqOrx) included in the yellow-striped envelope application/kit for NON-EU citizens (available at the Post Office for free or at Bocconi during the Welcome Activities). To fill in the kit, you can refer to the tutorial you'll receive via e-mail during the Welcome Activities, or to the guide you can find on the [YesMilano website](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/how-to/residence-permit-students).

**IMPORTANT!**

**1)** Please write the following address on the application form: **Via Mercanti 8, 20121 (Milan)**. This address must also be included on the €70,46 payment slip. Please note: this address is valid for the permit of stay **first-release** application, not for the permit of stay renewal application.

**2)** Make sure to **indicate your Italian phone number** in the application documents to be notified and updated on next steps!

**• Photocopy of the official Bocconi acceptance letter**(same used to apply for your Visa, better if sealed by the Italian Consulate during the visa application process)

• **Photocopy of the passport** (personal data page + student Visa page only) or other equivalent document (you must show the original document when submitting the application at the Post Office)

• **Photocopy of insurance policy**: health insurance coverage is required in order to obtain a permit of stay in Italy. It must cover against the risk of illness and injury throughout the country, and for the entire period of validity of the Visa/permit of stay. If you have a private health insurance provider, you must have a certificate in English that specifies the start and end dates of this coverage which must be valid internationally.

• **Photocopy of Financial Statement**: document certifying adequate financial resources (approximately **€6.900** per year) for the validity period of the residence permit (e.g. provide the most recent bank statement or a bank letter of financial good standing)

• **Italian duty stamp**(Marca da Bollo) of **€16,00**. It can be purchased at any Tabacchi shop and it needs to be glued on Form 1 of the permit of stay application

• **Receipt of payment** of **€70,46** paid at the Post Office for the Permit of Stay. You will have to fill in the payment slip, included in the Post Office Kit, by indicating this amount in euros and by specifying the following address: **Via Mercanti 8, 20121 (Milan)**. **Please note:** students who write the Via Mercanti, 8, 20121 (Milan) address in the post office application form, will get an appointment with the Immigration office at the Milan’s Police Headquarters at Via Montebello 26.

• **Application Fee: €30,00** to be paid at the Post Office when submitting the application

After submitting these documents at the Post Office, the applicant will receive the 2 following documents:

1\. A "ricevuta" (mod.22A, certified letter receipt) with the user ID and password needed to monitor the status of the permit of stay application on the [Portale Immigrazione](https://www.portaleimmigrazione.it/ELI2ImmigrazioneWEB/Pagine/StartPage.aspx) ( **IMPORTANT**: when typing your password, do not include the dash (-)). **N.B.** Until your Residence Permit is issued, **your application receipt will act as proof of your legal immigration** registration and status, so always keep it with you!

2\. A letter\* with the date and time of the appointment at the Police Station to have photos and fingerprints taken. Students are **required** to go to the Immigration office on the date and time specified on the postal receipt.

\*If this letter is not given to the applicant at the Post Office it will be sent later on as certified mail, called "raccomandata", to the applicant's address in Italy. In case you don’t receive the letter by mail, please monitor your permit of stay application status on the [Portale Immigrazione](https://www.portaleimmigrazione.it/ELI2ImmigrazioneWEB/Pagine/StartPage.aspx). **IMPORTANT**: when typing your password, do not include the dash (-). You can access this website by using your user ID and password found on your post office receipt ("ricevuta").

Permit of Stay application step 2 - Immigration Office (Questura-Ufficio Immigrazione)

Once students have applied for the Permit of Stay at the Post Office, they'll receive an appointment for finger prints and photos to be taken at the Immigration Office ("Questura").

**IMPORTANT**: don’t miss this appointment, since the fast-track option is available only once. If you miss this appointment, you will need to schedule a new one through Bocconi, which will take longer. Moreover, in this case, you will be required to provide a valid justification with the reason why you missed the first appointment.

The following documents are required ( **bring originals and photocopies of ALL your documents**)

- Passport or other equivalent document
- Official Bocconi acceptance letter (same used to apply for your Visa, better if sealed by the Italian Consulate where the entry visa was issued)
- 2 identical passport-size photos
- Insurance policy: it must cover against the risk of illness and injury throughout the country, and for the entire period of validity of the Visa/permit of stay
- A certificate of enrollment in the Bocconi academic program for Immigration Purposes (in Italian). This certificate is mandatory ONLY for students who - while in Italy -do not live in the city of Milan (and therefore have to apply in the city where they live). Those students who declare they are living in the city of Milan when filling out the permit of stay application DO NOT NEED to bring this certificate, thanks to a specific agreement between Bocconi and the Police of Milan
- Financial Statement: document certifying adequate financial resources (approximately €6.900 per year) for the validity period of the residence permit (e.g. provide the most recent bank statement or a bank letter of financial good standing)
- Copy of the Accommodation Contract (MANDATORY when you show up at the Police Station).
o If you live in a private flat a copy of the rental contract, which must state your name, must be signed and registered with the Tax Authorities (it’s your landlord’s duty to register the contract).

o If you live in a university dorm: a declaration issued by the Housing Office stating that you live there

o If you stay in a hotel/accommodation facility (e.g. Airbnb): a declaration from the administrator confirming that your place is booked

o If you are hosted by a friend or a family member: declaration of hospitality ( [dichiarazione di ospitalità](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/713c36e9-4e2f-4579-b141-85742d058e9f/3.+MODULO+COMUNICAZIONE+DI+OSPITALITA.doc.pdf?MOD=AJPERES&CVID=nG6PplW)), a document stating that you are being hosted. Please refer to your landlord for all details.

The document should be submitted by the host in person to the local Police station ("Commissariato di zona" of the area where you live) or sent via:

• certified mail (“PEC”) to [immig.quest.mi@pecps.poliziadistato.it](mailto:immig.quest.mi@pecps.poliziadistato.it)

• registered mail (raccomandata A/R) to the local Police station ("Commissariato di zona" – Ufficio Immigrazione). It must be dated and signed by the person who provides the accommodation, together with a copy of the student’s and the host’s ID, and a copy of their residence permit (if a non-EU citizen)
- Post office receipt + receipt of payment of €70,46 paid at the Post Office for the permit of stay
- During the appointment, students will have to specify their home address in Milan

For further information, please visit:

[http://www.portaleimmigrazione.it/](http://www.portaleimmigrazione.it/)

[http://www.interno.it/](http://www.interno.it/)

[http://www.poste.it/](http://www.poste.it/)

[http://www.poliziadistato.it/](http://www.poliziadistato.it/)

Collecting your Permit of Stay

After successfully completing the two steps of the application for the Permit of Stay, students will receive the next appointment at the Immigration Office for the collection of their permit of stay. Only students with an Italian phone number will receive a confirmation via SMS from the Police. Once you're notified that your Residence Permit Card is ready for collection, visit the designated location (as instructed in the notification). Bring with you: a valid **passport** \+ **original residence permit application receipt.**

It is possible to track the status of the Permit of Stay application on the [Portale Immigrazione website](https://www.portaleimmigrazione.it/ELI2ImmigrazioneWEB/Pagine/StartPage.aspx) by accessing it with the user ID and password found on the Permit of Stay post office receipt ("ricevuta"). **IMPORTANT**: when typing your password, do not include the dash (-).

_Please make sure to regularly check the Portale Immigrazione for updates and confirmation of your appointment._

Post Offices closest to Bocconi
- Via Cordusio, 4 (Central Post Office of Milan) - **English, Spanish and Chinese speaking staff**. Opening Hours: Monday to Friday: 8:30 - 19:00 \| Saturday 8:30 - 12:30
- Viale Sabotino, 21/a - Opening Hours: Monday to Friday: 8:20 - 19:05 \| Saturday: 8:20 - 12:35
[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-when-you-arrive/permit-stay#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Freshly enrolled students - When you arrive

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-when-you-arrive)

**Within 8 working days** of their arrival in Italy, all **NON-EU** citizens who hold a valid student visa for Italy must apply for a **permit of stay for study purposes** (Permesso di soggiorno per motivi di studio).

The permit of stay is issued by the Police (Questura - Ufficio Immigrazione).

Applicants must submit their application through the Post Office (Poste Italiane).

N.B. Regular freshmen students who are applying for a First Permit of Stay will be able to **pick up a Permit of Stay Kit** at any post office or during Welcome Days starting from the 20th of August at the B in Touch Point (Piazza Sraffa 11) until stocks are exhausted.

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Freshly enrolled students - When you arrive

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-when-you-arrive)


Before the official start of classes, in late August and early September, Bocconi invites all first-year students to participate in our Welcome Days. A series of events and initiatives created to welcome you at Bocconi campus and introduce you to our course program, with special events for international students.

Your schedule is tailored to fit your specific group.

## Welcome Days

[**Regular and Double Degree Students - BSc Programs**\\
\\
Your Bocconi learning experience starts with welcome activities to meet faculty, make friends and have fun!](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/welcome-days/welcome-days-bsc-taught-english "BSc Programs")

[**Regular and Double Degree Students - MSc Programs**\\
\\
Your Bocconi learning experience starts with welcome activities to meet faculty, make friends and have fun!](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/welcome-days/welcome-days-msc-programs "MSc Programs")

[**Incoming Exchange Students**\\
\\
Every semester, the Bocconi International Student Desk organizes an intensive calendar of Welcome Activities, designed to meet the needs of incoming exchange students. Check out the program here.](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/welcome-activities "Check out the program here")

[**Single Courses Students**\\
\\
Bocconi welcomes Single Courses Students at the beginning of each semester with a welcome session on campus!](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/single-courses-visiting-students "Single Courses Students")

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-when-you-arrive/welcome-activities#addtoany "Show all")

## NON-EU Students! Finalize your immigration status by getting your Permit of Stay

**IMPORTANT!** The **Visa** is the document that a **non-EU citizens** get from the Italian Embassy or Consulate in their country of residence and is **required for entry into Italy and the Schengen area**.

**Once** **in Italy**, it is necessary to **apply for the Permit of Stay for Study Reasons within 8 working days** of entry to stay legally in Italy.

[How to Apply for Your Permit of Stay](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-when-you-arrive/permit-stay)

text:
## Bocconi facilities

B in Touch

B in Touch can answer all your questions about the University. Visit the B in Touch Point, a welcome area on the Bocconi campus. You can also access the B in Touch service online from the

[yoU@B](https://agenda.unibocconi.it/ "mailto:you@b") student diary and search for the information you’re looking for by reading the always up-to-date FAQs or contact the various offices on campus!

Wi-Fi Connection

Wi-fi coverage is available throughout the entire Bocconi campus. Students can access the "bocconi-studenti" network using their ID number and password. Please note that you must approve terms and conditions of use of the service in your yoU@B Student Diary.

If you need assistance with your connection, you can refer to the **Wi-fi Help Desk**.

For problems, suggestions or clarifications, please send an email to the following address: [helpwifi@unibocconi.it](mailto:helpwifi@unibocconi.it)

Library

Know more on the l [ibrary](https://lib.unibocconi.it/*eng) facility available for students

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-when-you-arrive#addtoany "Show all")

text:
For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Freshly enrolled students - Before you arrive

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-you-arrive)


Deep dive into the Bocconi University academics world. Find below all practical core details on deadlines, class and exam schedule according to your student profile.

Regular Students

Before the beginning of your academic year at Bocconi, check out:

- The [Guide to the University](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/guide-university "Guide to the University") with respect to your specific degree course
- [Timetable calendar](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/timetables-calendars-and-rooms/academic-calendar "Academic Calendar") with the key dates for the academic years
- [Course profiles](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/courses-offered-academic-programs-2025-2026-ay "Courses offered in Academic Programs 2025-2026 a.y. ") based on your specific program
- Surf your [yoU@B](https://youatb.unibocconi.it/) Student Diary! A reserved area online available to all Bocconi students. Through yoU@B, students can receive information from the University, use online services dedicated to teaching, administer some activities in their academic careers, register to exams, check dates, times and rooms for both exams and graduation sessions, check class timetables and so on.

Remember to log in and enter your student ID number as the username and your password.

All enrolled students will receive an e-mail granting them access to their yoU@B Diary.

Double Degree Students

Before the beginning of the academic year at Bocconi, Double Degree students receive a **tailored and individual** study plan, which is based on the Double Degree agreement between Bocconi University and Home Schools and it is approved by the Director of the Master of Science program in which students are enrolled at Bocconi.

It may include a mix of courses of the 1° and the 2° year of the Bocconi MSc program, according to the courses' matching between Bocconi and Home School. Bocconi credit system is ECTS equivalent.

Before the beginning of the academic year, you will be asked – if foreseen by your individual study plan – to choose your electives at Bocconi, with the possibility to change 2° semester electives during the “add/drop period” in January.

For all further information check the [Guide to the University](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/guide-university "Guide to the University")

Single Courses Students

Incoming visiting students are required to follow a specific procedure, find all further information [here](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/single-courses-visiting-students "Single courses for visiting students")

Italian and Bocconi Academic System

Find out more on the peculiarities of the Italian Academic System, as well as on Bocconi Academic approach and rules

[Find out more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-you-arrive/italian-and-bocconi-academic-system)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-you-arrive/academics#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Freshly enrolled students - Before you arrive

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-you-arrive)


The Italian tax code ("codice fiscale") is an alphanumeric code of 16 characters issued for free by the Revenue Agency to identify Italian citizens and foreign citizens.

The Revenue Agency is a non-economic public body that is mainly responsible for collecting tax revenues, providing services and assistance to taxpayers.

**N.B.: The tax code you find in the ISEU certificate, the one you can see in the portal during the admission process or on your YoU@B agenda is not the official one but is automatically generated. To obtain the official one, you must request it from the Italian Revenue Agency as it is the body in charge of issuing the Fiscal Code.**

You will need your own "codice fiscale" to:

- open a bank account
- register at the National Healthcare Service (SSN) - apply for the health card
- sign other type of contracts (i.e. phone number)

International students can apply for a Fiscal Code once in Italy in one of the Revenue Agencies/Agenzia delle Entrate. Find further information on that [at this link](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/how-to/get-italian-tax-code-codice-fiscale).

In addition to this procedure, if you are an international BSc or MSc student (including Double Degree students), you can request a “Codice Fiscale” through a special agreement between the Revenue Agency and Bocconi.

Around mid-July, instructions on how to request your fiscal code through this procedure will be shared with all regularly enrolled BSc and MSc students. Please note that this service will be available for a limited time only and is reserved to students that are at least 18 years old when the procedure is activated.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-you-arrive/fiscal-code#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Freshly enrolled students - Before you arrive

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-you-arrive)


All international students are required to have appropriate health insurance. In Italy, the National Health System is provided by a public health care service (SSN). Only foreign citizens with a valid permit of stay when needed may apply for the service offered by the ASL (local branch of the National Health Care Service).

Learn more on the **Italian National Health System** on the [YesMilano webpage](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/how-to/national-health-service-students-step-step).

Each patient is assigned a color code based on the clinical severity assessed:
- Red code: very critical, life threatening, top priority, immediate access to treatment.
- Yellow code: moderately critical, presence of evolving risk, potentially life threatening, non-deferrable service.
- Green code: not very critical, no evolving risks, deferred service.
- White code: non-critical, non-urgent patients.
After the triage, the emergency room doctor completes the patient's clinical assessment, confirming or adapting the code assigned at entrance, based on the findings made.

If, after medical intervention, the patient is discharged with a White Code (as it has been clinically shown that the service provided was not urgent), a €25.00 fee must be paid. All services provided that are identified by the emergency room doctor's clinical assessment as Green, Yellow or Red Codes are free.

**When accessing the Emergency Room, you should:**
- bring your Health Card/Regional Services Card or at least your Fiscal Code card, as well as any certificates exempting payment.
- bring your health records from any previous hospital admissions, laboratory or additional tests, etc.
- know the names and details of any treatments in use.

## Medical Services

Emergency Rooms

**Single Emergency Number: 112**

Access to the hospital Emergency Room is regulated by an urgency assessment system, triage, which identifies priorities for providing care.

Upon arrival at the emergency room, individuals receive an immediate assessment of their level of urgency by health workers, with the assignment of a color code that establishes the priority of access to care based on the severity of the case and regardless of the order of arrival at the hospital.

## Services available on campus

On-Campus Medical Center

Bocconi University has set up an on-campus medical center, a clinic on the mezzanine floor of the Piazza Sraffa 11 building. The medical center will mainly meet the needs of non-residents in Milan and those who have difficulty accessing the services of their general practitioner.

- The medical center is NOT an emergency service. In the event of an emergency, you should always call 112, the European emergency number, or the Bocconi emergency number, +39 02 5836.2121, which will activate the ambulance service if necessary.
- This is a first-level internal medicine service, which can carry out medical examinations with a medical report and provide medications and prescriptions (however, it will NOT be able to issue prescriptions covered by the National Health Service, as by law they can only be issued by your general practitioner/family doctor).
- This service is only available to members of the Bocconi community and cannot be extended to others (family members and acquaintances).

Timelines, procedures and contact information will be available in the Health and Safety widget in the yoU@B Diary and on the Intranet; please write to [hse@unibocconi.it](mailto:hse@unibocconi.it) for any questions you may have.

Stationing of Croce Bianca Ambulance

Considering the daily presence of over 15,000 people on our campus, the University has decided to guarantee the ongoing daily presence of the Associazione Croce Bianca Milano Onlus.

An ambulance with two health workers is available Monday through Friday from 9:30am to 5:30pm. They can provide first aid/healthcare monitoring to anyone who comes to the infirmary, located on the mezzanine floor at Via Bocconi 12 (e.g. taking vital signs and assessing the need for transport to an emergency room).

In addition, the presence of Croce Bianca Milano allows prompt interventions in the event of a health emergency (accident/illness) on our campus. The ambulance service can be activated by calling the Bocconi University emergency number on campus: +39 02.5836.2121.

Individual Counseling Service

Bocconi offers a free Individual Counseling service, which aims to help you identify and resolve situations of difficulty and discomfort, as well as develop your personal resources, through a track tailored for you.

The service is available in Italian, English or French.

For more information visit [this page](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/counseling-self-empowerment-and-wellbeing/counseling "Counseling").

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-you-arrive/health-services#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Freshly enrolled students - Before you arrive

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-you-arrive)

The Italian Education System is made of a 13-year cycle of pre-university education (5-year elementary school cycle, 3-year middle school cycle and 5-year high school cycle).

The University reform of 1999 transformed the organization of the Italian university, changing the structure of both the study plan and the degree programs in the fields of Economics and Management from one long four-year cycle to the so-called 3 plus 2, divided into two cycles (undergraduate and graduate).

The two study cycles are to be considered separate; at the end of each, a final exam is required, as well as the awarding of a diploma and the certification of the title obtained. Once the 3-year cycle has been completed the student can decide whether to enter the job market or continue the university studies by enrolling in a graduate program (Master of Science or a Specialized Master). In addition, Bocconi University's School of Law offers a five-year Integrated Master of Arts in Giurisprudenza (“Law”) program in Italian.

To access the university education system, there are some governmental requirements that must be fulfilled. Each university has then its own access requirements and educational approach.

Since the start of the Bocconi University graduate programs, the selection process has been distinct from that of the undergraduate one; therefore, admission to an undergraduate degree program does not imply the automatic admission to a Master of Science program. Check Bocconi [access requirements](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi "Applying to Bocconi").

You must have a 12-year diploma. If you have an 11-year diploma it must be integrated with 1 full year of university.

metadata.title: Italian and Bocconi Academic System | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Italian and Bocconi Academic System | Bocconi University

text:
There are then specific requirements for different type of diplomas:

- American diploma with a minimum of 3 APs
- IB full diploma (no IB certificates)
- British diploma with minimum 3 A Levels and at least 6 GCE

If your home country educational system requires a qualifying exam for university admission applicants must pass this exam to access the Italian University System as well.

For all further information, please visit [this page](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/application-and-admissions/secondary-school-diplomas "Secondary School Diplomas")

To access admissions to a Master of Science program, students must hold a Bachelor Diploma (1st cycle qualification) that must be suitable and valid for the purposes of accessing a 2nd cycle of studies (like a Master of Science Program is).

Bocconi University deems as valid a Bachelor Diploma (I-level degree/undergraduate Program) with at least:

- 180 educational credits (ECTS)
- a length of at least 3 years
- not professional
- comparable to an Italian Bachelor Program
- issued by a foreign University that is recognized within the educational system of the Country issuing the degree which allow continuing studies in a post-graduate (II level) program

(i.e. an undergraduate degree issued by a US University in Greece must be recognized as valid for the purposes of continuing studies in a post-graduate program in the United States).

For all further information, visit [this page](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/master-science-and-ma-programs/application-and-admissions/valid-recognized-qualifications "Valid Recognized Qualifications")

In Bocconi, exams and assessments are marked out of thirty (for exams) or given a pass/fail grade and are always individual.

A mark of between 0 and 17 out of thirty is considered unsatisfactory.

A mark of between 18 and 30 out of thirty is considered satisfactory.

The examining board may award "lode" (cum laude) to students who are awarded thirty out of thirty (30/30). A 30 "cum laude" is awarded the value of 31.

The assessment methods may be different for attending and non-attending students. Students are considered "attending" if they meet the course attendance requirements established by the faculty member.

Registration for written oral, partial exams and seminars is compulsory and exams are held in person on Campus.

Withdrawing from an exam is equivalent to not taking the exam. Handing in written exam papers (whether they are partial or general exams) specifically means accepting the mark the teacher gives the paper, no matter what it is.

For all further details, please refer to the [Guide to the University](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/guide-university "Guide to the University")

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-you-arrive/italian-and-bocconi-academic-system#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

Overview


At Bocconi we welcome an incredibly diverse group of international students for our regular programs, exchange programs and single courses. Anyone relocating to a new country for study purposes needs to consider a series of procedures before and after arrival.

We aim to guide you throughout your journey at Bocconi University to make the most out of your experience.

Whether you are a prospective student, a freshly enrolled student or a current student, here you will find concrete information on immigration procedures, relocation and administrative steps, academics and tips on how to enjoy your stay at our Campus and in Milan.

text:
For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)

The **Course Selection** for the Fall semester of the AY 2026-2027 will run from **August 24-26, 2026,** through the yoU@B student diary at [**agenda.unibocconi.it**](https://agenda.unibocconi.it/ "agenda.unibocconi.it") **.** Courses will be assigned on a **first come-first served** basis and no waiting list will be available. To maximize your chances of securing classes, we recommend staying flexible and expanding your pre-approved tentative course list to include backup options, in case you are unable to register for your preferred classes.

Exchange students will also have the possibility to change their study plan during the **Add/Drop period** scheduled between **September 7 and 15**, 2026\. After the Add/Drop deadline **no changes** will be allowed on your study plan.

**All courses** included in the students' study plans **will appear** in their Official Bocconi final **transcripts**.

Since exchange students can choose from courses across all years and Degree programs, class schedule **overlaps** may occur. Class groups with the same course code may have different schedules and may be assigned a different number of credits.

In case you need assistance in building your study plan, you can contact the [**Academic Advisors**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/services/academic-advisors).

metadata.title: Academics for exchange students: Course selection | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Academics for exchange students: Course selection | Bocconi University

text:
**STUDY PLAN GUIDELINES**

1. Exchange students must enroll in a **minimum of 1 and a maximum of 5 courses** per semester while at Bocconi and must ensure that they also satisfy their home university requirements.
2. **Undergraduate** students can select only courses at the undergraduate level (identified with code 30xxx). **Graduate** students are allowed to choose both undergraduate (30xxx) and graduate (20xxx/21xxx and 50xxx) courses.
3. Students who wish to complete all their exams during the first exam session must **ensure** there are no schedule conflicts among their exams. The exam schedule is available at the following link [www.unibocconi.eu/exams](https://didattica.unibocconi.eu/esami/index.php?&urlBack=/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/info/current+students/timetables%2c+calendars+and+rooms/exams/).
4. If **two** final **exams** are scheduled on the **same** day and at the same **time**, students can select both courses and sit the exams in **different available exam sessions**. Bocconi University has multiple exam sessions, with Fall semester exams held in December, January and February and Spring semester exams held in May, June/July.

**Additional Course Selection Rules**:

· **Language courses**: exchange students **cannot** **add language courses** to their study plan

· **Attending vs. non-attending**: students may complete courses as either attending or non-attending. There is no need to specify this status when selecting courses, as it is managed directly with the instructor after the start of classes. Students must follow the instructions outlined in the course syllabi or explained by the instructor in class

**Course restrictions**:

- some courses have specific prerequisites: carefully review the **"Suggested Background Knowledge"** section in the [course profile](https://didattica.unibocconi.eu/ts/tsn_ord_ir.php) section.
- some courses might have overlapping contents/program: students should consult instructors to determine whether they can take both courses simultaneously.

**Special Programs Requirement** s

**CEMS** students are required to select their courses from the [**CEMS specific list**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/services/courses). For further information on CEMS requirements, please refer to [cems@unibocconi.it](mailto:cems@unibocconi.it)

**THEMIS students** can find further information about courses approved by the THEMIS network and program requirements on the [**Themis webpage**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/international-mobility/network-programs/themis-law-network "THEMIS Law Network"). For further information on Themis requirements, please refer to [themis@unibocconi.it](mailto:themis@unibocconi.it).

For more information on **CIVICA** requirements, students can reach out to [civica@unibocconi.it](mailto:civica@unibocconi.it)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/academics-exchange-students-course-selection#addtoany "Show all")

text:
For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)


Exchange students are allowed to take exams during the available **official exam sessions,** within their exchange period and **on Bocconi** **premises** only. Students who sit and pass all the exams of their study plan during the first available exam session are free to leave campus starting from the **Earliest Departure Date**. Check the Earliest Departure Date on the [**Academic Calendar**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/services/academic-calendar-exchange-students). Students are entitled to sit and re-sit exams during the second exam session as well. Exam re-sits are allowed **only in case of failure**.

**Registration** for each exam must be carried out within **4 working days** prior to the exam date (Saturdays, Sundays and public holidays are not included), through the yoU@B agenda, in order to have the grades registered. Exam dates and registration deadlines are available at [**this webpage**](https://didattica.unibocconi.eu/esami/index.php).

Exams can be either oral or written; some courses might foresee mid-terms. More than one exam may be scheduled on the same day. Exam **rooms** are posted on the student's yoU@B agenda the afternoon of the working day before the exam.

**Grades** are assigned on a scale of **30/30**:

- 0 to 17: failing grades
- 18 to 30: passing grades

Exams that award a passing grade cannot be re-sat, while failed exams can be attempted again in the next available session within the exchange period.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/academics-exchange-students-exams#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)


**MBA level** incoming **exchange** students at Bocconi will be eligible to take **graduate (MSc) courses** offered within the Bocconi exchange course catalogue.

For a **limited** number of students, however, Bocconi offers the opportunity to take some **MBA courses** belonging to the **SDA Bocconi** School of Management MBA curriculum. These courses are offered in English, and they might change every year, therefore additional information will be provided as the course start date approaches.

Students regularly enrolled in the SDA MBA program will have prioritized access, therefore enrollment into MBA classes **cannot be guaranteed** in advance. Places are **limited** and assigned on the basis of the student’s CV, which will be evaluated by the SDA Bocconi MBA office.

MBA students will directly be contacted before the course selection with detailed information.
