text:
For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)


Incoming exchange students who are enrolled in the exchange program at Bocconi University are provided with instructions on how to autonomously access their **official  Bocconi University digital transcript**.

After each exam session, the transcript will be updated with the exam results, as soon as these grades are officially registered in the Bocconi systems. This process is not automatic, and might require some time.

**Please note**: grades obtained during midterms will not appear on the transcript; only final exams grades will be listed.

An official PDF version of the digital transcript will be sent to each partner university Coordinator, at the end of the semester. **Hard copies** are sent to our partner universities **ONLY upon request**.

To ensure the security and authenticity of e-transcripts, these documents are released through the Bocconi system and can be verified at this [link](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/student-administration/certifications/verification-certificates-electronic-format).

The conversion of Bocconi grades is managed by the student’s home university, based on their grading conversion criteria.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/academics-exchange-students-transcript-grades#addtoany "Show all")

text:
For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)



For further information about the cost of living in Milan, you can also visit this webpage on YesMilano's [website](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/cost-living-milano).

Housing

- Single room, on-campus dorm: 4000-4800€ per semester
- Private single room: 600 - 1100€ (per month)
- Private double room: 500 - 750€ (per month)
- Flat: 800 - 1800€ (per month)

Transports

- Electronic card: 10€ (first issue)
- Monthly pass for students up to 27: 22€
- Monthly pass for students over 27: 39€
- Single ride ticket: 2,20€
- Daily ticket: 7,60€
- Bike sharing: 4,50€ per day - 9€ per week - 36€ per year

Meals

- Italian breakfast: 2 - 4€ (espresso, coffee or cappuccino + croissant)
- Brunch: 20 - 30€
- One main course at the university canteen: 5€
- Lunch at cafes: 10-20€
- Ice cream: 2 - 5€
- Dinner at restaurant: 30 - 50€
- Dinner at pizzeria: 15 - 30€

Permit of Stay

- 100,46€ + 16€ for the revenue stamp

Health insurance (WAI ITALY)

- 6 months: 91€
- 1 year: 140€

Italian Language Courses

- In-person Italian Language Crash course: 250€
- In-person Italian Language Follow-up course: 200€

Entertainment

- Aperitivo/Happy hour: 7 - 15€
- Cinema: 7 - 13€
- Pub: 7 - 20€
- Concerts: 15 - 100€

Cell phone

- Sim card: 10€

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/estimated-costs#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)


The Italian Tax Code (“ **Codice Fiscale**”) is a personal alphanumeric code (combination of numbers and letters) used to recognize individuals in Italy. It is required when dealing with public offices and/or the public administration.

The “Codice Fiscale” is not compulsory. However, incoming exchange students need to obtain one to open a bank account, sign specific contracts (i.e. phone line), register for the National Healthcare Service (SSN). **Please note**: according to the Italian Revenue Agency guidelines, exchange students who are not registered as temporary residents in Milano are [**not** required](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/how-to/get-italian-tax-code-codice-fiscale#paragraph-11861) to obtain a fiscal code in order to sign a rental contract.

The Codice Fiscale must be requested **after** arrival in Italy. Exchange students can follow the application procedure at this [link](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/how-to/get-italian-tax-code-codice-fiscale).

The fiscal code application procedure is **free**.

Please note that Bocconi University does not provide exchange students with a  codice fiscale, and that the one generated automatically through the Bocconi application and the Universitaly application is invalid.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/fiscal-codes-request#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)


Please **check out this** [**link**](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/how-get-milano) **if you want to know more on how to get to Milan.**

**PUBLIC TRANSPORTATION**

Milan public transportation system is managed by Azienda Trasporti Milanesi ( **ATM**) and offers 5 **metro** lines and several **tram** and **bus** lines. More information on how to get around the city with **public transportation** as well as on tickets at this [**link**](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/subway-trams-and-buses).

ATM tickets can be purchased directly on a tram, bus or metro contactless with a credit or a debit card. More information on the [**ATM website**](https://www.atm.it/EN/VIAGGIACONNOI/Pages/default.aspx).

Students can apply for an **ATM transportation card**, an electronic, rechargeable pass which allows you to travel throughout the Milan urban area by ATM subway, buses and trams. ATM offers different kinds of cards in order to meet all travelers' needs, including a monthly, unlimited pass.

Please visit [**ATM website**](https://www.atm.it/en/ViaggiaConNoi/Abbonamenti/Pages/Tesseraelettronic.aspx) for more information.

**SHARING MOBILITY**

In the last years Milan has embraced several **sharing mobility services** by pursuing a more sustainable and livable city lifestyle.

Students can find how to use **bike, car sharing, motorscooter and kick scooter sharing** apps at [**this webpage**](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/kick-scooter-bike-motorscooter-sharing-rental).

**CAR**

Students who wish to **explore Milan by car** can find useful information **on parking** and **traffic restrictions** and regulations at this [**link**](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/car-sharing).

**TAXI/CABS**

Milan **cabs** are white. Late night, Sunday and extra-luggage surcharges are applied. Not every taxi accepts credit cards, we suggest to verify in advance. If in doubt of the price, ask the driver for an estimate before getting in ( **for booking**: Radio Taxi 02 8585; Taxiblu 02 4040 or Yellow Taxi Multiservice 02 6969).

There are **fixed fares** from and to the airports, more info [**here**](https://intaxi.it/tariffe-taxi-milano/).

**TRAINS**

The **main stations** in Milan are Stazione Centrale, Milano Rogoredo and Milano Garibaldi. Remember to get your ticket before your journey as ‘on-board tickets’ are subject to a surcharge. Remember also to **validate** your **ticket** (if it is a paper ticket) by stamping it through the validating machines at the track platform or around the stations. Expired or not validated tickets are subject to fines. If you **did not** **validate** your ticket, contact immediately the ‘on board’ representative.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/getting-around#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)


Before their arrival, students should ensure they  are properly **covered** in case they require  **medical care during their stay in Italy**.Bocconi University does not provide students with an insurance policy.Students shouldcheckif the social security scheme in their  country of residence covers the cost of medical treatment in Italy.

**EU citizens**: be sure to bring the **European Health Insurance Card** ( **EHIC**), which entitles the holder to get necessary treatment at the same cost as  citizens of the host country. The card is valid only for **state-provided services**, asprivate healthcare is not covered. We recommend that , before departure, students verify with the social security administration of their home country how to obtain  the card and which services are covered.

EU citizens can also apply for **Form S1**. They should obtain their S1 form from the healthcare institution of their  home country **before  departure**. Students should confirm eligibility with the social security institution of their country of residence, as requirements may vary between EU member states.

**Non-EU citizens**: before leaving their home country these students are strongly advised to obtain **health** **insurance  with international coverage.** To apply for a Permit of Stay, students will be required to prove that they are covered for medical expenses  for the entire duration of their stay in Italy.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/health-insurance#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)


Incoming exchange students will have the chance to reserve a room in a student residence hall located on campus or in its immediate surroundings.

For the academic year 2026/2027 incoming exchange students will have a dedicated housing application. Housing at Bocconi is not guaranteed and rooms are assigned on a first come - first served basis.

In order to apply for housing at Bocconi, students are required to complete a **separate housing application**, managed directly by the Bocconi Housing Office.

The Housing Office will contact nominated students before the housing application opens, by providing them with all the relevant instructions on how to complete it.

Information on AY 26-27 housing is available on [this webpage](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/exchange-incoming-housing-ay-2026-27).

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/housing-information-incoming-exchange-students#addtoany "Show all")

Bocconi offers you the chance to make the most of student life by staying in one of our 8 university residence halls. You’ll live in the heart of campus, in an international, intercultural and stimulating environment.

It is a life experience that will stay with you forever.

Bocconi has a detailed system of tuition and fees, financial assistance and scholarships. This system aims to provide all students the necessary financial means to complete their studies.

The University also offers students the chance to make the most of their university experience by staying in one of the 8 university residence halls.

**CONTACT US**

The International Student Desk at Bocconi University is the office in charge of incoming exchange students.

Our **On-campus Front Desk** and **Virtual Info Desk** are here to help answer any questions about your exchange.

- Tuesday: 2.30pm-4.30pm

- Friday: 10am-12.30pm (virtual)

The On-campus Front Desk and Virtual Info Desk will be **closed** during the Welcome Week (January 26–30). You can find the ISD Team at the Welcome Desk, on floor -2 of the Roentgen building at Via Roentgen 1.

**March 3 to June 30**

- Thursdays: 10am-12.30pm

International Student Desk

Università Bocconi

International Relations

Piazza Sraffa, 11 - Room 303

20136 Milan - Italy

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)



Since the Exchange is a full time program, internships cannot be activated during the exchange teaching period.

Students can refer to the Career Services to look for internship opportunities and prepare for job interviews.

Full details on the support provided are available on the [Career Services webpage](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/career-services "Career Services").

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/internships#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students [Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students) Incoming Exchange Students [Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students) ### **Italian Language Crash Course** **Kick off your exchange experience with a 2-week immersive Italian Crash Course!** Before Bocconi classes begin, you’ll have the chance to start learning Italian while meeting fellow exchange students from around the world and building your first friendships in Milan. Through dynamic, interactive lessons led by experienced native Italian teachers, you’ll develop essential language skills—speaking, listening, reading, and writing—while discovering Italian culture and improving your intercultural communication. | | | | --- | --- | | **Registration and Payment** | Interested students will be able to enroll and pay for the course in the Exchange Program online application, after taking an online placement test. After submitting the test, students will no longer be able to access it. | | **Registration fee** | 250€ (textbook not included) | | **Levels** | Beginner, Pre-Elementary, Elementary, Pre-Intermediate, Intermediate and Advanced. Please note: levels will be activated according to the number of enrollments. | | **Class period** | Fall 26: August 25 - September 5, 2026
Spring 27: TBC | | **Class Schedule** | available in the exchange program online application, upon registering for the course. The duration of each class ranges between 2 and 5 hours. | | **Workload** | 40 hours | | **Attendance and Final Certificate** | Students who attend a minimum of 75% of the lessons and pass the final test will receive the Italian Language Course Participation Certificate, which includes an overall evaluation: pass, fair, good or excellent. Late arrivals or early departures will be counted towards the total number of absences. Please note: the Crash course is an **optional**, non-credit granting extracurricular course, therefore it is not included in the official Bocconi transcript or in the study plan. | | **Refund Policy** | The registration fee will be fully refunded if:
- the course is cancelled by the ISD for reasons of force majeure or the ISD is unable to confirm the student's course registration
- the student withdraws from the Exchange Program |

## **Italian Language Follow-Up Course** **Keep the momentum going!** After completing the Italian Crash Course, exchange students can continue improving their Italian throughout the semester with the Follow-Up Course. **This course is available only to students who enroll in and pass the Crash Course** (Beginner to Pre-Intermediate levels), offering a great way to keep practicing the language while staying connected with international classmates. | | | | --- | --- | | **Registration and Payment** | Interested students will be able to enroll and pay for the course in Exchange Program online application. **Please note:** it is not possible to register for the Follow-Up Course without registering for the Crash Course first. | | **Registration fee** | 200€ (textbook not included) | | **Levels** | Pre-Elementary, Elementary, Pre-Intermediate and Intermediate. Please note: levels will be activated according to the number of enrollments. | | **Class period** | Fall 26: September 21 - November 23, 2026
Spring 27: TBC | | **Class schedule** | Classes of 2 hours twice a week held in the late afternoon. The schedule will be available in the exchange program online application, upon registering for the course. | | **Workload** | 30 hours | | **Attendance and Final Certificate** | Students who attend a minimum of 75% of the lessons and pass the final test will receive the Italian Language Course Participation Certificate, which will also include an overall evaluation: pass, fair, good or excellent. Please note: the Follow-Up course is an **optional**, non-credit granting extracurricular course, therefore it is not included in the official Bocconi transcript or in the study plan. | | **Refund Policy** | The registration fee will be fully refunded if:
- the courses are cancelled by the ISD for reasons of force majeure or the ISD is unable to confirm the student's course registration
- the student withdraws from the Exchange Program
NO refund is foreseen for: 
- students who are enrolled in the Follow-Up Course but do not sit the Crash Course final test 
- students who are enrolled in the Follow-Up Course but do not reach the Crash Course attendance requirements (75%) 
- students who are enrolled in the Follow-Up Course but do not pass the Crash Course final test
- in case of overlap with Bocconi classes: please check carefully Bocconi class timetable at [this link](https://didattica.unibocconi.eu/lezioni/ "this link") | [More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/italian-language-courses#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)



As preparing to live abroad for one semester can be quite challenging, exchange students can find **general information about Milan** on this [**YesMilano webpage**](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/leaving-home).

**Milan** is known as the **economic capital** of Italy and the leading Italian hub for services, finance and industry. **Milan is the engine of the country's economy** and home of the Italian stock exchange. This cosmopolitan, modern and forward-looking city is world-renowned as a **European capital of fashion, design, architecture, finance, publishing, modern art and music**. It is deeply rooted in a flourishing past and hosts authentic treasures.

During the stay at Bocconi, students will have the opportunity to take part in various activities and explore a wide variety of interesting places around the city.


Must-see attractions

The cultural and sightseeing offer of Milan

[Have a look here](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/attractions)


What's on

Learn more about festivals, concerts and events

[Have a look here](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/whats-on)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/living-city#addtoany "Show all")

Incoming Exchange Students

#### **Euromedica**

Health center located at **Viale Bligny 11**. After signing up for an annual membership card at the **cost of €12**, students, faculty and staff have a **10%** discount on specific outpatient services. For further information [click here](https://www.euromedica-assistance.com/).

#### **Individual Counseling**

A **Counseling and Self-Empowerment** desk is available to students, to support their psychological well-being and help them develop greater self-awareness of their personal resources. Individual counseling (offered in Italian, English or French) aims to help **identify** and **resolve** situations of **difficulty** and **discomfort**, while also strengtheningtheir personal resources, through a personalized pathway developed together. Further **information** available [**here**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/services/counseling).

#### **Health Services**

For further information about **health services** and the on-campus medical center during your stay in Italy visit [**this webpage**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/freshly-enrolled-students-you-arrive/health-services "Health Services").

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/medical-assistance#addtoany "Show all")

For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)



The Fall 2026 online application will be open from April 9 to May 20 at 3pm (Italian time), 2026.

To apply online you will need the USER ID and PASSWORD that will be sent to you and your Home University coordinator.

IMPORTANT: the online application will open on a rolling basis, based on the date your nomination is received from your Home University.

In the application you will be required to upload the most updated version of:

- CV (resume)
- Grade report
- Headshot photo (for the Bocconi ID card)
- Passport personal data page (EU citizens can submit their national ID)
- Disability certificate issued by the Home University (only for students in need of special accommodations)

For questions on the Exchange program, please refer to [inexchange@unibocconi.it](mailto:inexchange@unibocconi.it).

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students/online-application#addtoany "Show all")

Students must complete a 2-step process:
- Pre-register and submit the application on the Universitaly portal (mandatory) at [https://www.universitaly.it/](https://eur03.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.universitaly.it%2F&data=05%7C02%7Cvaleria.crincoli%40unibocconi.it%7C052b57df291447b9c2f708dd72c6de4c%7C6bf3b57a9fb447c29ada51156518f52f%7C1%7C0%7C638792920634676347%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=gAVv02%2BS2Xyk3NBAHk7mzdoSqAfnJ0N74ao2qQ8zOr0%3D&reserved=0 "Original URL: https://www.universitaly.it/. Click or tap if you trust this link."). This application is different and separate from the Bocconi Exchange Program online application. _Please note: the procedure to complete the Universitaly application for the Academic Year 2026–2027 is not yet open; a specific communication will follow with detailed instructions on this mandatory step._
- Apply for a Study Visa: procedures may vary from Consulate to Consulate. Students should follow the instructions provided on the website of the Consulate where they plan to apply. We recommend that students review visa procedures and start searching for a **visa appointment well in advance**.

text:
Please note that, according to the Italian Authorities and ministries, acceptance into the exchange program does not guarantee the issuance of a visa. The decision is made solely by the relevant diplomatic mission, Consulate or Embassy.

Universitaly

_Please note: the procedure to complete the Universitaly application for the Academic Year 2026–2027 is not yet open; a specific communication will follow with detailed instructions on this mandatory step._

Pre-register on the Universitaly portal at [https://www.universitaly.it/](https://eur03.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.universitaly.it%2F&data=05%7C02%7Cvaleria.crincoli%40unibocconi.it%7C052b57df291447b9c2f708dd72c6de4c%7C6bf3b57a9fb447c29ada51156518f52f%7C1%7C0%7C638792920634676347%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=gAVv02%2BS2Xyk3NBAHk7mzdoSqAfnJ0N74ao2qQ8zOr0%3D&reserved=0 "Original URL: https://www.universitaly.it/. Click or tap if you trust this link.")

To register, click on " **First steps in Italy**" box in the homepage and follow the registration guide. After that click on “ **Pre-enroll now**" that will appear in your personal area.

You will be asked to fill out a **3 step-form**:

**STEP A**:
- complete the form with your personal data: it must be entered exactly as shown on your passport.
**STEP B**:
- fill out the form with your passport number and expiration date, then upload it (the page with your personal data only)
- upload a passport-size picture of your face
- select the country and the Italian Embassy or Consulate where you will be requesting your visa; then in the section “specify the reason for the visa application” type in “I want to spend a period of mobility for study/traineeship (e.g. Erasmus)”
- select the University (Università Commerciale Luigi Bocconi), select Corsi Singoli in “course type” drop-down list
- in the “course name” tab, type in “Exchange Program”
**STEP C**:
- as an exchange student, in this section you must submit your Bocconi acceptance letter (downloadable from the Bocconi online Exchange Program application). In the “Qualifications and Language Certificates” drop-down list, select “Other”, then type in “Bocconi Acceptance Letter” in the “Qualification name” tab and upload the letter.

text:
For International Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students)

Incoming Exchange Students

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-students/incoming-exchange-students)

All non-EU exchange students must apply for a **Student Visa** before coming to Italy.

To learn more about visa application deadlines and **requirements**, visit [http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en.](https://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/?lang=it_IT "this link") A guided procedure will help you find out whether you need to apply for a visa and where - based on your nationality, country of residence, reasons for visit and length of stay. Depending on your situation, you may be required to apply for a visa in your home country, in your country of residence or in the country where you are currently studying.

Visa requirements may vary. The official Acceptance Letter, issued by Bocconi University, is always required.

**Application Process**

text:
For International Students

Overview


At Bocconi we welcome an incredibly diverse group of international students for our regular programs, exchange programs and single courses. Anyone relocating to a new country for study purposes needs to consider a series of procedures before and after arrival.

We aim to guide you throughout your journey at Bocconi University to make the most out of your experience.

Whether you are a prospective student, a freshly enrolled student or a current student, here you will find concrete information on immigration procedures, relocation and administrative steps, academics and tips on how to enjoy your stay at our Campus and in Milan.

The MSc in Finance offers students different study abroad options, both for short periods (such as Exchange programs) and for longer periods (Double Degree), depending on the track Finance Track or Global Experience Track you’re enrolled in.

_Double Degrees_

If you are attending the Finance Track, you can apply to participate in a Double Degree program. These programs allow you to study at two top business and economic schools and receive two diplomas, one from each University. You will spend the first academic year at Bocconi and the second academic year at a prestigious partner.

- **ESADE** Escuela Superior de Administracion y Direccion de Empresas (Barcelona, Spain)

The special programs that must be **selected during your online application** are the following:
- **IM CEMS MIM** is recognized as a one-of-a-kind global community, founded in 1988, bringing together the world’s best students, schools, alumni, and corporate and social partners. Admitted students will spend their first year at Bocconi University and one semester of their sophomore year at one of the premier universities in the **CEMS alliance** (33 schools across 6 continents).

- **IM Asia - China MIM** is a Double Degree program with Fudan University, one of the top universities in Asia. Students attending this program will study at the Bocconi Milan campus in Italy during the first year and at the Fudan Shanghai campus in China in the second year. They will have the chance to live and study in one of the world’s most influential and rapidly evolving countries. Students will deepen their understanding of China and the global economy, acquiring the expertise and insights needed to start an international career.

- **IM Asia - DD ESSEC** is a Double Degree program with ESSEC Business School, offering students the incredible opportunity to study at three different locations: the ESSEC Cergy campus in France, the ESSEC Singapore campus, and the Bocconi Milan campus in Italy. This unique format provides a comprehensive understanding of both Europe and Asia, allowing students to explore economic, political and management issues from diverse global perspectives. In addition to a rich mix of courses and internships, participants will gain valuable research experience while working on their thesis.

- **IM Concentration - DD ESSEC Luxury Management** is a Double Degree program with ESSEC Business School, offering a global outlook on luxury management. Students start their journey in Milan — Italy’s fashion and design capital — then learn more about luxury business at ESSEC’s dynamic campus near Paris. Participants in the program will gain global exposure, hands-on experience and a powerful network to launch their careers in one of the most exciting industries in the world.
_Exchange Programs_

If you are attending IM Global Experience and IM Concentrations you have the opportunity to participate in an Exchange program, which will allow you to study abroad for one term at a top partner university. A few of our most prestigious Exchange partners include:
- **Duke University**, Fuqua School of Business (US)
- **London Business Schoo** l (UK)
- **National Chengchi University**(Taiwan)
- **Jiao Tong University** (China)
- **The University of Sydney**(Australia)
_Double Degrees_

If you are attending IM Concentrations, you can apply to participate in a Double Degree program. These programs allow you to study at Bocconi University and a second top business and economic schools and receive two diplomas, one from each University. You will spend the first academic year at Bocconi and the second academic year at a prestigious partner to broaden your knowledge regarding specific markets and to integrate your academic profile with a specialization in functional areas/industries.

- **Bayes School of Business**(London, UK)
- **Darla Moore School of Business**(Columbia, US)
- **ESADE** Escuela Superior de Administracion y Direccion de Empresas (Barcelona, Spain)
- **FGV-EAESP** Escola de Administração de Empresas de São Paulo da Fundação Getulio Vargas (São Paulo, Brazil)
- **HEC** École des Hautes Etudes Commerciales (Paris, France)
- **HEC** Montréal (Montréal, Canada)
- **HSG** University of St. Gallen (St. Gallen, Switzerland)
- **IIMA** Indian Institute of Management (Ahmedabad, India)
- **IIMB**(Indian Institute of Management, Bangalore, India)
- **NHH** Norwegian School of Economics (Bergen, Norway)
- **RSM** Rotterdam School of Management, Erasmus University (Rotterdam, the Netherlands)
- **The University of Queensland**(Brisbane, Australia)
- **WU** **Wirtschaftsuniversität Wien**(Vienna, Austria)

The MSc in Politics and Policy Analysis offers students different study abroad options, both for short periods (such as Exchange programs) and for longer periods (Double Degrees).

**Some of these options can be chosen after enrollment, while others have to be selected at the time of application.**

The Double Degree programs that must be **selected during your online application** are the following:

- **Sciences Po** (Paris, France), a Double Degree program in the area of European and International Public Policy and Politics for outstanding students, taught by renowned experts in public policy and politics. The first year is spent at Bocconi, and the second year will be at Sciences Po.
- **London School of Economics and Political Science**(London, UK), a Double Degree program in European and International Public Policy and Politics, delivered by world-leading experts in public policy and politics. The first year is spent at Bocconi, and the second year will be at LSE.

**At the end of the first semester** of your first year **at Bocconi** you may be selected for other Double Degree programs or Exchange Programs.

- **Universität Mannheim** (Mannheim, Germany)

Each course consists of **48 hours.** The sessions include: - **teaching activities** running from **Monday to Friday,** usually **between 9:30am and 12:30pm** - **company visits** In addition to the above activities, students will be required to complete individual/class assignments and will have the possibility to take part in **extracurricular activities**, which will take place outside of the time reserved for the academic activities. Students fulfilling attendance requirements will receive a digital certificate attesting their participation in the course. Important: students who are also interested in earning **6 credits\*** are required to sit a **final exam (on the last day)** on campus in Milan. \*in accordance with the ECTS - European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System - on average 1 credit point corresponds to 25 hours of work for student; about 8 hours (but no more than 10) are spent in the classroom. Sample schedule | | | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | | Monday | Tuesday | Wednesday | Thursday | Friday | | **9:30am - 12:30pm** | Lecture | Lecture | Lecture | Company Visit | Lecture | | **12:30pm - 2:00pm** | Lunch break | Lunch break | Lunch break | Lunch break | Lunch break | | **2:00pm - 5:00pm** | Lecture | Group work | Group work | Lecture | Group work | Participation Fee The Summer School participation fee **includes** the following: - academic program (including guest speakers testimonies) and access to materials (for the duration of the program) - one company visit - extracurricular activities - official certificate (electronic format) - transportation card The Summer School participation fee **does not include** the following: - accomodation - meals - airport pick-up - visa support | Fee structure | | | --- | --- | | **Regular fee** | €2,700 | | **Reduced fee**
Students enrolled in a program at:
\- a Bocconi partner university/school (see list [here](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-network/partner-schools)) 
\- a university/school part of the ELFA network (see list [here](https://elfa-edu.org/elfa-members/)) | €2,500 | | |

**Refund policy**

A refund of the participation fee will only be granted in cases of **force majeure** **and supported by official documentation**(e.g., visa rejection letter, medical certificate).

Each request will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis by the Summer School Committee.

In such cases, an **administrative fee of €350** will be retained by Bocconi.

No refunds will be provided for other reasons.

Housing

Summer School participants will have the possibility to be accommodated at **Aparto Residence Giovenale**, in front of the campus ( **limited availability**).

Students interested in reserving a room will be required to fill out a separate application.

**More information are available** [**here**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/summer-school-2026-housing) **.**

Visa

Obtaining a visa for Italy is a **responsibility of students** and Bocconi recommends starting the process as soon as possible.

Students unsure whether they need a visa or where to find more information can take a quick **survey** [here](https://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/?lang=en_US) (when selecting the "Reason for your stay," please choose "Study - UNIVERSITY SINGLE COURSE").

For visa applications, students should contact their local Italian Embassy or Consulate. Bocconi provides an **official letter of acceptance** **to the program**, which can be used as part of the visa application process. This document can be downloaded directly from the admission portal once the application has been successfully submitted.

Programs

Summer School

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/summer-school)

[MyApplication](https://admissions.unibocconi.it/admissions?cod_lingua=eng)

Bocconi Summer School for Bachelor Students will be held from **June 29** to **July 17**, **2026\.**

Please find below all the information to apply.

How to Apply

Bocconi Summer School offers a three-week study program open to motivated **students enrolled in a Bachelor program**.

The summer school is dedicated to **students from Italian and non-Italian Universities** (it is not open to Bocconi students).

In order to apply, candidates must fill out the **online application** through the My Application portal (applications must include the most recent university transcript in English. If a transcript is unavailable, a certificate of enrollment will suffice).

Applicants are asked to **select their desired course, proceed to the payment section and submit their application** to secure their spot. Spots may fill up quickly. After this step, course changes will not be accepted.

Application Timeline

**Applications for Bocconi Summer School 2026 are now open.**

NEW DEADLINE: Applications will close on **May 7** ( **6 p.m. Italian time**).

Spots may fill up quickly, it is strongly recommended to apply as soon as possible to secure the participation.

Course Structure

Programs

Summer School

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/summer-school)

_Please find below useful information on security and safety measures for your staying during our Summer School programs._

International SOS

Bocconi has partnered with **International SOS** to offer medical, safety and security advice to its international students coming here on campus.

International SOS - **that is not an insurance company and does not replace personal medical insurance** \- provides Bocconi students with quality health care and **medical** and **security advice** services worldwide 24/7.

Services are designed to help with medical, personal, travel, security, logistical and legal problems that may arise when away from home.

International SOS services include **advice** on health and security issues as well as **assistance** in case of medical or security emergency.

Through the International SOS Assistance App you will be able to contact the Assistance Centre, read Italy country guide and receive notifications.

**Please check your e-mail for further details on how to activate and use International SOS.**

Control Room

On the Bocconi Campus, a system of prevention and security is in place to respond to any emergencies that may occur.

The Control Room monitors the security of our Campus on a daily basis. Two professional operators are in charge of presiding **the Room 24 hours a day, 7 days a week** controlling the most crowded spaces in our Campus as well as the most hidden ones through **96 screens** and approximately **500 AI-powered cameras and the SOS Alarm Columns**, which are placed in several areas of the University buildings. The room is also constantly connected with the police forces and health emergency enforcement in case of emergency.

Emergency Number - 02.5836.2121

The Emergency Number **02.5836.2121** is available on campus for anyone who detects a dangerous situation or an emergency within the Bocconi Campus.

According to the specific needs, the operators in the Control Room, who will promptly get in touch with the occupational safety workers, police forces and medical supplies (including the ambulance which is always on Campus), will answer.

**Reported details should be as clear and complete as possible, to allow operators and emergency teams to intervene promptly.**

The single emergency number **can also be called through the SOS Alarm Columns** widespread on Campus.

First Aid on Campus

An ambulance and two paramedics are present on campus from **Monday to Friday** **from 9:30am to 6:00pm** to ensure immediate availability of an ambulance for transport to the nearest Hospital, along with the support of specialized personnel for the management of illnesses and other health emergencies. The service can be activated **through the internal emergency management number +39 025836 2121.**

Read the paragraph "On Campus Medical Center" for all further info on the services available on campus.

On Campus Medical Assistance

Bocconi University has set up an on-campus **medical center**, a clinic on the mezzanine floor of the Piazza Sraffa 11 building to meet the needs of non-residents in Milan.

**The doctor is available from Monday to Friday from 9:00am to 6:00pm.**

The Medical Center is a first-level internal medicine service, which can carry out medical examinations with a medical report and provide medications and prescriptions. This service is only available to members of the Bocconi community and cannot be extended to others (family members and acquaintances).

**Please note that the medical center is NOT an emergency service**. In the event of an **emergency, you should always call** **112**, the European emergency number, or the **Bocconi emergency number, +39 02 5836.2121**, which will activate the ambulance service if necessary.

Besides the on-campus doctor, **nurses** will be available **24/7** for medical assistance.

**Steps to apply:** 1. Register on the My Application Portal ( [www.unibocconi.eu/admissionsportal)](https://www.unibocconi.eu/admissionsportal) 2. Fill out the online application with the information and documents required 3. Select 2 preferred labs **\*** 4. Pay the Program fee 5. Submit data and submit application to secure your spot Once submitted the application you will not be able to change it anymore. **Required elements to apply:** - Scan of a valid national ID card or passport - Scan of a valid parent/guardian national ID card or passport - Digital photo for student ID (headshot) - Student transcripts of school year 2024-25 in English or Italian with subjects studied and marks obtained (including a school profile or a conversion table indicating the min. and max. achievable grade, if available). \*Students interested in the “Bocconi Summer School in Law – Lab: Diritto dell’economia e delle nuove tecnologie,” offered in Italian from July 6 to 10, 2026, have the following options: - **Option 1:** Participate only in the “Bocconi Summer School in Law – Diritto dell’economia e delle nuove tecnologie,” offered in Italian from July 6 to 10, 2026, by selecting the corresponding lab. - **Option 2:** Participate in both the “Bocconi Summer School in Law – Diritto dell’economia e delle nuove tecnologie” (July 6–10, 2026) and one of the labs offered by the Summer School for High School students (July 13–17, 2026). Application Timeline **Bocconi Summer School Application for 2026 is sold out.** Few spots are still available just for the Summer School in Law **in Italian** from July 6 to July 10, 2026. Applications will close on **April 9** ( **6 p.m. Italian time**). Spots may fill up quickly, it is strongly recommended to apply as soon as possible to secure the participation. Sample Schedule The on campus program includes **live classes usually scheduled between 9:30am and 12:30pm**, as a general rule. Students will follow lectures taught by the Bocconi Faculty and will work on a **group project** that will be presented in the last class. Additionally, students will have the chance to hear **guest speakers** and testimonials from prominent actors in various industries. Each course schedule will be made available to participants before the start of the program. | | | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | | **Monday** | **Tuesday** | **Wednesday** | **Thursday** | **Friday** | | **9:30am**
**—**
**12:30pm** | Lecture | Lecture | Lecture | Lecture | Project presentations | | **12:30pm**
**—**
**2:00pm** | Lunch break | Lunch break | Lunch break | Lunch break | Lunch break | | **2:00pm**
**—**
**5:00pm** | | Group work | Guest Speakers/
Testimonials | Group work | | Participation Fee The Bocconi Summer School participation fee is **€2,700**. The Summer School participation fee **includes** the following: - academic program (including guest speakers testimonies) and access to materials (for the duration of the program) - extracurricular activities - official certificate (electronic format) - transportation card

The Summer School participation fee **does not include** the following:
- accommodation
- meals
- airport pick-up
- visa support
**Refund policy**

A refund of the participation fee will only be granted in cases of **force majeure** **and supported by official documentation**(e.g., visa rejection letter, medical certificate).

Each request will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis by the Summer School Committee.

In such cases, an **administrative fee of €350** will be retained by Bocconi.

No refunds will be provided for other reasons.

Housing

For a **full Bocconi life experience**, Summer School participants may choose to **stay in one of our university residence halls**, all walking distance from the campus.

**Summer School Housing:** [**more information**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/summer-school-2026-housing)

**Room Type**

Rooms available will be all single bedrooms, with shared bathroom.

**Period**

Check in: from July 5 at 10AM

Check out: 17 July at 6PM

**Price**

€ 1,600 (for the whole period, 2 weeks)

**How to apply**

Students interested in reserving a room will be required **to fill out a separate application**. Please make sure to complete the program application first.

[Here](https://www.campusvr.unibocconi.it/en/Tour/List) you can find a Virtual Tour of the campus.

**Meal plan**

There is no meal plan provided in the fee. However, students can access the canteens and cafeterias on campus and there are plenty of restaurants and bar nearby offering a wide variety of food options at affordable prices.

In the weeks before the arrival we will provide students with information on the logistic: how to reach our campus, breakfast/lunch/dinner options on campus and nearby the university, public transport info, etc.

Visa

Obtaining a visa for Italy is a **responsibility of students** and Bocconi recommends starting the process as soon as possible.

Students unsure whether they need a visa or where to find more information can take a quick **survey** [here](https://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/?lang=en_US) (when selecting the "Reason for your stay," please choose "Study - UNIVERSITY SINGLE COURSE").

For visa applications, students should contact their local Italian Embassy or Consulate. Bocconi provides an **official letter of acceptance** **to the program**, which can be used as part of the visa application process. This document can be downloaded directly from the admission portal once the application has been successfully submitted.

**Application for Admissions to Bocconi Bachelor of Science programs 2026-27**

Please note that participation in the Summer School is **independent** from the selection process for Bocconi Bachelor of Science programs.

Programs

Summer School

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/summer-school)

[MyApplication](https://www.unibocconi.eu/admissionsportal)

How to Apply

Application is open to high school students in their **third-to-last and second-to-last year** in Italy or abroad.

Only students **who will have already turned 15 years old** by the start of the program are eligible to participate in Bocconi Summer School.

## Schedule & Activities

Here you can find all the details you need to know about our exciting **curricular** and **extracurricular activities**.

Explore our comprehensive **schedule**, stay updated with important updates, and access a variety of links to enhance your summer learning experience.

Make the most out of your summer with us!

**Welcome and opening ceremony**

**Monday 6 July, morning (via Roentgen 1, Aula Magna)**

Welcome to Bocconi Summer School!

After receiving your welcome kit at the reception desk, you will attend the opening ceremony and then join a campus tour.

You will receive an e-mail with the meeting time.

**Lectures and group work**

**Monday 6 July, afternoon**

The first lecture will take place on the first day, after lunch. All the details will be shared in the morning during the opening ceremony.

For all the following lectures and group work sessions you have to refer to your course syllabus, on [Blackboard](https://blackboard.unibocconi.it/) (Bocconi e-learning platform).

**BSc Programs Presentation**

**Wednesday 15 July, afternoon (via Roentgen 1, Aula Magna)**

The Summer School can be a way to experience Bocconi before considering it for your Bachelor. Find out about our BSc programs: offer, admissions procedures, financial aid, student and campus life. This is the perfect moment to ask all your questions!

**Cooking Class**

**First week of the program, after 5pm (FoodGenius Academy - viale col di lana, 8)**

Make new friends while learning how to make Italian pasta with a professional chef... and don’t forget to come hungry!

**Bocconi Sport**

**During the program**

Enjoy our Sport Center! Summer School students have the possibility to access Bocconi sport facilities to make the most of their experience.

**Farewell Party**

**Thursday 16 July, evening**

Celebrate your Summer School experience with your classmates during a farewell "aperitivo" - location and timing will be sent via e-mail


Blackboard

Available from June 23

[Log in](https://blackboard.unibocconi.it/ultra/institution-page)


Discover Milan

[Discover more](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/summer-milan)


Share your experience with the world!

[Follow us](https://www.instagram.com/meet_bocconi/)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/summer-school/high-school-students/summer-school-bocconi-what-awaits-you#addtoany "Show all")

Malpensa airport is easily connected to downtown Milan through the [Malpensa Express Train](https://www.malpensaexpress.it/en/) (13€ single ride): you can choose to get off either at the Central Station or at Cadorna Station. For all further information on public transport see the section below.
- If you take the train to the Central Station ("Centrale F.S."), from there you can take the metro (yellow line – direction "San Donato") to "Duomo" (4 stops). Then, go to the stop "Duomo" of tram n.15. Take tram n.15 in the "Rozzano" direction and get off at the stop "Porta Ludovica" (4 stops).
- If you take the train to Cadorna Station, from there you can take the metro (red line - direction "Sesto 1 Maggio FS") to "Duomo" (3 stops). Then, go to the stop "Duomo" of tram n.15. Take tram n.15 in the "Rozzano" direction and get off at the stop "Porta Ludovica" (4 stops).

text:
**Malpensa Shuttle (bus) + public transport**: it takes about 1 hour and 30 minutes. For all further information on public transport see the section below.

As an alternative to the Malpensa Express train, you can take the [Malpensa Shuttle](https://www.malpensashuttle.it/). The ticket costs around €10. From Central Station ("Centrale F.S.") you can take the metro (yellow line –direction "San Donato") and get off at "Duomo" (4 stops). From there you can go to the stop "Duomo" of tram n.15. Take tram n.15 in the "Rozzano" direction and get off at the stop "Porta Ludovica" (4 stops).

_**From Linate Airport**_

Airport Code: LIN

Distance from city center: 7Km

**Taxi:** it takes about 20 minutes and costs approximately € 15. Please keep in mind that the cost can vary depending on traffic conditions.

**Public transport:** it takes about 30 minutes. Exit the airport following the directions for the metro station. Take the metro (blue line - direction "San Cristoforo") and get off at "Santa Sofia". From there, you can either walk or take the tram 15 (direction "Rozzano") and get off at stop "Porta Lodovica" (3 stops).

_**From Orio al Serio International Airport**_

Airport Code: BGY

Distance from city center: 50Km

**Taxi:** it takes about 60 minutes. It cost around €120. Please keep in mind that the cost can vary depending on traffic conditions. For all further information on taxi please see the section below.

**Orio Shuttle (bus) + public transport:** by bus it normally takes one hour to reach the Central Station ("Stazione Centrale"). At [this link](https://www.milanbergamoairport.it/en/bus/) you can find the list of all different bus providers that connect Orio Al Serio Airport with the Central Station. The price of the ticket is around € 12. From the Central Station ("Centrale F.S."), take the metro (yellow line – direction "San Donato") and get off at the stop "Duomo" (4 stops). Go to the stop "Duomo" of tram n.15. Take tram n.15 in the "Rozzano" direction and get off at the stop "Porta Ludovica" (4 stops). For all further information on public transport see the section below.

From a train station

[Trenitalia-Ferrovie dello Stato](https://www.trenitalia.com/en.html) is the national railway transport service and has several stations in Milan.

Besides Trenitalia, [Trenord](https://www.trenord.it/en/) is mostly a commuter rail and serves some of Milan’s neighboring cities such as Como and Varese as well as most of the northern Lombardy region.

_**From Stazione Centrale FS (Central Railway Station)**_

**Taxi:** it takes about 20 minutes and it costs approximately € 20. Please keep in mind that the cost can vary depending on traffic conditions. For all further information on taxi please see the section below.

**Public transport:** take subway (yellow line - direction "San Donato"), and get off at the stop "Duomo" (4 stops). Go to the stop "Duomo" of tram n.15. Take tram n.15 in the "Rozzano" direction and get off at the stop "Porta Ludovica" (4 stops). For all further information on public transport see the section below.

text:
_**From Garibaldi Railway Stations**_

**Taxi:** it takes about 25 minutes and costs approximately € 20. Please keep in mind that the cost can vary depending on traffic conditions. For all further information on taxi please see the section below.

**Public transport:** take the metro (green line - direction "Abbiategrasso"), get off at stop "Porta Genova" (6 stops). From there take tram n.9 direction “Stazione Central FS” and get off at stop “Via Bocconi” (6 stops). As an alternative, take the metro (green line - direction "Abbiategrasso"), get off at stop "Sant'Ambrogio" (4 stops) and change with the blu line of the metro. Take the blu line (direction "Linate airport") and get off at "Santa Sofia" station (3 stops). From there, you can either walk or take the tram 15 (direction "Rozzano") and get off at "Porta Lodovica" station (2 stops).

For all further information on public transport see the section below.

#### How to get around Milan

Public Transport

Urban tickets allow you to use the entire urban ATM network, which includes busses, trams, the underground and rail network. **A ticket costs €2.20 and is valid for a duration of 90 minutes on the whole Milan urban area**.

You can **pay directly with your credit card at any metro station or on the bus/tram**. As an alternative, you can be buy a rechargeable card at any tobacconist or newsstand shop, or at the vending machines at every underground station. The ticket must be stamped at the start

of every journey and is valid for 90 minutes after stamping.

Tickets are available also online through the ATM app, or by sending an SMS with the message "ATM" to

the number 48444.

Please refer to [www.atm.it/en](https://www.atm.it/en) for all further information as well as for any possible updates on metro and bus lines.

Taxi

Outside every airport or station there is an area reserved for taxis. Taxis in Italy are white cars with a sign saying "TAXI" on top. If you decide to take a taxi, make sure to take an official one. Do not agree to a ride with any other driver who may approach you.

It is also possible to book official Milan's Taxi from these Apps: appTaxi; free now; itTaxi; WeTaxi; TaxiMilano; inTaxi.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/summer-school/how-get-bocconi-and-beyond#addtoany "Show all")

text:
### Make Your Summer Matter

Watch video

#### On campus courses

Digital Marketing

**Prof. Cristian Chizzoli, Armando Cirrincione**

This course describes the growing relevance of digital marketing in the competitive arena, both for customers and firms. The contents aim at educating participants on how innovative digital tools (Internet, smartphones, etc.) and methods (such as SEO, SEM, content marketing, social media marketing, emailing, marketing automation, etc.) are revolutionizing the world of marketing, often determining the strategies implemented by global players to develop products, to persuade customers, to distribute items effectively and to price them correctly. The course will focus on the potential that all electronic media have and the most important keys to success as well as on the challenges faced.

Entrepreneurship and Innovation Management

**Prof. Carlo Salvato**

This course provides real world, experience-based learning on what it’s like to actually start and develop a new venture, either within an existing company, or as a new startup company. The main objective is to allow you to directly experience the earliest phases of an entrepreneurial startup process. Focus will thus be on building and testing a novel business idea. In this course you will be asked to actively engage in developing the initial business idea, but also in talking to potential customers, suppliers, partners, and competitors, as you confront the chaos and uncertainty of how a real new venture actually emerges from the entrepreneurs’ efforts.

Equity and Venture Capital Financing

**Prof. Gimede Gigante, Matteo Arpe**

Discover all the key factors in value creation by private equity and venture capital firms, taking a close look at management issues, business models and investment opportunities. You will learn to act from the perspective of financial intermediaries specialized in scouting, consulting and financing successful entrepreneurial activities and startups, right in the center of Milan, one of the most important business centers and stock markets in Europe.

Global Law for Companies, Institutions and Citizens

**Prof. Luigi Buonanno**

This course explores how Global Law frames Companies, Institutions and Citizens' life in an interconnected world. In an era marked by major international tensions increased importance of digital technology, and daily interference of technology with Law, our main objective is to allow you to directly understand opportunities and challenges offered by Global Law for any business, public and social activity, Focus will thus be on building, growing and testing your abilities to develop your own skills in areas such as Democracy and the Rule of Law in Europe and in the World, Internationalization of Law, Artificial Intelligence Law, Business and citizens, Corporations and their compliance, new forms of Justice, etc. In this course you will be asked to directly and actively engage in developing your legal reasoning but also in exercising in writing and talking, in order to apply knowledge acquired.

International Business Strategy

**Prof. Cinzia Parolini, Yangyang Cheng**

Is the world getting increasingly global or is globalization threatened by rising protectionism? Are we on the verge of a new economic order? Which strategies could companies adopt to thrive in this fast-changing environment? How do we analyze the characteristics and potential of international markets? This program addresses these questions with a very practical and hands-on approach, alternating lectures with case discussions and in class exercises. The course has been designed for motivated business and non-business major students, interested in understanding how international developments can impact their life and their future careers.

Luxury Management

**Prof. Gabriella Lojacono, Laura Ru Yun Pan**

Through this course you will explore the fundamental strategies and business models of different companies with a luxury or premium positioning. You’ll be actively involved, analyzing global competitive trends and sharing best practices in a broad range of luxury brands and industries, such as fashion and leather accessories, food and beverage, jewelry, automotive, and hospitality. Learn to use the alchemy of blending creativity, product development, manufacturing and go-to-market strategies that leads to success.

Sustainability Transformation, ESG Finance and Energy Management

metadata.title: Summer School for Bachelor Students | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Summer School for Bachelor Students | Bocconi University > Make Your Summer Matter

text:
**Prof. Francesco Perrini, Andrea Biancardi**

The course is intended to familiarize students with the challenges of sustainable development, climate change, energy transition, corporate sustainability, ESG and sustainable finance. Specific emphasis will be oriented to firm innovation processes, new business models and the development of sustainable value. The course will equip students with skills and competencies to address problems related with these new challenges. It will include the exam of best practices and various case studies showing how firms can meet sustainable goals and market/financial goals.

Recent Trends in Artificial Intelligence

**Prof. Laura Sanità, Luca Biggio, Andrea Tangherloni**

This course provides a comprehensive, experience-based introduction to Artificial Intelligence, from its historical foundations to its modern advancements. You will explore the key differences between traditional and modern AI, the driving forces behind the AI revolution, and the current landscape of AI tools.

The course takes a deep dive into fundamental concepts such as Neural Networks, Deep Learning, and Natural Language Processing.  You will engage hands-on with the mathematics behind neural networks and train your own AI models, gaining practical skills to navigate the rapidly evolving AI field.

text:
## Schedule & Activities

Here you can find all the details you need to know about our exciting **curricular** and **extracurricular activities**.

Explore our comprehensive **schedule**, stay updated with important updates, and access a variety of links to enhance your summer learning experience.

Make the most out of your summer with us!

**Welcome and opening ceremony**

**Monday 29 June, morning (via Roentgen 1, Aula Magna)**

Welcome to Bocconi Summer School!

After receiving your welcome kit at the reception desk, you will attend the opening ceremony and then join a campus tour.

You will receive an e-mail with the meeting time.

**Lectures and group work**

**Monday 29 June, afternoon**

The first lecture will take place on the first day, after lunch. All the details will be shared in the morning during the opening ceremony.

For all the following lectures and group work sessions you have to refer to your course syllabus, on [Blackboard](https://blackboard.unibocconi.it/) (Bocconi e-learning platform).

_Please note that Blackboard will be available starting from one week before the beginning of the program._

**Visits**

**During the program**

Besides the lectures and the group work, you will be involved in one or two visits.

Please refer to your course syllabus, on [Blackboard](https://blackboard.unibocconi.it/) (Bocconi e-learning platform) and follow the instructions given in class by your Professor.

_Please note that Blackboard will be available starting from one week before the beginning of the program._

**MSc Programs Presentation**

**Tuesday 14 July, afternoon (via Roentgen 1, Aula Magna)**

The Summer School can be a way to experience Bocconi before considering it for your Master. Find out about our MSc programs: offer, admissions procedures, financial aid, student and campus life. This is the perfect moment to ask all your questions!

**Exam**

**Friday 17 July, morning**

The final exam will take place on the last day of the program.

Only students who attend at least 80% of the academic activities and pass the exam, will receive the final official certificate and 6 credits.

**Cooking Class**

**First week of the program, after 5pm (FoodGenius Academy - Viale Col di Lana, 8)**

Make new friends while cooking with professional chefs... and don’t forget to come hungry!

Attention: to attend this activity it is mandatory to register.

**Bocconi Sport**

**During the program**

Enjoy our Sport Center! Summer School students have the possibility to access Bocconi sport facilities to make the most of their experience.

**Farewell Party**

**Wednesday 15 July, evening**

Celebrate your Summer School experience with your classmates during a farewell "aperitivo" - location and timing will be sent via e-mail.


Blackboard

Available from June 23

[Log in](https://blackboard.unibocconi.it/ultra/institution-page)


Discover Milan

[Discover more](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/summer-milan)


Share your experience with the world!

[Follow us](https://www.instagram.com/meet_bocconi/)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/summer-school/summer-school-bocconi-what-awaits-you#addtoany "Show all")

text:
### Discover all our labs!

Communication and Advertising - sold out

**Prof. Arianna Brioschi, Prof. Stefano Prestini, Prof. Elena Bellio**

Just how familiar are you with an advertising campaign? A business may live or die based on its ability to communicate successfully. Through this lab you will understand the most important issues when planning and evaluating marketing communications and executions. You will have the chance to meet with some of the best in class in the advertising industry and take a peek at what happens behind the scenes of a communication campaign.

Computer Coding - sold out

**Prof. Massimo Ballerini**

In recent times, coding has emerged as a primary skill not only for IT specialists, but also for managers, professionals and researchers in a wide spectrum of fields. Learning the basics of programming is therefore a valuable chance to develop a new approach to work and technologies. This lab explores the basics and the logic of programming using Python, one of the most widely used programming languages.

Data Science - sold out

**Prof. Omiros Papaspiliopoulos, Prof. Pieralberto Guarniero**

The laboratory will introduce participants to some fundamental ideas in Data Science with the primary goal to develop and understand sufficiently well some core methodologies in order to apply them to some really cool current applications, ranging from visualizing complex data to understanding criminal networks. The laboratory will start with a lecture dealing on the fundamentals of Data Science and a discussion of the relation between predictive modelling and causal inference.

Digital Marketing - sold out

**Prof. Deborah Raccagni, Prof. Laura Colm**

The lab focuses on a theoretical rather than a technical understanding of e-marketing and the digital environment, adopting a customer journey approach to explore the main practices and exploit tools and techniques that can be used to this purpose.

Diritto dell’economia e delle nuove tecnologie

**Prof. Stefania Boffano**

Le tecnologie emergenti possono portare a scoperte fondamentali per il mondo degli affari e non solo, presentando sfide sia per i regolatori che per i politici. Il laboratorio fornirà un'ampia panoramica delle implicazioni legali delle nuove tecnologie e di come l'attuale quadro giuridico stia affrontando le sfide che esse presentano.

Scopri di più sulla Bocconi Summer School in Law [cliccando qui](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/corsi-di-studio/giurisprudenza/summer-school-law "Summer School in Law")!

Entrepreneurship - sold out

**Prof. Irene Dagnino**

How can a business idea be generated and evaluated? How can one go about testing the validity of the business by creating a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) and how can the arrangements be made for starting up the business? Students will be introduced to tools and methods like Lean Startup, the Business Model Canvas and the Elevator Pitch. They will be asked to generate and present a new business idea to a potential investor or partner (a representative of the business community or an entrepreneur).

The European Union and its economic policies in the post-globalization - sold out

**Prof. Stefano Riela, Prof. Paolo Palamiti**

Globalization, offshoring and multilateralism used to be the drivers that shaped the world order in the last decades. Recent global crises - such as the pandemic and the war in Ukraine - revealed the critical vulnerabilities of a highly open world economy and of global supply chains and worsen international relations. In this context, the European Union is asked to make the most of its policies such as the European single market, competition policy, trade policy and a modernized industrial policy.

metadata.title: Summer School for High School Students | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Summer School for High School Students | Bocconi University > Discover all our labs!

text:
This is an introductory course on EU institutions, decision making and its main economic policies in the framework of the international economy.

Finance - sold out

**Prof. Andrea Cerri, Prof. Marta Zava, Prof. Anastasia Giakoumelou**

How does the modern financial system work? How do new peer-to-peer digital business models fit into it? At the end of the lab, students will be asked to evaluate the sustainability of one of the existing peer-to-peer digital platforms in financial services and present their findings to a representative of the financial community.

Game Theory - sold out

**Prof. Laura Bonacorsi, Prof. Monica Bonacina**

Doing business is like playing chess. In order to beat the competition in your industry you have to foresee what other companies will do and make your move accordingly. In this lab students will learn how an optimal choice can be made, in particular for a business organization, while taking into consideration a variety of possible decisions by partners and competitors. At the end on the week students will be asked to put themselves in the shoes of a CEO facing an important dilemma and present their conclusions to a senior representative of the Italian business community.

Global Economic Scenarios - sold out

**Prof. Gabriele Deana, Prof. Elisa Borghi**

Designed for students with a foundational understanding of economics, this course analyzes the main forces driving economic growth and shaping global economies. Key topics include the impact of globalization, technological innovation, and international trade on economic development. Participants will engage in discussions on inequality, poverty, and sustainability, analyzing how these factors influence growth patterns in both developed and emerging markets.

Global Law of Business and New Technologies - sold out

**Prof. Viktoriia Lapa**

Emerging technologies can lead to fundamental discoveries for the business world and beyond, presenting challenges for both regulators and policymakers. The lab will provide a broad overview of the legal implications of new technologies and how the current legal framework is facing the challenges they present.

International Politics - sold out

In this lab we examine the historical origins, role and functions of international organizations and discuss how international organizations make and enforce decisions, then we apply this knowledge to international trade. We will simulate a negotiation within an international organization: students will be divided in groups and each group will form the diplomatic delegation of a country.

Introduction to Economics - sold out

**Prof. Michela Braga, Prof. Sara Negrelli**

The quantitative analysis of causal relationships allows sound personal and institutional decisions to be made: What are the economic returns of attending university? Do better-paid public servants perform better? Students will be asked to perform empirical research on a social or economic issue of their interest and present their findings to a senior research analyst.

Luxury and Fashion - sold out

**Prof. Erica Corbellini, Prof. Stefania Saviolo, Prof. Lucia Paladino**

Fashion is inspiration, creativity and intuition. But it is also organization, strategy and management. The lab provides the tools for understanding the fashion and luxury industries, which are the key players, success factors and trends in the “new normal.” Special emphasis will be placed on Generation Z and Millennials and new trends such as digitalization, sustainability and inclusivity.

Students will be asked to think about the product of the future for a prestigious fashion brand and design the best strategy to communicate it. A manager from the company will evaluate their output.

Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence - sold out

**Prof. Riccardo Zecchina**

Artificial Intelligence is a multidisciplinary science that gives us a greater understanding of the world. It is speeding up new discoveries across all sciences, from helping mitigate the effects of climate change to improving diagnostic capabilities in healthcare. It makes our lives more efficient everyday and it will push humanity further than we ever dreamed of. But you need to master the right tools if you want to control where it leads us and generate true innovation.

metadata.title: Summer School for High School Students | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Summer School for High School Students | Bocconi University > Discover all our labs!

text:
This lab will provide you with the skills needed to understand the machine learning tools revolutionizing the social and natural sciences.

Management of Entertainment (NEW) - sold out

**Prof. Simone Autera**

Movies, TV series, games, live shows, songs, reels, memes — entertainment content travels fast, shapes identities, and fuels global culture. Across the entertainment industries, content is more than “fun”:

it is an economic asset that behaves differently from traditional goods. Why do some releases spark global conversations while others disappear into the endless scroll? What happens behind the scenes to turn creative content into something that captures attention instead of getting lost in the dark?

To explore these broader entertainment dynamics in a concrete and hands-on way, the course will focus on the music industry as its primary laboratory. In a world of digital convergence, music no longer lives in one format or one channel. Artists move between TikTok, Spotify, live shows, gaming spaces, and social media ecosystems, competing in an intense attention economy where visibility is scarce and algorithms shape discovery.

Students will analyse how strategy adapts to new consumption patterns, platform logics, and the growing power of fandoms.

The lab includes a group project where participants become label managers responsible for launching a new single by their favourite artist.

Curiosity about entertainment and global culture is essential. This course invites students to look beyond the ‘playlist’ and understand the strategic thinking that makes content a ‘big hit’.

Strategy - sold out

**Prof. Charles Williams, Silvia Zamboni**

Why do some companies succeed while others fail? How does strategy contribute to success? What is strategy? How do we identify and describe a firm’s strategy? This course will equip you with the concepts, tools, and skills required to formulate and implement strategy across a range of business contexts — from a single-business firm to a multiproduct, multinational corporation. Our emphasis will be on applying the tools of competitive strategy analysis to real business situations through case studies, examples, and our own experiences.

#### Are you an Italian student with a passion for law?

Don’t miss the opportunity to join the Bocconi Summer School in Law! Choose "Diritto dell’economia e delle nuove tecnologie", **a program held in Italian** from **July 6** to **July 10**, **2026**, and explore the intersection of law, economics, and emerging technologies.

Discover more about Bocconi Summer School in Law [here](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/corsi-di-studio/giurisprudenza/summer-school-law "Summer School in Law")!
